Great find. Highly recommend These jeans feel wonderful and fit exactly as I had hoped. I debated on what size to buy and finally decided on 28S. I am 5'4" and a fit 155 lbs and they fit tight enough but not too tight. The length is just right with about a 2" heel. I typically wear a size 8 in pants with a 30" inseam. Some reviews suggest that the jeans are heavy weight, but I find them to be just right. Not too heavy and not too light. Highly recommend purchasing.

Great pants for the price! These are great! I'm a college student with a 40-hour/week summer internship where I can't wear jeans, and I don't own a ton of business casual-type clothing. I only had two pairs of dress pants, so I wanted another pair to break up the outfit rotation (and to make laundry easier) but didn't want to break the bank on something I probably will only wear once during the rest of the year. These fit the bill! I am 5'7" and 140 pounds with a 28 inch waist and a 39 inch hip at the fullest point (aka I have a huge butt). I ordered the medium, and apart from being just slightly too long (by maybe half an inch, but someone with a smaller butt would probably need a hemming job), they fit really well! I like that they're a higher rise in the front (so my tummy is contained) as I just find that really comfortable. They're VERY comfy, flexible, and stretchy, so I could see someone who wears an 8 or maybe even a 10 at my height fitting in these well (I usually wear a 4 or 6 in pants depending on brand). They don't have pockets, so they're pretty much leggings made out of nicer material, but they do have inside and outside seams so they look like any other skinny fit dress pant. I ordered navy and it's very true to color. Thanks, Amazon!

Great fit and SO comfy!! However... had to size down. I’ve always heard great things about Grey’s scrubs, so I figured I’d give them a shot. With that being said, I am beyond glad I did! These scrubs are SO SOFT. I love the side pocket with the zipper and the slits at the bottom of the pants. When I ordered these the first time, I ordered a Small Tall. The length fit perfect but they were baggier than my liking, so I did have to send them back for an XS Tall. For reference, I’m 5’6 and around 135 lbs. They fit my waist and legs great. The length could be just slightly longer for my liking (I have long legs and a short torso, so my pants sit up higher on my waist— and I also have slim legs so finding pants that fit my body type can be hard) but overall I’m very happy with my purchase and would recommend these pants! I’ll probably end up buying another pair.

Bad fit for my body type. Good quality though It can be hard to find pants for myself, especially skinny jeans, because I have large hips but skinny calves and ankles. My height is 5’ 4” and weight is 175, I have a pear shaped body and usually a size 14 in jeans so I ordered the 14. ▪️These leather pants on me started bunching up halfway down the thigh and got worse lower down my leg. Snug fit in the upper thighs without being tight though. ▪️They have a pretty wide leg opening and I like my skinny pants to be very snug at the ankle. ▪️ The rise of the pants come at least 2 inches below my belly button and I prefer pants to hit at my belly button. I also tend to carry my weight in my midsection so the pants are a little bit snug at the waist band. ▪️ I will be returning these pants, but again my body is very hard to fit so if you tend to be less curvy and are more thin than I am these pants will probably still look good on you. They are great quality though.

Runs way too large and no communication from company As embarrassing the photos are of these scrubs on me I am posting to show how large they run as I wish someone had shown or told me :) I made an order of several pair of scrub bottoms (including these red ones) in different colours and styles trying to find the right fit. The colour is a vibrant red and after washing in hot water and drying the colour remains but the size did not change. Perfect fit! These fit perfect as I am just under 5 ft and 120. I order a size 1. Normally I am a size 3 so do not be afraid to size down. Just what I was looking for my fall wardrobe as I wanted to add a pair of corduroy. Will be buying more colors!!

Perfect!! Will buy more! These are amazing!!!! I will probably buy more colors!! I’m 5’7, 126-130lb normally and now 14 weeks along and I bought XS. I Just hit the stage where all my jeans are uncomfortable to wear around the waist, especially in the evening. I honestly didn’t have high expectations for these but they’re almost perfect. They fit snug around my legs but the material is stretchy and lightweight so comfortable. I’m skinny with thin legs so I don’t think I’ll gain a lot of weight in my legs. The waist band does start very low in the front so I will definitely have to wear my longer shirts with it but since the color of jean is dark I won’t really worry about the band showing. The rise is higher in the back. Love that it has real pockets in the back. They look like jeans I already own and love, I don’t think anyone will notice they’re maternity jeans.

Long, long, long,but ok I'm 5'5", 140ish lbs, and the small petite fit as expected: fitted, but not skintight... but they were SOOOOO LONG. They still are just barely off the ground with a petite. The material on these is a thick-threaded, stiff fabric, it almost reminds me of the fabric tents are made of, or work coveralls (but thinner), or low thread count sheets... For the fabric being so stiff, i was super surprised with how comfortable these scrubs actually were. Not like your fave pair of pjs or anything, but not bad at all. I ordered both the pants and top on Amazon, and they match perfectly in style, material, and color. (I've included both tags in picture so you can compare item #s). For a $30 set of scrubs, these aren't bad... but they're not as amazing as some of the reviews state. I guess i was expecting more.

great flattering scrub pants I am obsessed with these scrubs. I am 5'4" with a 30" waist and 39" hips. I am usually a medium in pants and generally prefer a looser fit, but with the waist not being adjustible I decided to order both a Small regular and Medium petite. The mediums were just too loose in the waist and the moment I bent down the waistline fell with it. The length on the petites look better to me with my height, but I have a very athletic build and the cuff was too tight over my muscular calves. On the other hand, the size smalls fit PERFECTLY in the waist. I was worried they'd be too tight in the hips/rear but the fabric has a surprising amount of stretch and I've been very comfortable with them at work (I work in a veterinary clinic so I spend a lot of time squatting and wrangling animals). They did shrink ever so slightly with my covid-compliant hot wash/hot dry cycles (the photos of the small size pants were taken post-wash) but again, still an amazing fit, highly recommend

Advice for Ladies that are ~5’4” and weigh around 65 kg The product is exactly what you expect. It’s slightly tighter around the waist, it goes above the navel, slightly long and baggy at the bottom. I am 5’4” and weigh 65kg. If you have a small butt then your butt will look pretty flat, it’s not ugly flat tho. If you are a female around my height I would recommend a small short. A medium for a hip size above 35inches would be too large. As for material, the best I can compare it to would be a shower curtain. It’s a pretty thin material.So if you wear a women’s size 7 or 8 in regular jeans I highly recommend these because they are pretty cheap compared to the others.

Falls down in back Lined pants so they don’t stick too badly. I’ve ordered and tried on all three inseams at size 14. I’m regularly a 16, 5’7”. The short pant was about upper ankle height. Long was past my toes and the middle size, 29” inseam, was still a little long...almost to the floor. Problems come at the top. Waaaay high waist (above belly button) in front (cool triple buttons get covered up unless you wear a half shirt) and dips down in back. Enough to have me needing to hike them up relentlessly and that is just intolerable for me. Bunches up all along legs too so it doesn’t end up looking as smooth as it does in the pictures. I badly want to keep them because they look bad ass but they’ll be going back.

Could be worse, but could be A LOT better! Just starting nursing I'm finding it so hard to find nice but affordable cute scrubs. Not only that but looking for ones that fit me right is even more of a struggle. These pants however are not the greatest I do like the fit on me though. The fabric is not stretchy it's stiff and not soft and the elastic waist comes up past my belly button. There is also a lot of extra fabric in the hip area that's not needed for me. With that said a small fits me relatively nicely I like my scrubs to fit me more on the snugger side with a little bit of looseness but definitely not baggy everywhere. I don't have to hem the bottom the length hits a little below my ankle hovering above the ground. My measurements are hip: 40, waist: 30, wt: 152 ht: 5'4. I hope this helps some else see if these work for them. UPDATE: I wore them to work once and they were the most uncomfortable scrubs pants I own. I tried to make it work but they were not staying up, kept falling down at the waist. They were super tight in my thighs it felt like it was almost gonna rip when I sat down. I ended up returning them 😑 disappointed....

Good Leggings - Will buy again I'm sick of those soft or so called velvety stretchy spandex nylon-like leggings! I love the cotton ones. I feared buying from online. You got all these size differences with this one being a size down (or 2 or 3 or 4) and that one being 1 to 2 to 4 sizes up. I ordered ONE pair as experiment. Perfect. I compared them to my No Boundaries cotton leggings and there was barely a difference. I buy the 15-16 large in theirs and bought extra large in these. I normally wear size 9 or 10 jeans. My size leggings in No Boundaries brand should be 11-13. But I like mine loose - less clingy.l I'm 5 ft 4 and 133 pounds if that helps. Don't hesitate. They are good leggings.

Small reg vs small petite (both fit great) Very good product. I like the material and the waist band elastic is comfortable, and doesn't extend fully in the front so it doesn't get bunched up when pulling the ties. Based on reviews, I ended up getting the small and small petite to try. I am 5'3.75" and 130lbs, usually wear a size 5 juniors or 4/6 depending on the pants as I have a booty. I was concerned as based on the measurements I didn't think my booty would fit in the small (39" hips) but they did, easily! Plenty of room in both sizes. I had room behind the thighs as well, and only had to pull the drawstrings slightly, which is surprising for me with pants in general. I laid out the small reg and small petite and tried on both. The only discernable difference I found is in the length of the pant leg. Small reg measured at 31" inseam, and it was 2" longer than the petite. For me both fit well, and just depended on what "look" I would want- fully over the top of the tennis shoe with wrinkles (reg) or just laying on top (petite). For reference the one pictured is the petite and the shoes are standard asics sneakers (about 1" heel).

High Quality, ALL USE Pants This yoga/scrub pants by KECOLS are high quality, fit very well and are true to size. The fabric is soft to the touch, and thicker than normal yoga pants and is a 4-way-stretch which gives a lot of freedom of movements. They have plenty of pockets and even with a dedicated phone pocket there is no pulling down typical with thinner fabrics in cargo pants, where with the weight of the pocket is very uncomfortable to walk because of bouncing and weight. Not in this case. However for me the bad part was that the pants are 1 inch too long in the inseam as seen in the photos. I am 5'3" and 130 lb for reference.I eventually had to alter the leg by eliminating the slit on the sides which I really liked, hem and run an elastic in the ankles as seen in photo. The result was a customized look which I love anyway but definitely changed the style of the pants. Washability of this garment was easy and practical . I washed them with same color in hot water and dry them at high heat. No damage, no wrinkles, no color fading all around a very well made pant that I hope will last me for a while.

oversized and soft This review is for the GRAPENT Wide leg palazzo pants, xx-large, dark blue. I usually wear an XXL in pull on pants, 17/18 in jeans, and XL (XXL is usually a little too big) in leggings. Approximate measurements are 41” waist and 51” hips, 5’6” and a 30 inch inseam reaches just to the floor on me. These pants are a delight to wear. They are unbelievably soft, thin, stretchy, and flowy. The style is meant to be oversized so definitely don’t size up even if you want a loose fit. The size chart was based on body measurement and not the dimensions of the pants. It says that XX-Large, US size 20 - US 22, waist 36.5 - 38”, hips 47 - 48.5”. My waist and hips are bigger than that and the pants still are a flowy fit on me. The waist has a few inches of smocking and is very stretchy. The fabric has a good horizontal stretch to it. These pants drag on the ground for me. I wouldn’t expect this with a 29” inseam but I measured and they are indeed 29 (maybe 29.25) inches. However even with the waist an inch or two above my belly button (which is perfect for me) the crotch sits a little low. It looks and feels good but that’s why the leg length is too long for me. They look like the could be a straight wide leg but are actually about 16” at the thigh and 20” at the hem. The pockets are huge and stretchy and don’t lay completely flat across the front but rather droop out a little at the top. As I mentioned, these pants are delightfully comfortable. They are a little too oversized and long for me to want to wear as everyday “real” pants. Instead I’ll wear them primarily as loungewear that I’m OK with wearing out of the house for something like running an errand.

Love luv love these! True to size. These boots are great! I am a true 7.5 but got an 8. The 7.5 probably would’ve fit perfectly. I wore regular socks and wore them for 4 hours and danced on concrete for 2.5 hours. When I wear them again I will get an insert with an arch. That’s the only part of my foot that was strained. They have a zipper which was really nice and easy to get my foot in. I’m 5’3” 117lbs. My calves are average for my size. The tops were a little loose but still looked great. No slouching. Nice chunky heel with good height that did not hurt my feet while dancing. I walked on tile, concrete, rocks, grass and felt they were sturdy for all of them. I did not notice an overly strong smell but I really would not have cared. Shiny white and the scuffs I got from the black rubber bottoms came off fairly easily with a Mr. Eraser sponge. I highly recommend these. I will definitely wear these for more than just costumes. I took off 1 star for the lack of any instep and the material around the heel might come off at some point.

Love the length the stretch the flare!!! I’m about 5’5-5’4 and weigh about 186 and I got a size XL. I’m on a weight loss journey, but I think these will be okay for awhile, as even though they’re stretchy, they are currently a tad tight in the waist. But I carry a lot of weight there unfortunately. I love the flare, the length and the stretch! I found two safety pins in the left pant cuff, which tells me somebody else had worn these pants, which to me is a dick move by the company. But I’m keeping them! Just washing well before wearing again! Love the pants and I will buy more from this company.

Very good product, good quality, elegant, and greate fit. I love them! (I would love for the leggings to have a little zipper in the waist so they could be a little more tight in the abdomen, but they are wonderful nonetheless.) The fabric of these leggings is thick and helps contour the figure. They are not sports leggings, they look a lot more elegant, more like pants, so they are perfect to wear in the office. They are made in the USA! with good finishes, and the high waist is perfect to wear with blouses (which you can put inside them) But ( there is always a but) the red color at least has to be used with light-colored underwear, You can see the dark underwear a little bit through them (as you can see in the pictures). To take care of the leggings I recommend washing them in a Delicates Laundry Bags , with COLD water and avoiding sitting on surfaces such as gravel, this way they will last longer. In the long term ( 1 year if you use them ones a week) and since they wear out on the butt with rubbing (and white elastics appear). I had some that were a very small size for me and for obvious reasons the butt was worn out before. I'm 5' ,121 lb and the small size fits me.

Smooth, Sculpting & Very Flattering (Charcoal Gray, Small – 28”) I ordered the Sunzel Sunzfly Invisible Butt Scrunch leggings in Charcoal Gray, size Small, 28” inseam, and they’re incredibly flattering. The fabric (80% polyamide, 20% elastane) feels ultra-soft and stretchy with light-to-medium compression. It smooths everything out without feeling stiff or restrictive. The material isn’t thin, and I feel confident wearing them for workouts or running errands. The high-waisted waistband provides solid tummy control and stays in place — no rolling down. The no front seam makes a big difference in comfort and gives a clean, smooth look in the front. The hidden scrunch detail in the back enhances shape subtly. It lifts without looking exaggerated. Very sculpting but still tasteful. The 28” inseam hits right at my ankle for a full-length look without bunching. The Charcoal Gray color is a true neutral — easy to pair with black, white, or muted tones. Overall, they feel supportive, smoothing, and flattering from every angle. Great quality for the price. I would love to get different colors.

I am so happy that I am on the verge of crying!!! In my teens to early 30s, I wore nothing but denim. I wore them hard too, b/c of my figure (way smaller than now), I always had issues fitting my hips and waist. I had to give them up after giving birth to my son due to medical issues. That was 16 years ago. I have been watching tons of reels of ladies working out and their new wardrobes, and it inspired me to try a pair of jeans again. I am shocked I found the perfect pair on my first purchase! Holy Cow! I am in love. Of course, they aren't as comfortable as leggings, however, this pair has stretch in all the right places. I am still working on losing weight and went with flare b/c my thighs are WAY bigger than my calves. I am just stunned that I have no back gap. I always had an issue there b/c I am swayed back. Seriously, check out this brand on Amazon. Read the reviews and measurements. FYI, Elastic, high-waisted pants are best for those with a sway & not a gap. I hate the idea of high-waisted pants, but these are so comfy. I wear large in leggings, which is what was suggested for this purchase. My waist is larger than the sizing chart, 40, and my hips are 43. I fit perfectly in the large for 30.5 - 32 waist and 40.5 - 42 hips. Keep this in mind. Color is on point. Value for money is great considering I have issues fitting in most years. The Fabric is of great quality and itchy. The seam isn't tight in the crotch/butt region.

Bootyliciously awesome!! Must have! Love love love these pants!!! I bought 2 pair, blue and red. I’m 5’3”, 154lbs and Large fit very comfortably! I was drenched in sweat from dancing all night but the pants didn’t make me overly hot at all. I was worried about under buttcheek sweat leaving a line lol but it was all good. Beautiful bright blue and didn’t fade or bleed at all. I haven’t worn the red ones yet so I’m going to be returning them to exchange them for a 30” inseam pair. I think they’ll be the perfect length. These are an absolute MUST HAVE. I’ve sent the link to all my friends. They are reasonably priced and I personally think they’ll are worth it!

Perfect Y2K Style Almost Goth Boots : ) These are exactly what I wanted them to be! The boots have a thick bottom, side zipper, and are tall for a combat style boot. They are very true to size - I usually wear an 8.5 but I get a 9 when I want to wear 2 layers of socks underneath.They fit really well and are very comfortable. For this particular style, they are pretty lightweight as well. The boots look very Y2K but are simple enough that they could be worn a number of ways. The half-side zipper is convenient for getting the boots on quickly. However, the ankles and calves almost fit me and I typically wear child-size clothing. That tells me the calf part of the boots may run small and if one has a bigger frame - these boots may not fit. They're not ultra-skinny but they're quite skinny. I'm 5'7" and 80-85lbs for a size comparison.Overall, I think the boots are made well, look great, fit well, and are comfortable. I have no criticisms personally and would definitely recommend these boots to others. : )

Perfect true trouser pants for my body You know, ordering pants is always just a bit hit or miss. But I was pleasantly surprised with the fit and quality of these jeans. There's definitely some thickness to them. Not a low quality material that is just going to stretch out and pucker. They sit nicely at my natural waist and are very loose/flowy through the thighs and legs. This is what I was looking for. I've got some things on me and "trouser" pants never fit me properly because of that. These were just right. I got these on sale for $29.97, so I'm pleased with that. They don't have any sort of "tummy control" which makes this postpartum mama a little insecure, but I still love them. Absolutely perfect length 5'3 145lbs size 8 short

*NOT* for the fabric sensitive 5’10” 173lbs (size 8 US) So I was looking for a tall girl friendly, Alo sweat pant dupe (65% cotton and polyester). The description says that this is what it is fabric wise. WELL good news? It’s almost tall girl friendly- I can wear flat shoes and be okay- if you’re over 5’10” I’d look elsewhere. I’d also recommend sizing up. I’m usually a medium, I sized up for a baggier look, and well it fits like a traditional medium on me. Where my issue really is? I can’t tell where the 65% cotton is. I’m super sensitive to fabrics and don’t love polyester but appreciate the flexibility it provides. The lining of these pants is 95% polyester and 5% spandex. It’s giving sateen sheets for pants.

Loose Fit HUGE compared with Original Fit The product description for Carhartt Women's Rugged Flex Loose Fit Canvas Work Pant says, "Loose Fit, previously known as Original Fit, is a label update in name only. Loose and Original size and fit remains the same." Not so! I love my size 6 Original fit (tan) so I bought a size 6 Loose (black; I also measured myself first). See the tan photo: fitted but not tight or loose. Compare the black photo: yes, that is my entire arm stuffed down the leg hole (along with my leg) up to my elbow. :/ Love Carhartt quality, so I ordered the size 4 (I am 5'7", fluctuate 130-135#, usually wear an 8 in pants). Edit: Just got the size 4 (4! I usually wear an 8), and it fits me! Upgraded my rating. So, despite the manufacturer claim that the new "Loose Fit" fit "remains the same" as the "Original Fit", know that (for me), a size 4 "Loose" fits like the size 6 (Fall, prior to COVID) "Original."

Flattering, Comfy, Cute These jeans are perfect! I am 5'5 ish and 175lbs ish and got a size 8. As someone with wider hips and thicker thighs, it's very difficult to find a good pair of jeans so I was extremely surprised that a pair of jeans from amazon could fit this well. They hug the right places and flare the perfect amount. I wish they were just a little bit longer so I could wear with high heels but thats just a preference (as you can see i'm wearing heels in the photo and they look okay where they hit my ankle). I don't usually leave reviews but these exceeding all my expectations and are so flattering and comfy!

Really Nice, Versatile Tall Boots I ordered these tall boots and the color brown. The brown is like a chocolate brown with a slight reddish undertone. I’ve attached a photo for reference, but the boots do look much better on then they do in the photo I ordered these boots in a size 9. For reference, I’m between a size 8.5 and a 9, but I often size up to a 9 in boots because I like to wear socks. So the size 9 fit me perfectly with socks. If you don’t plan on wearing socks, order your true size. The shaft of the boot is fairly wide, so if you have a wider calf, they should fit pretty well. Note, however, that the shaft of the boot is rather tall and it hits the very bottom of my kneecap. I'm 5'4" tall, so if you are much shorter, it may go over your knee. The heel is substantial and easy to walk on. It’s a little higher than I expected, but it will look nice with jeans and dresses. Overall, I’m really pleased with the quality of these boots. The fold leather is soft and realistic looking.

Normally I wear a size 6; in the dark wash I ordered 8 regular and in medium wash 8 short since regular length was unavailable. I love them both, just enough stretch to make them comfortable without being tight. No noticeable shrinkage after washing. The dark pair keeps their shape while wearing, the lighter pair stretched out a little by the end of the day.

5'8, 375 lbs, Apple Shaped- Happy with my purchase, will be buying again! ☺️ My issue with jeans is that they will usually fit my legs but then can't button them. I don't have that problem with these jeans. These jeans have a good ratio of denim to stretch where it stretches enough in the waist if you need that extra inch but also still feels like denim and not jeggings. They are not the thickest material but also not thin. I like the look, I don't think they look cheap for the price. I took these pictures right after taking them out of the package. I am planning on wearing them to an event next week. I will update how they held up through sitting and standing and through a wash. So far I can say I really like them and am happy with my purchase. I hope these pictures can help give you a reference if you are someone shaped like me. UPDATE: The jeans held up great! Did not stretch out, was comfortable to sit, stand, move around for multiple hours. Updated to add pictures wearing the jeans at the event.

Perfect in every way I have such a hard time finding a 38” inseam so when I saw these I had to jump on them and I’m so glad I did. I absolutely love everything about these! They are true to size, so comfortable and so cute. Also can’t beat the price! I will definitely be ordering more of these.

The (short) length is lovely! I’ve been going back and forth with these. They are comfortable. The lining inside is soft and cozy and the leggings are stretchy and the waistband has give that make them lovely for relaxing or just hanging out. I had no problem with sheerness (at least in the lighting I checked in lol). I ordered the short size, because I’m 5’1 and short and the length was great. It’s super hard for me to find comfy and/or activewear bottoms that are the correct length so this was a treat. This may sound nitpicky, but I just really wish they had pockets. I almost always purchase leggings with leg pockets and these don’t have them. The inclusion of a side pocket would make them a five-star for sure. The other reason for deduction is my concern about how they’ll hold up over time. I have machine washed/dried them a couple times and they held up fine, but the material just seems moderate and not great so I think they may end up piling fairly early on. I’ll update over time on how they hold up, but that’s my main quarrel. For the price the material is fair, but I’m taking into consideration since there are other options in a similar price point with less piling-inducing fabric. Otherwise though, I do like these and can’t emphasize enough how amazing it is to find pants like these with the right length!

Super comfortable with a slightly dressy look I ordered the X-Large / 24” inseam Petite, and these fit perfectly. For reference, I’m 5'1", about 195 lbs, and usually wear a large or XL. The length is ideal; they hit about an inch above my ankle bone, which is exactly what I want in a petite pant. The fabric is smooth, stretchy, and very comfortable, with a nice weight to it. They feel thick and high quality without being too hot, so the temperature control is excellent. The waistband is comfortable and flattering, not tight or restrictive. Very flattering from the back! The zippered pockets are very functional; the zippers are small, but the pockets themselves are surprisingly roomy, which I really appreciate. The pants also have a slightly dressy look, making them easy to wear casually or dress up a bit. Overall, these are comfortable, well-made, and thoughtfully designed. I would definitely buy these again and highly recommend them.

Fit great and nice and comfy! I sized up because sometimes I wear a 4 nowadays but some sizes run small in jeans and I kind of expected that with these jeans so I went for a 6 Regular. So I am short and only 5’3” but these jeans aren’t really that long plus I want to tuck them into boots in the winter as it’s getting cold out this time of year. Beautiful Boots! These are really nice boots! I was surprised at the quality for the price point. They run true to size - I normally wear an 8.5 and often struggle to find boots that fit both my feet and calves. I also have one foot that is slightly larger than the other and the front of my feet is very wide compared with my heels. Finding regular shoes is also a struggle because those that fit my heels (and don't rub) often pinch my toes. I've even had a podiatrist suggest that I consider purchasing custom made shoes due to the shape of my feet. I decided to take a chance and order these in my normal size and was pleasantly surprised when they fit perfectly. No pinching my toes and no slipping in the heels! I was also a bit skeptical about whether I would be able to get them on without a zipper that runs the full length of the boot, but there are no issues there either. They are very stylish and can be dressed up or down, as the occasion requires. They are very nicely made and seem to be great quality. I've seen similar boots priced much higher so the value for the money is good. The only reason that I removed a star is because I feel like they would have been even better with more padding for the sole of the foot. The heel height puts quite a bit of pressure on the ball of the foot and more padding would have made them even more comfortable. I plan to wear them mainly to church in the fall/winter for a couple of hours on Sundays, so I don't expect it will be too much of an issue for me. But I don't think I would be able to wear them for extended periods standing on my feet. Of course, I am nearly 60 years old and the padding on the soles of our feet degrades with age (I guess it moves up to our midsection - LOL!). Younger wearers will probably find them just fine.

Fits awkward body type! I have diabetes, and am insulin resistant. Because of this, my stomach bulges like a pregnant lady. It’s hard to find pants that fit right because they are always too tight in the waist and loose on the legs. If I get them to fit my waist right, the legs will be very loose and look so bad and frumpy. I am 5’7 and 175lbs, much of it carried in my stomach area. I chose 12Long but may have been able to size down 1 size, but they would be very snug. If you are my size and body type, I’d say stick with 12L, but if you prefer tight pants go down 1. If you have a big booty, go up 1. These pants fit my stomach AND legs properly. I have longer legs and the length covered my ankles very well and did not rise throughout the day like high waters, even with me bending and stooping down a lot at work. They were very soft and comfortable while still holding me in and giving me a nice shape overall. Made me look like a woman instead of an Oompa Loompa. I have only worn them once, but I have a cat and chinchilla so my black clothes always hold hair, but the hair did not stick to these. 5 stars! Will buy again! FYI: The back pockets are not real pockets.

Comfortable I'm so happy! I've always had issues finding long enough anything. I'm 5'8 140 lbs. I have a shorter torso and longer legs (31.5 from inner top thigh to ankle bone). I usually buy 34 in inseam because I don't like pants to ride up too far above my ankle when sitting. These are perfect. They're light weight, not winter worthy, and breathable. I have thinner legs, thigh is 21.5 in. around, so they aren't tight but fitting and not overly baggy. My waist/ hips area widest point is 39 in. and medium fits well. I do have animals and while hair attaches, it does not stick. If I see any I can easily shake it off. I always wash in cold and line dry so I cannot attest to the dryer and any shrinkage issues. This is my first full day of wearing and if anything changes I'll update.

Love these leggings! Overall, these leggings are great! The material is buttery soft while still feeling supportive, and the stretch makes them incredibly comfortable for all-day wear. I especially love the high-waisted band—it offers just the right amount of tummy support without feeling too constricting. The hidden scrunch and V-back design are subtle but flattering, giving a natural, lifted look. They’re perfect for life on the go or a workout at the gym. For reference, I’m 5’3” and 116 lbs wearing an extra small. I chose the 28” inseam, and while they’re slightly long for my height, I personally like having that little bit of extra length.

Love love love Holy moly, in love with the material. Super comfy, well made. Ordered a medium, I’m 5’8”, 165, they fit perfectly, even the length (see pics), hits right at the top of my feet. They were a little fitted in the bum area on me, so if you have a similar shape, it will be shown off. Also note, this brand carries a matching hoodie separately, and of course- also to die for.

Super comfy! These pants a super soft and comfy! They have so many pockets; 2 normal pockets and then 2 more, one on each leg. The waist hits right at the belly button and is adjustable with drawstrings. The fabric is stretchy and seems like a really nice quality. I am 5’11” with a 36” inseam and weigh 150lbs. I got the size large tall. The only reason I didn’t give 5 stars is because the tall size is still too short on me. It may just be my preference but I like my pants to touch the ground when barefoot and these do not. Most people will not have this issue but just a heads up for the tall girls. Anyway, there are great pants and I would definitely recommend.

Most comfortable work pants ever ! I finally found a pair of work slacks that really feel like yoga pants. These pants are lightweight, breathable and with the right amount of stretch. As you move they feel like a second skin. Perfect for hot humid days. I got a M- gray, the color I got is elegant and the fit is very comfortable. The slacks have an elastic waistband at the back and deep front pockets that fit my S23 Ultra, plus two back pockets that enhance my rear. At 5'5 the length is about 29" falling right at my ankles. Their high waisted style provides me with a smooth, flattering look fit, without a muffin top. I love this material because its 20% stretch makes it perfect for the office, and I don't have to worry about wrinkles, bending, or ironing. These are professionally styled and I'm happy I got them. At that value you should get a few colors.

Almost Perfect I honestly don’t know what magic they used to make these sweatpants, but the material is amazing. They are soft and stretchy with a velvety feel that’s comfortable to wear. I’m usually a size 8 and about 5'6", and I went with the medium in the 29" inseam, which ended up fitting really well. They look more polished than other sweatpants and I also appreciate how they don’t wrinkle easily, which makes them great for travel. The only thing I’m not a fan of is that the outline of the pockets shows through, and that does take away from the elevated look, especially because the pants feel high quality otherwise. Still, aside from that detail, I am really happy with these and love wearing them.

SUPER soft and comfortable! Super comfortable, well-fitting leggings! These are very, very soft. Sometimes the leggings that are described as “buttery” are kind of slick, but these a just super soft. I love the bright red color. The pockets on the side are great for holding my cell phone. The length is perfect and they fit all around (I’m 5’5” and about 125 lbs). It’s high-waisted and feels very comfortable around my waist, not too tight but enough stretchiness to fit snugly. I am wearing black underwear in my pictures so you can see they aren’t see through. If I had to make one complaint, I don’t really like the seams that run up the front of the legs, but definitely not a deal-breaker. I know I’ll wear these often!

Great Dancing Boots! Love these boots. I am 5’8”, slim/average built with long legs. I danced all night in these and not once did my feet get sore. I wear size 11 and true to size. The buckles are a bit on the gold tone but I’m sure no one noticed in the club. Material was pretty good considering the inexpensive cost of these boots. I would buy again.

Good Quality Jeans!! These are so good. I bought a blue wash and the black wash, and they’ve become my two favorite pants that I wear as often as I can. The style is great, goes with all my shirts, can be dressed up or dressed down. I’m 5’3”, 120lbs, and I usually buy size Small/size2-6 on jeans. With these I opted for size 6 because of reviews, and this was a great choice, 6 fits perfectly. The actual material of the jeans feels the same as some of my more expensive jeans. They’re stretchy enough, while still being a good amount of sturdy so that the barrel shape stands out. The butt pockets are actually huge lol! Fits the entire length of my iPhone 14pro with ease and room to spare. Overall i recommend these! This was a golden find on Amazon, I’ll be buying more shades.

Thank you reviews that recommended sizing down First off, I LOVE these jeans! Secondly, the reviews I read on here were a huge help in sizing. It’s weird because this are definitely Women’s jeans, but the sizing is in Juniors. For reference I am 6’0, 170-175 lbs, and a solid Women’s size 10/30, which would translate to a Juniors size 11. Per the suggestion of previous reviews, I reluctantly sized down to a 9. The last thing I wanted was for the jeans to fit for an hour, only to stretch beyond repair. I ordered a size 9 with 34” inseam. I am SO happy I did! When I pulled it out the bag I thought to myself, there’s NO WAY this is gonna work. But I squeezed myself into the initially doubtful fit. Wisely I decided to wear them around the house for a couple hours, w/o removing tags (just in case.. again, because of reviews). And to my pleasant surprise this wonderful pair of jeans decided to form to my figure in the best ways possible. So if you’re in doubt and you got some junk in the trunk, size down and trust the process. Because I could totally see if I had bought these in an 11, I wouldn’t like them now. Also the 34” inseam does almost hit the floor for me, but the next pair I order will be a 36”, just so I have some extra length.

The only thing I can’t on is the washing. Typically I buy one pair of black jeans a year and have to replace them due to fading after washing. I am going to try and keep these ones from fading if I can.

Very comfortable material and beautiful color. I’m 5’6”, 155#, and normally a size medium in bottoms for reference. I ordered the size medium with the 29” inseam in these lounge pants and they fit true to size. Very stretchy and comfortable. The material is super soft, supple, and a little cooling against the skin. I got it in the taupe color and it is a true mushroom taupe. The craftsmanship is superb. Really nice stitching and seams. The only reason I took a star off is that the outline of the pockets is visible through the front of the pants and the price point is a tad high. The 39.99 price point is like what I would pay for jeans, not loungewear.

I love the look and feel of the fabric, and the style of the cargo pockets. I ordered these in dark gray size large. I usually wear bottoms in numerical size US12. The inseam measured 31 inches. For me (5' 4" height) these are perfect if I am wearing shoes that aren't completely flat. They work with my athletic shoes which have some height to them (see photo).I love the look and feel of the fabric.Thes do not have an elastic-banded waist, the crossover style waist stretches due to the stretchy fabric they are mode from. In my photo you can see that I measured the unstretched waist of the pants (flat on the floor) at about 14 inches side to side. My waist is in the mid-thirty inches and these were comfortable. But I would not want anyone to be able to see every bump and bulge of my waist area while wearing these, so for sure I am wearing a longer length top that covers my stomach.These pants are pretty form fitting through the stomach and thighs until they flare out and are wide around the calves.I really like the style and interest that the cargo pockets add.

True to size perfection, if you’re shorter like me choose Petite. Just wow, these pants are seriously the most comfortable, beyond soft and I want all the colors now. I am so happy I ordered them, I actually hesitated as I thought they might be too warm for the almost non existent winter months where I live. Besides the buttery comfort factor, they fit amazing. The cargo style takes the traditional sweatpants up a notch giving them a cool vibe. The semi wide, straight legs isn’t so wide they look clownish and the U Seam Stitching across the backside really emphasizes that area, doing what it should. I have zero complaints. I chose my regular size SP and found the fit comfortable. The elastic waist does not dig in, the drawstrings are functional so you’ve got wiggle room if you prefer a more cinched waistline. Pockets are deep enough for everyday essentials and my iPhone Pro Max. For reference I am 5’ tall and weigh approximately 124lbs with measurements 34DD/27”W/36”H I’ve attached a few photos for you. Lifesaver Fit: I needed stylish slacks for an event. I read many reviews, and saw that many women much heavier than I am were raving about the fit of size medium. Since I am 73 years old and not interested in snug fitting clothing I knew that medium would be perfect for me. I am 5'3" and 120 lbs. I chose the 29" inseam.

A good inseam fit for up to 6’1” women!! I gave these pants a shot and at medium they were too loose annnd way too long, so here is what my sister said: THE Perfect inseam length for women over 5’9”. I would suggest ordering your true size because they’re stretchy but I would not go up a size. Even if you gain 30-40 lbs they will still fit, I promise. The side pockets are nice and lay flat. The seams are well finished & they are incredibly comfortable. I am 20 weeks and all belly & thighs so these are appropriate for work, church, running errands, or going out to lunch.

Beautiful!!! Sexy! My 18 yo daughter LOVES these. They are gorgeous, quality is amazing and they are surprisingly comfortable. The wide calf boot is great because we have LARGE calves and the boots fit both of us. The white is so bright and vibrant, it's amazing. Great value in my opinion.

No complaints, dare I say perfection! I absolutely adore these! Honestly they are so comfortable it almost feels like nothing at all. They drape lovely on the body with the front pockets large enough but yet without adding additional bulk. Nice a smooth to touch and look. There’s a fair amount of stretch for enough wiggle room if you’re carrying a little extra water weight. The waistband is smooth without rolling down. And love that we have the choice to choose our inseam. I am almost always a size small, wearing a size 4us at 27”W/36”H at 124lbs. I chose size small petite. For these, if I could have tried on first I would have chose XS Petite. So something to think about ladies if you’re close to my sizing. The Small Petite is fine, and I’ll make them work. It’s my own fault for not looking closely at the size chart, it actually suggests I order XS. Please look closely prior to ordering. The coffee brown color is a great choice going into fall. I will be wearing these on repeat and think I’ll need another color! I can easily style these for a smart casual look with a sharp white button down, or a simple tee with a coordinating sweater tied around my neck. The length is nice too. I’m barely 5’ but with sneakers or a little kitten heels or ankle boots they hang perfect. Seriously worth the try with versatile styling options, comfy fit and front pockets make these a great value in my opinion.

Super comfortable pants! These pants are very comfortable! You could even wear them to bed. I am going to be wearing these every week. For your reference, I am 5'7" (170cm) and 140 lbs (64kg) in the pictures, and I usually wear a size 2 in pants. UPDATE: These pants do NOT collect dog hair! Even my shiny track pants pick up some dog hairs, but not these. UPDATE: I bought the navy blue on sale, and they are fabulous too!

Then I hung them to dry, and they look great - no ironing needed! These do have a drawstring, but I just let it hang because the elastic was fine for my fit. I am going to be wearing these every week. For your reference, I am 5'7" (170cm) and 140 lbs (64kg) in the pictures, and I usually wear a size 2 in pants. UPDATE: These pants do NOT collect dog hair! My dog sheds a lot, but I can sit on my carpet, couch and bed without using the lint roller before I go out in these pants! It's just amazing! Even my shiny track pants pick up some dog hairs, but not these. UPDATE: I bought the navy blue on sale, and they are fabulous too!

Very cute and trendy denim capris; true to size and great weight I've recently lost 45 pounds and have realized most of my staple wardrobe items are way too big. I don't really like wearing shorts, but capris are a go-to pant for me.I am still trying to figure out what size I should be wearing, and based on the measurements provided in the description I picked a size 12. A SIZE TWELVE!!! I am still having a hard time believing it. The dark blue wash is perfect for me. The capris are very well made; the fabric is sturdy but still lightweight. Soft and stretchy. The distressing and frayed hem are on trend. I like that there are functional front and back pockets, a must for me.For reference, I'm 5'11" and weigh about 180. I have a very straight figure and have a hard time with pants fit, but these fit well enough without any areas where they are too tight or loose. These will definitely be a staple item for me in the months ahead. The price point is a little higher than I like to pay for casual pants, but these should last for a long time. Definitely recommend these and I'm considering getting in another wash.

Gorgeous but Hard to Zip Thigh Highs These are very cute and stylish knee-high boots. The shoe’s outer material is a semi-smooth faux suede and the heel is thick and durable. The sole is made of firm rubber about a centimeter thick, and the inner sole is lightly cushioned. I typically wear a size 6-6.5 in shoes, and got these in a 6.5. My feet are slightly wide, and these boots are more narrow. They squeeze my feet a bit, but the part of the boot that I have the biggest issue with is the zipper. They’re very difficult to zip— easier on the right side, but a real struggle on the left. My feet aren’t wide enough that I’ve ever had an issue zipping my shoes— and I’m even able to zip the shoe a bit first and then get my feet in. I really have the most difficulty zipping the left boot above my ankle. I have thin ankles, so it really doesn’t make much sense. The zipper dug into my finger and calloused it from trying to zip the boot. The fabric on the right shoe came together easily, the zipper just wouldn’t zip, but on the left side the fabric seemed too short and particularly restrictive. I knocked off a star for that, as they were a struggle to get on. Once they’re on, they’re comfortable for the most part (with the left boot feeling tighter than the right). They squeeze my feet, but not enough for me to constantly notice it. They’re not the easiest to walk in. I can walk around in them, but they keep my arch pretty high. They’re the kind of boots that I’d wear if I knew that I was only going to be walking to and from the car and then sitting down. If you’re better at walking in heels or wear higher heels often, you may not be bothered by the heel height. If, like me, you tend to wear shorter heels or stick to wedges and ones with a platform when you wear much higher heels— these could be uncomfortable. I’m 4’11” and they go about 3 inches above my knee, and look really cute. Overall, these are gorgeous boots, but they’re more for fashion than practicality. I love the style and the way they look on me, but I’m not a fan of the zipper and narrow fabric near the ankle of the boot.

Great Fit This is a nice, affordable pair of ladies Aeropostale jeans in a size 6. They are a perfect fit so they are true to size. The jeans are extremely well made, very durable. The jeans are high waisted with flared bottoms. I’m 5”9 and the length is just perfect. The lightweight jeans are very comfortable. The material has a stretch to it so they move with you rather than constrict you, I love how well the jeans fit. I’m impressed by the quality of the jeans, too.

A little cheap but still great! The material is a little odd, but that is both a pro and a con. Con: it's a little thin, a thicker material would make it seem fancier. Watch out for VPL. Pro: it shrunk length in the wash. This was a pro for me because i would have needed to alter it so it wouldn't drag on the ground. I'm 5'6.5, 175-180 lbs, just had a baby 4 months ago. Pre-pregancy i had 30-33 waist and 40-44 hips, now i have about 36 waist and 45 hips (self measured which is inaccurate sometimes) and had a hard time ordering things the right size so i aimed high and ordered a 2x. Fits great, like leggings, the top cinches the waist so if you wear a tight top, it will create an indent in your silhouette, and your tummy pouch will show on crop tops (i don't mind it). Leg is wide, doesn't feel as pajama-y as palazzo pants, but not as nice as dress pants. An easy way to upgrade your outfits and add pizazz. A little more of a blue green than a yellow green. I included a picture and a video to show movement!

Great for the price These flare pants are a soft cottony materiel. I wouldn’t say they are thick but they aren’t super thin or see through. They fit true to size, I normally wear a small but I bought a medium because I was pregnant. I’m 5”2 and 120 normally but in this picture I’m 170. Now that I’m back to pre-pregnant weight, these pants still fit just a little loose. So that tells you how stretchy they are! I’m giving these 4 stars instead of 5 because the fabric is kinda cheap. They wash and dry well but they wear out quickly. They have faded and I’ve had them less than a year.

The most comfortable pants I've ever worn! These pants are just amazingly comfortable! They are light weight, breathable, and just the right amount of stretch, and they are just thick enough to hide bumps and bulges, unlike knit pants. I'm 5'3", 210 lbs, and I got an XXL. They are slightly long on me, but when I have shoes on, they are fine. I could probably have gotten away with an XL for a tighter fit, but I like the comfortable fit of the XXL. They are not overly loose and fit well. They can be casual, and also great for the office. I'm getting more colors :-)

Sooo Comfortable!! These boots are so comfortable. I was in the market for some new ones when my old ones just gave out on me. I usually order from a department store but decided to go this route. So glad I did. I read the reviews and I was sold. I literally wore these all day with no issues. I like the fact that they don't make a lot of noise when walking on tile (an issue I had with my last boot). Everyone heard me coming...lol! I will say the reviews are pretty accurate on the wide leg gap. Even the models on the site show large gaps. However, for me it wasn't that big of an issue. All in all, a great boot that is comfortable, stylish and durable.

90's Style Sweatpants I bought these 90-style sweatpants for a costume: Rachel from Friends "the one with the football". It worked out perfectly. As far as the quality goes, these are good quality in my opinion. They are thick material, the black hasn't faded after multiple washes. My only complaint(s), and they are minor, is that they seem to attract every hair and every lint imaginable, way more than any other piece of clothing I own. That and the elastic at the legs are very tight and it can get uncomfortable after a while. For reference, I am 5'3", 112 lbs, and ordered small.

You can see in the picture that the length is just perfect for me!

Not for taller people My first impression when putting these pants on were regarding the fabric. Buttery soft and very comfortable. I actually kept them on all day because they feel like yoga pants. When it comes to wearing them as slacks, a few things to know…they have a working button, zipper and pockets. So they feel like legit work pants. But much comfier since I could just slip them on without unzipping. They have a lot of stretch to them which is great. I think they are more meant to be worn with a shirt tucked in because the pockets are high up on the high waist rather than on the sides. So they’d be trickier to access with a shirt untucked. The inseam was perfect on me when I pulled them up to the waist. But then the hem was too high for me at 5’7”. And pulling them down to make them longer made the crotch too low. So maybe they’re better for someone a few inches shorter. But with the right shoe, it probably wouldn’t matter. Overall they’re a great pant. Just wish they were longer. I’ll be wearing them with an untucked shirt just because the fabric does hug the body enough to show your shape and I’m not crazy about mine. For reference, I am 165 pounds. Waist is 35” and hip is 42” and I got an XL.

Nice Stretchy Wide-leg Pants Great little pants. I’m 5’6” and weigh 165, large fit great BUT I have long legs and wish I selected the longer pants. Having said that, the large are 31” inseam and come all the way down to your shoes. These pull up, zipper/buttonless jeans have decent pockets. The material feels good and is stretchy—doesn’t seem like denim. They are comfy. These will look good with office shoes or a bit dressier with heels. I have extra wide hips, please see attached pictures to see how this looks. When I ordered these, they were $42. Today there’s a coupon on it, making it an okay value.

Size 10, white I love these chinos. They’re cut really well and very cute. I got them at a great price. I usually am a size 6 in stretchy jeans and 10 with no stretch/100% cotton, but in the Amazon essentials chino shorts I’m a 10 so I ordered these in 10 but these are definitely cut roomier than the shorts. Still, they don’t fall down at all throughout the day and my only other white pants (jeans) are butt tight. These as with the shorts have a little stretch. I showed close up of how long they fall on me: 5’9, long legs. I wore black underwear with them and although you could tell a little my husband said you couldn’t really unless you were staring. Don’t do what I did and cut the tag out carelessly! I cut a hole right in the fabric (not on seam!) and had to sew it. It is tiny and no one would notice but I mean I do. The one criticism I could maybe have for Amazon brand chinos (these and shorts) are that the button seems to be sewn in flimsily. I’d get these if went on sale in another color, but would probably get an 8. I’ll dry these in dryer since they’re big and come back to update if the fit changes drastically. They’re very comfy and I even took a long, hot walk in them. They don’t get too wrinkled (I didn’t steam or iron these I just wore them out of the package; I know they’re not perfect but good enough for the day I wore them!). Edit: I tumble dried these on low heat and they shrunk a TINY bit, just enough that the size 10 is now perfect. Not too short or too tight after the dryer. As long as I hung them up immediately, no ironing needed.
These are perfect length for shorties like me: I’m 5’2 barely. I got a size Medium. And wear a size 8. It has an elastic waist. And they aren’t over powering baggy. If you know what I mean: 10/10!

The perfect mom Jean The perfect Jean for a postpartum mom! I’m 7 months postpartum and have been living in sweats and leggings and these jeans feel better than some of my sweats. So buttery soft, and length is perfect (I’m 5’3” and in a 6 short). About to order another color!

Love!!!!🤎 Worth every penny ! I am a bit thicker on the bottom weight around 140-145 height is 5’1 a bit long yes but nothing crazy so a large fit just perfect not tight and not huge perfectly comfortable

Super comfy and warm! These sweats are exactly as described. The material is a slick feel on the outside that I am not familiar with but they are definitely fleeced lined on he inside giving that warm feeling. I wore them with an oversized sweatshirt and sneakers and even though I was in a baggy look; I felt super cute and comfortable. My inseam is a 33. For reference. I am in the process of losing prednisone weight but still deal with an arthritic inflammatory disease. I’m 5’8 and 188#;. Longer legs with a shorter torso, a perfect fit is large or size US 12. Weight is mostly in tummy area now; 13# left to go. I still go one size up for extra looseness in clothing but it’s almost too big now to do so. Shoe size is about a 7 wide when inflamed. 7 medium when normal. US shoe size 7-8 is about normal depending on shoe. FYI: online purchases I try to keep rating simple. If I was in a local store and could hold and view it; 5 star would buy and take it home, one star would put it back on shelf and pick something else.#OnlyJesusSaves.

Excellent pants with some caveats I'm 5'8", about 120 - 125 lbs, but have a really short torso and the inseam of someone closer to 5'10". I first ordered these pants in size medium in white (pictured) and I loved them so much that I also ordered them in green. For reference, I'm wearing the pants pulled up to my natural waist and wearing 3" heels. I thought the medium fit great and looked ok on my butt, until I tried on the size small in green which looked way better on my butt than the medium, and better all-around. The problem with the green is that they are about 1.5" shorter than the white, despite the product page saying the inseam on small and medium are the same. The picture speaks for itself - clearly they are not the same length. Someone else made this same comment with a photo about the white vs. blue, only she ordered the exact same size and the white were a lot longer. I did not find them to be see-through at all as others have mentioned, but perhaps that's because I didn't buy a skin tight fit. I received many compliments on the white pair and will keep them, but unfortunately any other color seems to be too short, so the green pair is going back. Update: I ordered the blue in medium and they are as long as the white medium so it seems as if the color doesn’t matter, but the small is a lot shorter than the medium even though the product page says the inseam is the same.

Soft, Warm, and Great Fit for Tall Sizes These fleece-lined yoga pants are some of the softest and most comfortable I’ve owned. The fleece lining makes them cozy without being too heavy, and they’re perfect for colder weather. I really appreciate the option to choose inseam length—at 5’9” with more room in the thigh and booty area, the XL 33” Tall fit me perfectly. They have a nice high rise that stays in place and a wide waistband that smooths without digging in. The back pocket is bigger than most yoga pants and can easily hold my phone and keys, which is super convenient. The straight-leg cut also makes them versatile enough for lounging, errands, or even casual wear outside the house. If I had to point out one thing, it’s that they’re definitely warm, so they might feel too heavy for warmer days—but for fall and winter, they’re perfect.

too long, (way) too wide at the hips What I love about these jeans is that they truly are high-rise, straight-legged, and fit me well around my waist. I'm 5'4" and weigh around 176, so I got a women's 20 petite. What I dislike is that these seem to be made for a height of someone who is around 5'6". In addition, though perhaps it's hard to see here, the hips are way too large, and I doubt going down a size would make much difference. While I understand that these are to be relaxed fit, these are more like no-fit. Nothing like the photo in any case. Having said that, I know Lee is a good brand, it's hard to find black, high-rise jeans so I'm having them altered by having them taken up a good inch or so and having them taken in at a cost of around $35.00 (which really isn't too bad).

I was surprised by the fit - I ordered a 2 Short, I'm 5'4", 115lbs. They were slightly loose, sitting below my true 27" waist at 31" on hips. I like the color and am hoping a hot wash/dry will shrink them some even if they stretch again during wear (like a good pair of cotton denim jeans) but not fade. Was looking at the new Pierce model in-store, but received a 2023 version of this.

Runs GARGANTUAN! Should've read the reviews first because these pants are huge!!!! I ordered a small but they felt like they were twice the size. They are also tall, so if you're say 5'7 and under you might be safer ordering petite or just stick with rolling the pants leg up.

Comfy & cute Was looking for jeans that would be comfy for traveling & these are great. The elastic waist and slightly stretchy fabric made them comfy even on the plane and for a long car ride. I gave them 5 stars because they seem well made with a nice weight denim & fit according to the size chart. They did not shrink noticeably after washing according to directions & hanging to dry. My only criticisms are the front pockets are on the smaller side so I have to put my phone in the back pocket. Also, although they did fit me, if you have a larger backside you may need to size up. I'm 5'7/170cm tall and generally wear a US size 16 in jeans, and I bought these in size XL.

The best pants I love these pants, I wear them all the time. They’re great for wearing around the house or dressing up and going out. I got my first pair in white from Marshall’s, then I got 2 more pairs from Amazon in black and navy. Out of al the linen pants I own, these are the most comfortable and easiest to care for. They’re a little too long when I first get them, but once I washed and dried them, they slightly shrank, which is perfect for me since I’m 5’3”. If you’re taller and it fits great, maybe air dry and iron .

Tall Girl Win 20/10 love the fit, feel and length! I am 6 ft tall, 160-165 lbs, athletic build. These fit AH-MAZING. I am FLOORED. I ordered a Large with the 36’ inseam. Praise these pants for TOUCHING MY FEET! It’s a small miracle. I’ve been nothing but legs my whole life and this is soooo satisfying. They are light but not cheap feeling, comfortable, breathable. Very affordable. I’m obsessed with the pockets, I’m going to need someone to bring back pockets to women’s clothing, because.. WOW! I could see this for lots of outdoor adventures, I bought mine for fishing (lighter khaki color). But I could totally see how these would be super great to hike in. I may be investing in quite a few more pairs! Totally recommend!

Gorgeous! These are beautiful quality brown faux suede boots with a lot of design detail. They look a lot more expensive than what they really are. Even though I am 5’8” these boots reach to a little over the bottom of my knees. They have an extra tall shaft so if you are a short person just know they have an 18” shaft so for me 18” reaches a little over the botyom of my knee and I have long legs because I am 5’8”. Now as much as I like these fabulous chocolate brown boots, I have a wide foot and they are too snug. So if you have a boxy wide foot like me these are not going to be comfortable.

Look and Feel Great These are wonderful pants for summer. They are lightweight but no see through and the khaki color is perfect. They are true to size - small for 5;4”, 120 pounds. They would go great with a linen blouse or just a nice tee top. I washed them and they look as good as when I took them out of the package. They’re exactly what I wanted.

Very boxy…but very very soft I got the clear blue sky in a size small, and I am 5’4 and 125 lbs. Let me say that the fit is nothing like the pictures. It’s boxy. You can see in one of the photos I took how much the fabric stands away from my body. From the pictures on the model I judged it to be a more close-fitting cut, and longer. The neckline is also wider than it looks in the pictures, and the sleeves are a bit loose and long. That said, the color is spot on, and it is so super duper soft. Supima cotton for the win. I’ve washed and tumble dried it already, and no shrinkage (or very slight). I recommend sizing down. I’m only keeping it because it’s a hassle for my dad to do returns.

Fits great. Y'all these fit so cute!! Im a medical SW intern. Im 4'8 and 130 ish lbs. They have stretch and good quality for the price. I don't think they super thick but they defiently not thin either. I dont know how wrinkle resistance works as I haven't worn them out. Cute! Shrank but still fits. I am 5’9” and weigh 190 lb. I typically wear a US size 12-14 and ordered an XL. The photos were taken before they were washed, and one leg was slightly shorter than the other, maybe 1/2-1”. The first time I washed them I only let them dry about 15 minutes on low temp then hung them up to finish drying. They needed to be ironed afterward and did not shrink. The second time I washed them I forgot about them, and they went through a full dry cycle on low temp. They came out wrinkle free but shrunk 2-3” in length. They also shrank in width but still fit. They wear like hot pants from the 60s. The material is a stretchy twill and I’ve received numerous compliments on them. They also hold their shape well during wear. My only caveat is that it has no pockets. I’d love a hidden pocket in the back. Overall, I think these are a good value for the money and will order more.

They wear like hot pants from the 60s. The material is a stretchy twill and I’ve received numerous compliments on them. They also hold their shape well during wear. My only caveat is that it has no pockets. I’d love a hidden pocket in the back. Overall, I think these are a good value for the money and will order more.

Really warm, unique In the item description it mentions a 2-in platform, there is no platform hidden in these boots, they actually feel like slippers and there's just a small bit of cushioning between you and the ground.(The sole measures 1 in) I'm actually very happy for this because I did not want a platform but I took a chance on them anyway because they were so unique looking. When I open the package they did have a unpleasant odor, it wasn't overpowering but I'm also not sensitive to these things. The odor dissipated after I left them out of the packaging for a couple of days. I have thicker calves (16.5 in) And I am normally a US size 8, I ordered these in an 8.5 and I'm glad I did because they are perfectly snug on my foot with a pair of thin socks and I'm afraid the eight might have been too tight. I did have to struggle to get them up my thick calf but once they got there they were actually comfortable and didn't restrict, but I would not suggest this for anyone with calves thicker than mine. Also they are very warm, but I imagine they would be terrible in rain or snow since they are just a faux fur fabric. The soul is a brown rubber with treads. To give you an idea of height I am 5'3 And these came up to right under my knees.

Petite Small vs. Petite Medium I found the sizing chart to be somewhat confusing, so I have notes on both Petitie Small and Petite Medium! I posted a picture of me wearing the Petite Small and the other picture is me in the Petite Medium. It's difficult to tell by the photo but the Medium is looser. Petite Small (actual pants measurements): Waist 30" circumference Hips 38.5" Calf 13.5 Hip to crotch: 11.5" approx Petite Medium (actual pants measurement): Waist 31" Hips: 40" Calf: 16" Me: waist 28" Hips 35" Calf 13" The small fits on me, but snug in the calf. The medium also fits. The waist band is thick 2.5" but does have stretch. The actual material of the pants does not have stretch. Overall, the medium is looser like sweat pants but looks fine with a fitted top! I wear both the small and medium! A lot of my coworkers wear these in a tighter fit especially if they are pear-shaped and these pants look like leggings on them. :)

NOT SHORT GIRL FRIENDLY!!!!!!!!! NOT SHORT GIRL FRIENDLY. I was to start this review with saying do not order if you’re a short girl. I, thankfully, know how to sew and was able to hem the pants myself. For the average shirt girl, I would strongly avoid, unless you can sew or want to pay a tailor to alter them. I am 5’2 and had to cut off five inches from the bottom. You can see in my photo that I have it pinned in one, I was measuring for the hem. The length look ridiculous, honestly it looked like I was wearing scuba fins with how much fabric there was at the bottom. To GTP: either shorten the length to adjust to the average woman’s height (5’3-5’5) or provide a petite option because it truly was ridiculous. The pros are that they are stretchy and indeed comfortable. The quality is as expected from TGP, as always it’s not great but it’s not bad. They look cute, but they don’t look like real jeans either.

Yes Yes Yes 5’9 135lbs - Size Medium OK, I have a lot to say about these joggers because I am on the tall side and have always struggled with finding a nice versatile jogger that fits me in a flattering way. I love everything about these joggers. Material thickness: Perfect. I’ve worn these joggers to the gym, out to dinner with a lil jean jacket and some sneakers and have proudly lounged quite comfortably around the house/doing errands. Washing: 0 problems or leakage of color. Stretchiness: PERFECT. Waistband does NOT dig into your stomach. You can roll them down or keep them up and they stay put. Colors: Light grey, dark grey, black (awful with lint though), cream, olive green. 10/10 you can legit buy 3 of these for the price of 1 nike jogger and honestly, nike joggers just suck these days.

Best work pants I've found. These are exactly what I've been looking for. Surprisingly Petite Approved! I’m petite but these work for me! I’ve been on the hunt for low rise baggy/wide leg jeans that are also fitted on the glutes and I’m very happy with these! For reference I’m 5’0 ~110-112lbs, waist is 25” and hip measurement is 37”. I’m usually a 0-00 depending on the brand. In Abercrombie I’m a 24 short in the Curve Love line. My inseam is 27” and these are definitely on the longer side for me but they suit the baggy look and will pair really well with chunky sneakers, heels, heeled boots etc. The material is thick and stretchy and they are extremely comfortable. Quality is similar to other premium brands - I’d say fabric thickness and stretch is on par with Good American. Perfectly loose, comfy, and colorful I have these pants in 4 different designs. The pair I’m wearing in these pictures are my favorites, but they all are very cute and colorful. I had ankle reconstruction surgery and wanted pants that were comfortable, soft, cute, easy on and easy off, and would allow easy access to my ankle and foot during PT. They also had to be long enough and loose enough to cover my shoes…I have to wear 2 different shoes because my surgical foot and ankle is so swollen I had to buy a tennis shoe 2 sizes larger and wider than I normally wear. These check all the boxes. They are perfect. I will wear them even after I heal. I’ve worn them to PT, dinner, church, and during a day out with my husband. Well made, very high heel, smaller calf, possible pinch point I’ve obtained several pairs of boots for the winter. These run smaller than others, and are smaller through the calf as well. They do still fit but may cause issues if worn for long periods. I also noticed that the edge of the top of the boot comes rich at the bend in my knee. I noticed that as soon as I sat down with them on. I would prefer not having that hit at that spot and worry that it will rub or pinch. I’m not sure but that may just be an issue for someone at my same height (5’2’) or shorter. They look very nice and have the look that many like with the larger than usual soles and the sole pattern. These are very high. They feel good - soft and smooth with nice lining. I’m not sure I would pay this much for another pair considering the smaller calf and height at the knee bend.

Super cute sexy and so very flattering. My 2nd pair. 30 inch inseam was just right. I'm 5'1" 114 pounds and went with my normal size of small which was true to size and a perfect fit. This is actually my 2nd time purchasing these pants. The first time I went with a 28 inch inseam and they were the right length but due to the tight fit it caused the legs to raise just a little and it wasn't exactly where I wanted them to be. This time I went with the 30 inch inseam and they were absolutely perfect. These are made of a good quality stretchy soft comfortable polyester material. They are tight fitted but have plenty of stretch. The buttocks has a scrunched fitted design to help shape and make your butt more flattering and sexy which it definitely does. I've had several women ask me where I bought these. I use these for work so I do wish they had front pockets. That's the only downside to these pants. They do have two back pockets which can be buttoned up to keep your items, especially cash or your debit card, from falling out. My phone is too big to button up but the pocket still holds my phone. I use the other back pocket for my keys and vape. This is where the front pocket would come in handy because I also have to carry an ink pen with me and when I wear these I have to clip my pen to the neck of my shirt which constantly pokes me in the face and my makeup gets all over it from rubbing my neck. If these had even a simple small front pocket I could use it for my ink pen. Aside from that these are absolutely perfect and I love them even more now that I have a longer length. They are super flattering to the legs thighs and butt and very sexy. Highly recommend. I do wish these were a little cheaper though. It gets expensive trying to buy enough for the work week at $30 a pair.

true chocolate brown, roomy, stays up, and comfortable! For reference, these are my true measurements: bust - 38” waist - 31.5” hips 46”, and I’m 5’6 around 185. I hold a good amount of weight in my hips/glutes, and my stomach is on the natueal flatter end, with a natural lower belly protruding. I purchased a Large! PROS: I really like the pants and truly underestimated them! For someone with a hourglass shape/bigger waist to hip ratio, I was looking for wide trousers that had room around my leg but cinched at the waist, and these pants do! It has elastic in the back and a button/zipper in the front, with belt loops all around. With buttoning the pants, the elastic in the back allowed for a secure & tight, yet still comfortable fit around my waist—with and w/o shirt tucking. I am closer to 5’6.5 and a half than 5’6, with my torso & leg being of equal length. I ordered the 31” inseam pants, and wore them with a pair of 1/1.5 inch platform loafers; they are the perfect long length without dragging on the floor. CON: the only issue I had is the loose pouch that I got around my crotch area (see pic). Pretty typical for my body type with any loose-fitting pants, just because of its tailoring for straighter figures. Unless I pose a certain way, it is slightly apparent when walking. But doesnt bother me too much—as long as you wear an appropriate shirt that compliments the bagginess, it won’t pull in as much attention. I will be ordering in different colors!

Great everyday outdoor pant! Wore these in Iceland and they are the most comfortable, flattering pant ever! I love how long they are as I am 6'1 and these come at the perfect length. I’m Obsessed!! From the moment I put these on, I was feeling very comfy and warm with the fleece lining. They are nice and thick- perfect for winter!
