👠 Easy slip on Design, Flexible Soft Fabric and Petite Inclusive Makes These A WINNER ! For Reference I am : 168lbs , 5’4” Size Large I'll be the first to admit, my initial reaction to these pants was skepticism. As I unboxed them, I thought to myself, "These are going to be a disappointment." The fabric, while incredibly soft to the touch, has a clingy quality to it that I wasn't sure I'd be comfortable with. But, as I decided to give them a fair shot, I slipped them on and immediately Changed me mind about them. Moving around in the clingy fabric, which I had initially seen as a drawback, actually is quite flexible and comfy. I received these in my normal size Large in the color Dark Gray. The fantastic part of all this is that they actually come in a petite length which is so wonderful for a person like me who is on the shorter side. The fact that I don’t have to worry about my pants dragging on the ground or having to pay to have them hemmed is FANTASTIC ! The petite length is perfectly proportioned and hits me at my ankle. As per my measurements the inseam is XXX” and not to mention that when I am wearing pants that are the right length, I instantly feel I look more put together and polished. I don't have to worry about tripping over my own hem, or not being able to show my shoes. Another feature that really stands out to me is the waistband. It's like a little piece of heaven no annoying, tight elastic that digs into my skin, just a smooth, flat design that stays put all day. I've had issues with waistbands rolling down in the past, but that's not a problem here. The only downside I've noticed is that the pockets could be a bit roomier. They're not tiny or anything, but if you've got big hands or like to carry a lot of stuff with you, you might find them a bit cramped. Also, because the fabric is on the thinner side, when I put my hands in my pockets, it can look a bit awkward - my hands kind of bulge out and it's not the most flattering look (check out the picture I've included to see what I mean). Despite those minor quibbles, I'm really enjoying these pants. The comfort level is top-notch. In closing, I have to say that these pants might not be everyone's cup of tea, and I'm still on the fence about whether they're really my style. However, The softness of the fabric is unparalleled, Whether I'm running errands, lounging around the house, or just need something to throw on to walk my pooch these ft the bill. I think they look best with sneakers or sandals, and can easily be dressed up or down depending on the occasion. They are being offered in 10 colors , in tall, petite and regular lengths and 6 sizes. They are machine washable And dryer friendly.

8 1/2 shoe has 16” shaft height & not a good fit for short thick legs. I ordered these in black and gray in size 8.5 regular. They are nice boots, true to size as far as the shoe part. The heel is a nice height and the material is soft & leather like. The shaft height on the item description says 14.56 inches but does not include the shoe size with that height. When I measure both pairs, the shaft height is closer to 16 inches on an 8.5 size shoe. I’m a 5’2” with thick legs & very muscular calves which always cause an issue with boots and shaft size. The design of the shaft in this boot is curved. Since my legs are short, my calf muscle is at the very bottom part of this curve where they just begin to widen, resulting in a tight calf fit, while the top of the shaft is big and gaps a bit. The shaft WIDTH at the top is 16” (8” X 2 laid flat in the pics). Where the biggest part of my calf starts is 6 inches below the top, where it is only14 inches (7” X 2) so it’s really tight there, and hard to zip until you get to the part that’s wider. So if I switch to wide calf they will really be gaping and sloppy at the top. The gray was too tight to be comfortable, but the black was a little bigger (but snug) & I could zip it. The top of both pairs hit just below my knee cap. A 14 -15 inch shaft fits much better and is more flattering, but good luck finding a pair that height in an 8 1/2 shoe. They need to change that shaft height in the description, that’s the reason I ordered these. I’m returning the gray and on the fence with the black. I wish I could find a 14 inch shaft height.

Pants in a pinch I purchased the lite khaki and grey. They are okay in terms of fit. I’m 5’2” at 130lbs and bought size small. You’ll need blouses that cover the camel-toe and undies lines. They feel super smooth and the thick band holds the tummy a bit. Black undies will show through the lite khaki but the grey seem to mask it pretty well. If you need pants you can just throw on, then these are good for the price. The camel-toe, somewhat sheer, and show of lines is what bugs me about them.
The fit was good, but the length is a little long for them being "short". Size 16 short, 5'3"

Flattering and also wrinkly I got a medium size as someone who is 5'5, around 28 in around the waist. When I got the pants, it was wrinkly. I rarely give only 3 stars, but these "jeans" were a disappointment for me. First the material is not denim. It looks like it, but it definitely has a polyester feel. I love jeans with stretch, but these are more like stretch pants made to look like jeans. And even though I ordered the longer length - and I am only 5'6" - these pants feel short to me, like they hit just below my ankle bone, which would be okay if they were "skinny jeans", but having the wide legs, it looks like I am wearing the wrong size of pants. The wide waist band is nice, but the drop from the top of the band to the crotch area is too big for a medium so as I wear them it gets are crunched up and though they are not tight, I get that "smile" look in the front. I'm just not a fan.

Cute Lace Pantyhose Looks just like the seller's photos. I like the floral pattern of the lace. The price was good and they have stretch but they are lace so they are basically like a netting. I've seen some styles that have the lace pattern on top of a traditional pantyhose, this is not that. It is all lace and no other feature. There isn't any compression either, it's lace pantyhose and then a half inch waistband at the top. The waist came up to my natural waist but I am short. This may not be long enough for anyone over 5' 5". I've only worn these twice and have been gentle with them. I don't expect them to last longer than a season b/c they are all lace with no reinforcement anywhere. Next time I might look for a similar pair but that have a traditional nylon with the lace for durability and a reinforced toe (this pair has no reinforced toe).

These are not like the cheap ones I have. Very well made and not thin. I love the fabric these are made of. Heavy and cozy. I need to shorten them as they are really long and I am 5'8". The picture shows I need to shorten about 5" overall.

Very comfortable, fit great! At 5'5",183 lbs I am currently in a size 14 and these definitely fit true to size. I have more of a straight up and down figure, but they were not cutting in on my tummy. I do have it tiny bit of a muffin top, but not so much that I felt like I needed to go a size up. These pants are super comfortable. They are thick like jeans, but stretchy like leggings. I intentionally showed them with one boot in and one out so you can see that even though I would consider them a skinny jean cut, the pant leg has enough room to wear over the top of my ankle boots. They are also cut small enough, they could be worn tucked inside boots. If you wanted to wear them with taller boots, they would be nice and comfy because they would not be bulky. Overall, I gave these mid-rise jeans five stars because they are not only comfortable, but they fit nicely and are versatile. You can dress them up or dress them down according to your own style. You should definitely give them a try!

A great, practical boot for daily us I'm writing this based on my wife's experience with these boots. She has found them to be a practical option for daily wear. The main positive she reports is comfort. She has worn them for full days at a time without any issues or breaking-in period. The flat heel and round toe seem to work well for her. They have also held up in wet conditions. She wore them in the rain on one occasion and mentioned that her feet stayed dry and the boots showed no immediate damage. For sizing reference, she is 5'2". The shaft of the boot hits her right below the knee, which was the fit she was looking for. One thing to note is the tread. It's fine for paved surfaces like sidewalks and floors, but she felt it wouldn't provide enough grip for uneven or rugged terrain. Overall, based on her use, these are a solid and functional boot for general daily and urban use.

Lightweight, Comfy, and Long Enough For Me I’m 5'9" with a 27" waist and 37" hips, and I ordered a small (usual size) and the Tall inseam. Honestly, I’m relieved it worked — but just barely. These fit me perfectly at the ankle, but if I were even half an inch taller, we’d be entering “accidental cropped pant” territory. Length aside, these pants are fantastic. The fabric is lightweight, stretchy, and breathable, and they somehow manage to feel like lounge pants while looking polished enough for errands, travel, or even a casual round of golf. The zip pockets are a small but mighty victory — finally, a place to stash keys or a phone without worrying they’ll escape mid‑stride. The high rise and tapered leg make them flattering without being restrictive, and they pack down beautifully without wrinkling. They’re the kind of pants you put on for a hike and then forget to change out of because they’re that comfortable. If you’re on the taller side, definitely go for the Tall inseam — and if you’re taller than 5'9" check your inseam measurements.

Comfortable on plus size body! I am 5’7”, roughly 240lbs, size 18 and 2XL in tops. Was super nervous ordering based on reviews but I just went with my usual size, figuring they probably don’t stretch much, and I am glad I did. Size 18 fit perfect over my muffin top and around my waist. I was worried about length based on other reviews but these being high waisted jeans don’t drag on the ground for me and will be perfect with a basic sneaker. Looking forward to wearing them for Christmas photos with a sweater.

Size down! Good stretch Final review! 11/5/2022 So, like I feared, the waist was tight when I went down two sizes. I posted pics labeled size 2. I LOVE how they fit my butt and thighs, but the waist was too uncomfortable. I the pictures you can see that there is give if I pull it, but when it sits naturally on me it is uncomfortable. I tried these on at the END of my day to truly feel how they felt after a day of normal eating. These are made for women who have a small waist and large butt and hips. Not for my body type. I brought it down to 4 stars instead of 5 I love these. I left a video with details too. I got a size 6, but can definitely go down a size. I'm 5'6 about 133lbs and have a 32in inseam. These do not flatten the butt. Good stretch ALL around. The button is flimsy and seems like plastic. But I'm not too concerned because of how well they stretch. I gain weight in my mid section. Gonna buy more in different styles and colors. Hopefully this company is consistent with their sizing. UPDATE FOR SIZE 10/28/2022 So I did it. Went down a size from 6 to 4. I added photos with the size labeled. I did return them to try a size 2. As you can see from the pictures. It was still a bit big. I was very on the fence in returning them for. Smaller size. I decided to walk around my house and wear them a bit to see how they felt while I was moving naturally in them AND I did NOT like how they felt to loose in the butt area. I'm crossing my fingers to see if the size 2 are better. My worry is that the waist will be too tight and the butt/hip too loose still. But I will update for everyone

Order up if you want to wash! I gave these pants five stars because I like them a lot once I figured out what to buy. I usually wear a size 2/4 depending on the brand and am 5’6” tall, 120 pounds. The first pair I ordered were on this site called “ The millennium”. I ordered a small but received a medium and didn’t realize it. (they might’ve sent it to me because Company knew they were small ?) They were a little big when I tried them on and then I washed them and they fit perfect! So order up.I bought some more colors which I thought were the same, but weren’t. They were called “Point Pants”. Same brand and looked the same in the picture but weren’t the because they had more nylon in them than the millennium. I ordered a small and they fit perfect and didn’t really shrink. They were much thicker which would be good for winter, but it is spring and I wanted a thinner pant so I am keeping the medium size millennium pants so I can wash them. I hope this makes sense because these pants are great for winter and spring and summer. And they are also a great price!PS. I would’ve liked to have them a little shorter, but they were all out of the “short”pants.

They look like real jeans! I had surgery about 5 weeks ago and can't wear real jeans for a few months to protect my incisions. I was getting really sick of wearing nothing but joggers and pj pants so I decided to try these. I got the size small in the long/33inch inseam because the regular length felt a little short/flood-y on me. These are PLENTY long on me! In my pictures I'm wearing boots with a 3.5 inch heel and I'm 5'6 and they are perfect length with the heel. The waistband offers a little compression type support which was actually perfect for my post surgery recovery. It felt really nice to be able to have a cute outfit to wear to an early Thanksgiving dinner yesterday and feel put together for the first time in weeks! The waistband didn't roll at all, and these didn't bunch or sag or become loose during the day. The fabric is stretchy and soft and they held their shape perfectly and I'd definitely recommend to anyone healing from an abdominal surgery or for people who just don't like wearing jeans. If you're a leggings girl-you'll love these. The "claw marks" in the description is just the worn look "whiskering" at the hip area. These have a cross over yoga-pant style waistband. I prefer real jeans, but these are definitely great for me for the time being!These have real pockets too!They did have a slight funky smell right out of the package but not nearly as bad as another brand I tried! And the smell goes away if you wash with oxyclean + detergent.

Comparing NCL32 Cotton Leggings + HDL Active Flex What I ordered: NCL32 Cotton: - sizes: x-small & small -1in waistband HDL Active Flex: - sizes: small and medium -1in waistband My Measurements: - waist: 26in - hips: 37.5 - height: 5’3” - weight: 123lbs Here is a breakdown of the specs: HDL Active Flex: - 84% polyester, 16% spandex - made in Vietnam -buttery soft -sizes offered: small-3x -doesn’t shrink -unavailable in 1in waistband capri style with various sizing NCL32 Cotton: - 95% cotton, 5% spandex - made in the US -soft, but not nearly as soft as the Active Flex series -sizes offered: x-small–5x -available in 1in waistband capri style w/ sizing between x-small–3x Conclusion: I tried on both leggings and prefer the softer texture and fit of the Active Flex series. Since polyester in the Active Flex is more durable than cotton in NCL32, I wanted to actually size down to an x-small to achieve that smoothed out, perfectly slim look while still maintaining comfort and stretch. I’m disappointed in the company for not offering that size as an option in full-length or capri in the Active Flex 1in waistband. It seems the closest fabric blend being offered in a capri style is their Buttery Soft series at 92% polyester, 8% spandex, but they only offer One Size, One Size Plus, 1x–3x, and 3x–5x. I will gladly buy from this seller again if they make the Active Flex with a 1in waistband in x-small and begin to offer it in the capri style. For now, I’ll be sticking with Active Flex in size small to see how long it lasts.

Sleek and comfy and great choice of inseam lengths These pants are fabulous for either lounging at home or at work.. The material is 81% polyester and 19% spandex, so there’s plenty of stretch and this material needs no special laundering, comes out of the dryer wrinkle free. What makes these pants look a little dressier to me is that sharp front seam. Very classy looking! The waist clings much like a pair of leggings. I think this is actually designed to be that way to lend shape to your silhouette. I don’t find the waist overly tight or uncomfortable. I like the zippered side pockets for an athleisure flare.The big attraction for me was the choice of a 29 inch inseam because I am petite and have spent my life shortening pants! I ordered a size small with the 29 inch seam and I am 5 feet tall, 117 pounds. These fit perfectly and just drape over the top of my shoe. I will enjoy this alternative style compared to jeans, joggers, or leggings on occasion. Great quality and I highly recommend!

Different colors different fabrics I decided to try these because I was looking for high-waisted drawstring leggings for running. I have a lot of leggings I already love for lifting or for being comfortable, but they don’t always stay up well or are more compression than I want when running. I ordered the dark blue-green and the gray heather. The dark blue green arrived first and I happily took them for a test drive (run) shortly after. They worked great for a 5k, at no point did I feel the need to hike them up or adjust. I was also really happy with the material, they are smooth and soft and buttery with a nice sheen to them. Then the gray ones arrived. And I’m not UNhappy with them…but they are simply not as impressive. They are a thinner, cheaper feelings material. Maybe this is just the nature of heather print anything, and it’s not that it’s necessarily bad that they’re thinner…but following the material of the first pair of leggings, I feel pretty meh about them. I’m not going to return, because I’m sure they’ll still do the job with the high waist and drawstring, but certainly would not order another “heather” color. If the gray pair had been the first and only that I ordered to try, I would not have ordered a second pair. They are pretty thin and feel more like literal tights than standard leggings do. I also ordered racerback shirts from this brand, and the same can be said for those in the heather color vs solid colors - solids feel and look high quality, the heather is kind of scratchier and doesn’t fit/lay the same as the other material. So to summarize, fully recommend the solid color leggings. Personally wouldn’t buy the heather ones or recommend them. I would say they are both true to size, I wear a medium in leggings and the medium fit well (5’6”, 140 for reference). OH also - tie the drawstrings before washing, they are on the short side and I had to fish them out after the first wash.

Get Ready Boots… Groovy. Retro. Spunky…and most of all, Comfortable. Sleek boots at a great price. Appearance is the primary feature of these boots. They elongate my leg which is definitely a plus, considering that I am 5’3. I feel comfortable walking and standing with these boots on. They also give me a confident stride. I ordered the lighter color, which has a hint of pink, depending on the light. They do attract scuff marks and dirt easily. That means I need to watch my step (lol) to keep the boots spotless. However, they do clean up easily with a white non-abrasive sponge. I’m not sure what type of material was used for these boots, but it’s amazingly buttery. Supple and flexible. The heel appears steep, however, if you measure from the top of the platform to the top of the heel, that’s the actual height of the heel. The inside of the boot is cushioned. The zipper is durable. The boots run true to size. The boots are sewn and not glued completely. Let me see. What else would you like to know ? Water resistant?- Yes. Slip resistant?- Very. The soles are made of textured rubber material. Easy to put on ?-Definitely. And that is due to the length of the zipper which provides a wide opening when unzipped. At the end of the day…”These boots are made for walkin’ .” (N.S.)

Super soft and comfy! I love these jeans so much! I barely lost 80 lbs and thought I had to get a size 8 but I bought these size 6 short and they fit me perfectly. Short. Weird sizing. I’m 5’8” and 6 months pregnant. I got the black wash. I have other size 4/6 maternity jeans from old navy that fit great but they’re skinny style. These 4/6 (small) size are really short and tight up the crack. They’re also tight right along where my baby sits low. I first ordered medium (size 8/10) but they were really baggy in the butt and would slip down with only my phone in the back pocket. They also had a discolored spot. Best maternity leggings for tall girls I’m 5’9” currently 39 weeks and about 225 lb. Pictured is around 37/38 weeks. I ordered these around 30 weeks and I wish I would have ordered these sooner! They are actually long enough and feel so soft! I do wish I would have ordered the ones with the pockets but I didn’t realize they made them with pockets. Some loose threads on pocket | Great Fit | Very Well Dyed These fit really well! There was little to no gapping in the back of the waist. They were also a perfect length. I am 5'7" tall, but struggle with finding pants that are long enough, and these did splendidly. They are very comfortable too with good streatch and the zipper and button were easy - no sticking or difficulty. They are overdyed and very black - they look fantastic, but take care when washing these! I always wash my blacks and grays together and didn't have an issue, but some of my black jeans look a little more black hahaha. I washed on cold with detergent and white vinegar and did not experience any shrinking or issues with seams. Overall, I am very impressed with these. Despite the small quality issue with the pockets, which was easily mended, these are an excellent pair of pants. Also, one last thing to note, these are straight leg - NOT skinny jeans, so they are open at the bottom just like the picture shows. Just a heads up if you are thinking these are skinny jeans.

See Pics and Review for XL Measurements I took measurements prior to wearing. On an XL the waist is 34” unstretched, rise is roughly 13”; cuff is 12” across flat and inseam is 30”. I ordered these in a 31” inseam and they’re only 30”. This length works for me with flats but not if I want to wear anything with a heel. I’m deducting one star for the missing one inch in length. I’m fairly long waisted and these still come up higher than my other jeans but that’s not a bad thing and actually goes a long way toward preventing the dreaded muffin top in my case. They fit fairly snugly through the waist and hips but because they’re so stretchy they don’t pinch or bind anywhere. Dark color wash is slimming. They feel like I’m wearing yoga pants as opposed to wearing jeans which means they’re pretty comfortable. I’d order them again but I really need a 31-32” inseam. Otherwise no complaints.

Gonna order more! All I can say is....where have these pants been all of my life?! They are so lightweight and comfortable that I feel like I'm not even wearing pants. These will be perfect for Florida weather, possibly even in the summer. I'm 5'9, pants size 14, and ordered the one size extra long, and they are plenty long enough. I'm definitely going to get more colors, but I will try the regular length inseam option because there are more colors/patterns to choose from. Update: I ordered a one size regular length. They are long enough, but just barely. The elastic just comes to my ankles and I have to wear them at my belly button, the the long version I could wear the rise up higher, which I really wanted to fold the waist over a bit with the long size anyway. With the long version I end up with a more puffy look at the ankles and she shorter version is not puffy. I think the long version could accommodate someone up to 6' tall but they may get the less puffy look at the ankles. Overall, with my height, I like both. I also like the more fitted look I get with this brand compared to other brands I have tried that are very big and oversized in the width of the legs. I do wish there were more color/pattern options (especially in long size!) because that's one thing that other brands have available that this brand does not. I did find that with other brands that the inseam listed in the product description was longer than the actual pants, sometimes the pants were shorter by 3". Both types I've tried of this brand have accurate overall length and inseam. I do recommend this brand!

Sparkly fun tights but might be a bit tight I am 6' and 230lbs. The XL-4XL Large Plus Tall just barely fits. I had trouble pulling it over my thighs and was sure I was going to rip it or all of the rhinestones would pop off. Neither of those things happened, and I am pleased b/c that means they are well made. The top of the thighs are pretty tight, but once I put on shorts I did not notice it, although I think I would be aware of it while wearing a skirt. Only a couple of the smaller rhinestones popped of throughout the day when I was adjusting, but I can't tell where they came from due to the randomness of the stones (which is a good thing). They are sparkly and fun and made my outfit stand out. My only issue was the 'ankle' where the rhinestones suddenly stopped. I wish that it had been gradual as it made it hard to make a shoe choice because the line of demarcation went well above my ankle and looked silly. I would probably only wear these with higher ankle booties as my Mid Jordan's did not cover it.

100% Awesome Cowboy Boots! This review is for the 'POYOIOR Cowboy Boots for Women Wide Calf Round Toe Chunky Stacked Heel Knee High Cowgirl Boot Heart Pattern Classic Embroidered Tall Western Booties Pull on.' I ordered these in Red, Size 10.I normally wear a 9 in all shoes, boots; however, I ordered these in the 10 so I would have room for socks and/or gel inserts. I am happy to say, it was a good decision; the boots fit exactly the way I hoped they would - indicating to me that they are true to size! The width of the ankle and thigh is perfect, not too skinny or too wide, and they are very easy to put on and take off. The Faux Leather is good quality bc they look like real leather (at least the red ones do), the color is a beautiful bright red, the stitching is even and pretty, and the heels and soles are sturdy and appear to be attached well. So far, they have been comfortable wearing around the house, breaking them in. I can't yet say how they will feel after wearing for an extended amount of time, but I'm not worried because I have room for gel inserts - LOL!I do have 3 'cons,' but they are personal preferences and in no way warrant a decrease in stars: 1) I'm not crazy about the hearts being white, I think it makes them look like Valentine's Day boots - so I'm going to paint or bedazzle the hearts; 2) I wish the heels were 3/4 to 1 inch higher - it just helps make your foot look a bit smaller; and 3) they are a bit high on me, I am 5'5" tall and my heel to inside knee bend measures 17" - the side seam of the boots measures 18" so on me, my knee is right in the 'V' of the boot which makes my legs look a little short. None of these are a game changer for me but I wanted to be thorough in my review.Overall, I recommend giving these boots a try; for the price, they are awesome boots!

Expect some shrinkage in length I have purchased several pairs of these jeans in different colors and they all fit great with plenty of stretch. Be aware that these jeans do vary slightly from one color to another. I noticed the darker colors seem to run slightly larger than the lighter ones. I am short (5’-2”) so I bought the short inseam (actually 30” not 29” as noted in description), as recommended by the size chart. Upon trying them on, the leg length fell perfectly at my heal. After a few washes the length is now above my shoes and looks awkward. I then purchased the medium inseam (actually 32” not 31” as noted in description) which was a few inches too long. I had to fold them so they wouldn’t drag on the floor or bunch up at my shoes. After a few washes, sure enough, they shrank and the length is now perfect. I would recommend purchasing the longer inseam if you are in between the heights noted on the size chart. Also, if you are like me and have bigger thighs, the material will pill quickly in the crotch area as well. I snapped a photo after a few months of wear, for reference. Overall, I am satisfied with the jeans and would purchase again.

Cute decent fall boots! Exactly what I was looking for. Cheap knockoff without a dramatic heel that will last likely 2 seasons. The chunky heel is great because it adds height without the stark footbed angle that causes pain like other high-heeled boots. I ordered the burgundy and the brown and decided to keep both because they both gave such different vibes. I am 5’2” and they are still true calf boots, not over my knee thank goodness. The bed feels like memory foam, very comfy! And the toes don’t squish, they are rounded enough to mostly fit them all. The colors are pretty but I imagine will be easily scuffed. Though I can’t confirm that yet, most boots of this price range and quality are not meant to be long lasting. I usually wear my boots with some small scuffs, and get up to 3 years before they are just embarrassing or peeling. As for sizing, I am about an 8 (sometimes 7.5 but I lean towards more room), and they fit true to size for size 8 and not squished at all, especially with my wool blend socks I use in the colder months. If you wear thin socks only and are between sizes you might be able to go with the smaller size. So far, I would recommend. I’ll try to update after some constant wear in the fall/winter.

Denim and Stitching Color & Pocket Size As much as I like the style and fit of these - Sidefeel Women's Wide Leg Jeans High Waisted Stretchy Big Pockets Capri Denim – jeans, I am disappointed with this purchase. The color of the jeans that I received differs from what is shown in the photographs in the listing, as is the stitching. The thread in all the photos looks to be white. The pants I received, the thread is orange. I had ordered the “Bluing” color of denim pants which, in the photos appears to be more navy blue than what I received which looks closer to the “Ashleigh” color. You either like them or you don’t These cropped high waisted pants are straight-lined. They don’t have any stretch but are comfortable…if they fit comfortably around your middle. The only drawback to these pants is that if you’re a leaner build, the pants will sit straight down your frame, but if you have a full figure, you’re going to look fantastic. Recommendation: I’m a size large, tall at 5’8” and 160 lbs. personally I didn’t like how they look on me but others have told me that they were cute-looking. Highly recommend! I was VERY skeptical of these jeans due to the cost, let's face it, Levi's for under $20 is VERY rare! I ordered the "Totally Shaping Bootcut Jeans" in a size 6 short (I am 5'3, USA) and while they are a bit loose at the waist, they are super comfortable! PROS: VERY comfortable. My iphone 15 fits about 3/4 of the way in the back pocket and when I bend forward or sit down, my phone does ride up but it does not fall out of the pocket which is a BIG plus! I washed in cold water and color did not bleed. I also put in the dryer and while I am not sure if it shrunk or not, it definitely fit better, but again, still a bit loose at the waist. It is a "bootcut" and as you can see on the pictures, they have a fabulous flair which I believe works great with my figure. CONS: While I listed it as a "pros" above, I do believe it shrunk a little bit in the dryer which I loved as it fit better, but if it fits tight when you initially try on, make sure you air dry as it will shrink a bit. Overall, these jeans are a complete steal and highly recommend, they are true levi jeans so you get great quality material, great fit and super comfortable jeans!

Great when NEW jeans are shipped! I will say this has been a process. The first pair of jeans came with NO tags and had obviously been worn and washed. They had what appeared to be paint stains and were very worn looking. The material had obviously been washed in either very warm or hot water. They fit very well or I probably would have just returned them for credit and bought Levi’s 315. I was looking for an alternative to spending upwards of $65.00 on a pair of Levi’s. I ask for a replacement and I noted the pair I had received were used. While I support recycling, if you purchase a pair of NEW jeans, they should be that, new with tags. These jeans should be labeled as returned in good condition with stains with a price reflecting that. It took about 2 weeks for the replacement pair to arrive, but they are NEW, unwashed jeans with tags. They fit very well. In comparison to the Original Levi’s 315, the material is thinner. The fit is very similar, but not identical. The 315 size 2 short are snugger through the hips and thighs and the flare on the bootcut is not quite as much. The rise is about a 1/2 inch lower on the original 315. In comparison with price being the main selling point, the Signature series is a good buy. I don’t know if they will hold up to the Original Levi’s quality. I only wash my jeans every 3-5 wears. I turn them inside out and wash with other jeans on cold water, gentle cycle. The front pockets are adequate and the same as Levi’s original. The back pockets have the Signature stitching and are similar to the original in size and shape. These are a lightweight denim and I don’t see them as being warm, probably better for a milder climate, or Fall weather. I have attached pictures for reference. The tags are on the new pair. For reference, I am 5’4” have long legs and a short torso and weigh 112. I wear a size 2 in Original Levi’s so I say these run very true to size. The inseam was right at 29 1/2 inches. Due to these being bootcut jeans, if you shorten them you would loose the “bootcut”. If you need a true short length of 28”, I don’t see these working.

I'm 5'7 and they are the perfect length for me. I'm so excited to wear these over the holiday season.

Stylish, Comfy, Perfect fit! For reference I am 5'6" 243lbs and I ordered the recommended size XL. I am super super happy with the fit and comfort of these baggy jeans. This is my first pair and they give me the exact vibe I was going for while feeling comfortable and stylish. I could live in these they are so comfortable. They fit very well but don't feel tight. The leg/thigh fit is amazing. I have thick thighs and somehow these give fitted but loose looking vibes I can't explain it. Like im Wearing baggy jean but they don't feel like im wearing baggy jeans. I can bend and move flawlessly in these and no backside spillage if ya know what I mean. The length is perfect I paired a simple pair of slip on sneakers and the length was on point. I can literally run Ari and in these all day or night and feel comfortable. They move with my body and do not cut into the waist area. And trust me I have a thicker waistline. The price was well worth it and a great value. There are so many jeans to choose from and I'm so happy I found these and this brand. The pockets are of good size the button and zipper closure work well. I just don't know what else to say about these. I'm super happy with these.

Nice jeans. More of a bell bottom than flare or bootcut More of a bell bottom than a flare or bootcut. However, I love these jeans. They are light weight and not too think which makes for a good summer jean. They fit well and hug the butt nicely. I’m 5’5” and 185ish, I purchased a 12 short.

Good material, flexible fit. Surprisingly Flattering and High-End Quality! I couldn’t believe how great these jeans are! I’ve always been loyal to high-end denim brands, and these totally exceeded my expectations. Right now I’m in between sizes—I’ve gained some weight but am already on my way back down. I ordered both the size 14 and the 12 in short length. I normally wear a 14 (I’m 5’2” and 180 lbs), and the 14s fit comfortably, but I was shocked to find that the 12s fit too! They were snug, but the material is stretchy, soft, and extremely forgiving—surprisingly comfortable even in the smaller size. Both sizes looked amazing in the mirror. The short length hits just right for me when I’m wearing 3–4 inch heels, which is my go-to look. In these images I’m wearing heels, about 3.5 inches. The jeans graze the floor in a flattering way and have that perfect bootcut silhouette that works beautifully over high-heeled boots. If you look at the product photos, you’ll see they fit like a dream and give that sleek, elongated leg effect. One thing to note: the short length runs a little longer than expected, so I wouldn’t wear them with sandals unless I hem them slightly—but with heels? Perfection. I highly recommend these jeans, especially if you’re curvy and love a high-end look at a great price. I’ll probably keep the size 12 as my motivation jeans, but honestly, both sizes are a win. 👖✨

My favorite jeans! 😍 These are hands down my favorite jeans. They fit great and are super stretchy without losing shape. I have two pairs- a short length for chucks and sandals and a regular length for heels and boots. I’m 5’2, 160ish pounds, wearing a size 6 short and 6 regular. The seams look white in the side pic but it doesn’t look that way in person.

What's up with the cost difference? I purchased 3 *different* pairs of these jeans. One pair of "Immaculate, One pair of "Janice" and one pair of "Mascara." All in the same size, 16L. All described to be the same cut, "Gold Label" etc. etc.. All you have to do is chose the 'color.' But the price varies wildly. So I assumed something other than the color of the jeans was the basis for this reasoning. Wrong. Apparently the price is soulely based on popularity. Seriously? Please read on, because I do love these jeans & I did get a more than satisfactory response from Amazon. I'm 5'9" and thick in the middle and prefer a little extra length to my jeans. I probably could have gone a size smaller, but I wanted to allow for at least ten percent possible shrinkage due to washing. And I don't want to look ridiculous. The jeans I ordered have slightly different shades of dark blue with gently faded patches. But there really is no other way to tell which pair is which. Remove *all* the tags, and you have 3 pairs of the same jeans on in different shades. When I showed my husband, he didn't notice much of a difference between the pairs either. Just that the 3rd was slightly darker. However all three pairs cost different prices. The most expensive pair being titled "Immaculate." Once the jeans came in the mail and I had tried them all on, surprisingly *they all fit exactly the same.* I have no problem admitting that I'm very happy with all three pairs. But laid out side by side, all 3 pairs of the jeans look *very* similar. Was I sent the same model *twice*, or what? Why is one pair so expensive? No where on the jeans' tags do I see any difference between them other than their scan numbers. They have no 'names' or clear numbers, like my beloved "501's." If I had gone to the mall and picked out the same jeans myself, they would have all been from the same shelf at the same price. They are all the same length, cut, rise, style, and fit exactly the same. The inside and outside labels, text/print and logos are nearly the same as well. And only one pair states "Gold Label" on the outer back leather tag. It's like the one pair of jeans came out a year earlier under a different name - but is otherwise the same in every other way to the other pairs. So I don't get why one pair cost me twenty one dollars, the next pair costing me twenty four dollars, and the last cost a whopping thirty eight dollars? I'm seriously considering returning the most expensive pair/s (which ones though?) and reordering the cheapest pair/s again. Like I said, I *really* like these jeans. They all *fit* the same and fit well and are flattering. But I feel like someone is trying to pull the wool over my eyes here, and I'm really annoyed by the difference in cost because that is the only thing that really differentiates one pair from another, aside from the color. Because if it's color/dye that costs more, that's absurd, and one or more pairs has to go back. In the mean time this whole thing has kept me from actually wearing any of the jeans. So. Which pair or pairs of jeans do I return?!

Good Leg Shape / Heavy & Uncomfortable / Waist Gap Re: Medium Blue Vintage & Black (14 Regular, Low Stretch) Review keeps not posting -- guessing too long? -- trimming and retrying: After reading Jennifer Romolini's glowing reviews, I had high hopes for these jeans. Unfortunately, both pairs fell short of expectations. As a fan of vintage & contemporary rigid denim, and a sometime wearer of semi-stretch denim, I found the fabrication of these jeans perplexing. They're somehow stiff, *heavy*, and uncomfortable, while also being slightly stretchy, and exceedingly quick to bag out -- even during a brief try-on. The straight leg shape is good. However, on both pairs, the waist is unusually high, especially in back. (I'm 5'11" with a long torso, so to have a waistband hit so high is unexpected.) The blue pair was so unflattering overall -- creating a bizarrely matronly figure / butt shape (even worse than the one which exists) -- that I immediately removed them without taking a photo. They were HORRID!! The black jeans looked decent from the front, if bulky; and because they're black, weren't so awful from the rear view (though they felt stiff and immobile, while somehow being quick to sag). Because the legs had the straight, full-length silhouette I'm seeking, I considered keeping the black pair -- but in addition to their stiff uncomfortableness, the waist gapped *too much* in back -- and with the odd fabrication and low price, aren't worth the trouble of having altered. Everyone's taste and body type differ, and perhaps these jeans will work well for others -- but please see attached photo for the waist-gap situation on me. (My specs: 5'11", approx. 186 pounds, long torso / shorter legs, hourglass figure.)

I usually hate jeans. Love these! SIZE 29. 5’6ft, waist is 30-33in bloated, hips 40-42in. These Jeans fit great, not too tight & not too loose, the back doesn’t buckle out. I could wear sneakers or a heels since the length is perfect. The waist length perfect, pants sit right below my button. I did have to exchange a size 30 for 29. They do run a lil big. Very happy with my purchase, I will be ordering more pair. ✨

Reliable Levi's, though the Amazon size listing is confusing I’m 5’3”, 118 lbs and ordered 28 reg based on another pair of Skinny Levi’s I have. Based on the confusing chart for ordering, I wasn’t sure what I would end up with. I got a 28/32 that also says 6 Medium ( guess that translates to the “reg”). I would say the fit in this Vintage pair is comfortable, if not a bit roomy, but at least not tight or restrictive. Length is perfect. I ordered color StayPut, and the actual version is not as faded looking as the pic online, but I like what I got just as much if not better. I really like the flare (ready for a change from the skinny). I also ended up with the actual “vintage” version, which has really cute 3 row stitching on back and front pocket. I noticed at least one review pic had the traditional 2 row Levi stitching, so odd that you don’t know what you’re getting from this particular listing. Anyway, I am very satisfied with my purchase, with a very fair price for new Levi’s.

Flattering & Stretchy Wow, these jeans are IT!! I’m 5’4” & weigh 160lbs. I ordered the 31x32. The length is perfect, not too short yet not too long. There is room in the waist so I could’ve done a 30 waist, but I don’t like tight jeans. They are stretchy denim, not stiff. I’m not a fan of stiff denim, so these are perfect. Love the dark color of the jeans,. They can go with anything. They are durable and you can dress them up or down. They are so flattering on the peach 🍑. I think they run a little big but not so much that you need to size down.

Comfy and Stretchy For reference, I am 5'5", 165 lbs. I am more bottom heavy than top. When I ordered, there were 3 choices of inseams, and I chose the middle one. I almost think I could have gone for petite. I also got the XL based on measurements and weight, and they are a bit too loose for the stretchiness to matter much.These are some very comfortable jeans/leggings. Very stretchy. I could buy groceries, do yoga and cook supper and work in the yard, all in 1 day in these. Easy to get on the floor and play with kids/ fur babies. They are extremely easy to move around in. extremely functional. Stays up, so doesn't stretch off of you when you squat or put things in the pockets. I did not exactly like the fit on me tho. They seemed a little frumpy looking to be a stretchy material. (again, while ordering based on what should be my size.) I am not thrilled of how I look in them. I realize that is my figure, but I believe a closer fit, would have been a bit better. The pics are after washing and putting in dryer. I do not think the fit changed any from the fit before washing. So should be dryer safe, but may lessen the lifespan. I do think they are a good value based on the functionality, comfort, and pockets - even the looseness if that is what you are looking for. So worth the price, especially if there is a coupon. I just do not feel they are flattering

Runs a bit small. 5’6” 200# and an apple. I carry most of my weight in my belly. OK, so now that we got that out of the way, onto the pants. I really like these. I bought them in a size 16 medium because I find that Lee pants always run a little small and since they’re for work. I really did not want them to be snug. I got the black and white thin plaid pattern and I really like it. The rise on these is good. It’s a fairly high-rise, but it’s not super high-rise. The waistband is a bit big on me but I’d rather that then have it dig in. The length is perfect for me, but if you’re any shorter than 5’6” you may want to order the shorter size unless you want your pants really long for heels or something. The back pockets are sewn shut, but you can open them up, and the front pockets are pretty deep. Material has a good thickness and has a bit of stretch to it. Everything seems to be pretty good quality and I love the price at $18. These will be great work pants, they might be a little heavy for the summer, but we’ll have to wait and see. Between the fit the fabric and the price. I definitely recommend these.

Long enough! Yay Insanely comfortable and flattering. I'm over 60 and love the trouser look but want the informal look of jeans without going "boyfriend" and sloppy for a woman my age. These are spot on. I'm 5.7 at 115 with 36.5" hips 27" waist and the small is a comfy fit for me but I could've easily gone Xsml if I was looking for snugger fit. These have a nice detail surprise at the waistband. They're not just a wider band. They're sewn well and kind of like a two tiered double waistband which looks very nice and finished well. At my age having a squared up looking rear view is something to keep an eye out for and these don't do that. Very flattering from the rear view and as close to "heart" shaped as I'm going to get at this age and I'm very very pleased. Lots of stretch but they don't look like stretch jeans. Love these so much I got a pair in white. Usually for a trouser look I either had my pricey linen trousers w/ zip, elastic waist or draw string waist. These can be dressed up or down

Comfortable and flattering! These jeans are just what I was looking for- they’re roomy in the top half of the pants, and bell at the bottom giving a nice shape with comfort. With certain jeans, you get one or the other… a wide leg looking like a boyfriend jean, or a tight leg with a belled bottom. Feels like I hit the jackpot finding a jean with both. With that being said, the color of the jeans themselves are traditional blue jean color with a lighter wash on the legs. The area above the pockets has the already washed and worn look, but it doesn’t look natural. I find this in a lot of jeans though, and some may like it, I’m hoping it fades over time with washings to look more natural. They’re stretchy, not only in the legs, but also the waist, which is nice! I like a little forgiveness in my jeans… for eating out, and having room for the dessert, busting out the millennial dance moves, and simply being able to sit and breathe in them without wanting to unbutton one or all of the buttons depending on the day. No discomfort sitting in these! lol Really hoping these hold up, the material and stitching seems a reasonable thickness for a stretch jean, and the stitching is intact and straight. I am slightly concerned about the buttons, as I’ve had ones like these before (slightly wiggly, if ya know what I mean?) and the top usually ends up popping off. In the event they do, I wouldn’t have a second thought to replacing them with a higher quality button. For the price- this is a great jean. I can already tell they’re going in my go-to, tried and true rotation of pants. (I am 5 foot 1- 145lbs with an athletic, chunky build if that helps. Also wearing flat shoes.)

Nice fit but don’t look like advertised The jeans I received look nothing like what I ordered. The waist is completely different and the color is much lighter than the “blue” I ordered. Disappointed because the waist was the selling point for me- I liked the buttons. It was different than regular jeans. All that being said, I’m still giving this four stars because I did really like the fit. I’m 5’1 so to find jeans that actually fit me length wise is really difficult and these are the perfect length for me in boots. And the fabric is very stretchy and very comfortable.

Pretty nice jeans I felt compelled to write a review because there were none in my size range. I usually wear a 6R at 5'7" 140ish lbs, which is what I ordered. Something I noticed before ordering was that the measurements on the size chart for sizes 6 and 8 were the same. What? So how do I know what size to get? I usually get a 6, but knowing this brand is usually for younger people it felt like a risk. As you can see from the photos, it's a really snug 6. I did try these on at the end of the day when (for me) pants are tightest because of gravity 😂 I think that after some wear and washes, they will get softer. The material is a bit rougher than I usually like, with less give, but you get what you pay for. They will be good for cool weather. I also like the wash, I don't like huge lines on my hips and these aren't too in-your-face. (I would have opted for a darker wash but it wasn't available in my size.) So overall pretty good, I'll throw them in the rotation and pair them with a thrift store flannel to keep the dream of the 90s alive 😎

Might need more colors! Love these! I usually wear an 8L but ordered a 10L after reading reviews that they feel small. I think it’s because it’s not the stretchy denim most jeans are made out of lately. I love the structured waist that doesn’t roll at the button. I have a 34” inseam and these are perfect.

Fantastic! Comfortable real demin looking jeggings! Heathyoga are MY GO TO yoga pants. They are the best fitting most comfortable yoga pants I've ever found and I own an entire collection. They are tight in the legs down to the ankles for the full length ones and offer great tummy control and good compression without feeling restrictive or uncomfortable. They are so soft against my skin and hold up well wash after wash by washing in cold water and hanging to dry. The dark colors don't fade. The pockets are perfectly sized and placed. I have a horrible time finding jeans that fit. I have extremely thin legs and not much of a butt. I like slim cut tight jeans but the legs and calves are always too big and the hip and butt area always sag. I don't have much in the way of curves. My measurements are 41.33. 44. When I came across these jeggings I was super excited! I read the reviews and immediately order a pair in my regular size large for this brand. I'm absolutely thrilled with them! The length is perfect for me at 5'6". They fit to my super skinny legs and are nice and snug on my hips and thighs while still feeling just as comfortable as my yoga pants. They do look like actual denim which is another big plus. My only negative comment is the sizing seems to run a tad bit larger than their tummy control yoga pants so there's not much tummy control in these. I would suggest ordering down one size from your regular heathyoga pants size if you want a snugger fit. I will definitely be ordering these in multiple colors and lengths, the cropped look quite cute too, but I will be ordering a medium for more compression and tummy control. I will still wear the large pair I bought as they do fit great but will probably save wearing them for days I feel bloated and don't want anything super tight on my tummy. I highly recommend giving these a shot. I'm so glad I did.

Petite Curvy Girl Approved! These are fantastic!! The material is super stretchy, moderately soft and lightweight, but substantial enough to smooth the lines a bit. My measurements: waist 26", hip 37", 116 lbs, 5'4" tall. Size Small, 31" length fits perfectly. Most jeans do not work for me because of that 11" waist to hip difference, but these are stretchy enough to accommodate my little bubble butt, haha. I do still have a tiny amount of gaping at the back waist with these, but it's nothing major. The pockets are generous and the stitching looks pretty good. I love that they don't have those cheap rivets that come apart quickly. Best of all they are suuuuper comfy! Works well for me!

I'm 5'2" 110lbs. These jeans are very soft and the perfect length. I like how they're tight in the waist so I don't have the awkward gap from having a booty. While the pants look smooth in a still photograph, the front pockets bunch and gather when I walk giving me an unflattering front. Perhaps the pockets can be cut out and sewn up, but if I'm paying this much for pants, I don't want to make the effort of altering them.

The 1st pair was great, others are not consistent I ordered 1 pair of these to start and thought they were great! They fit true to size and felt very comfortable and stretchy (almost like a jeggings material/feel)! Although I don't normally wear jeans in the "long" length, I ordered it in these since the regular length was listed as an inch or so shorter than most regular length jeans. I am 5'5". The long length on the first pair seemed great. They were a little long, but I would rather that and make a small rolled cuff at the bottom than too short. Because I liked the first pair so much that I just got, I ordered 2 more. None of them are consistent. They are all made of different blends of material and have a different texture. The sizing and styles are not consistent. Each pair is a different length. The design on the back pockets of each is different. It does show this in the product photos, but I didn't expect the blends/textures and lengths to also be different. While each pair does fit (some longer than others), the fits do vary. The front pockets are also different. Two of the pairs have what I would consider "normal" front pockets for women's jeans. The 3rd pair has pockets less than 2 inches deep that they're basically pointless! I love the 1st pair I got but will likely send the other 2 back. Just don't expect them to be consistent.

Soft & Stretchy Jeans These jeans are super soft, comfortable and flattering! They have a lot of stretch all over, making these jeans ideal for any occasion. They hug my curves, accentuate my butt, prevent love handles, and have a slim fit all throughout. The waist sits right below/at my belly button, which is good because I prefer a mid-rise (super high rise jeans on someone my height/with a short torso isn't great). Because the waist is extra stretchy, I can tuck a shirt in without it feeling tight. I can be bloated as hell and still feel comfortable! The one main negative is the crotch area. When I sit down, the fabric gathers, hardens and points outward in an awkward way (see photo). I absolutely despise the way it looks, but these jeans are so awesome in every other way that I can overlook it. The length is just about perfect on me, which is rare! The shorter inseam is ideal for those 5'-5'3". Because these jeans can expand so much, I can most likely afford to size down. I do have to keep pulling the back up after sitting/lying down. There's plenty of extra room in the waist area so maybe I should've sized down. I usually wear a size 2 Petite in pants and these feel roomier. If you're not sure between two sizes, I would size down, unless you want a roomy waist. Overall, for under $35, these are really nice jeans, and I would get them in other colors/styles. The fabric is lightweight so they're not ideal for wearing in really cold weather. But they work for the hot weather here. They look good, they feel good, and they don't break the bank. What more could you ask for?

Soft & Stretchy Jeans These jeans are super soft, comfortable and flattering! They have a lot of stretch all over, making these jeans ideal for any occasion. They hug my curves, accentuate my butt, prevent love handles, and have a slim fit all throughout. The waist sits right below/at my belly button, which is good because I prefer a mid-rise (super high rise jeans on someone my height/with a short torso isn't great). Because the waist is extra stretchy, I can tuck a shirt in without it feeling tight. I can be bloated as hell and still feel comfortable! The one main negative is the crotch area. When I sit down, the fabric gathers, hardens and points outward in an awkward way (see photo). I absolutely despise the way it looks, but these jeans are so awesome in every other way that I can overlook it. The length is just about perfect on me, which is rare! Difference between pure and non-pure versions I ordered these jeans in a size 9 in both Amal and Amal Pure colors. I already ordered the jeans in a size 9 in the color Lake and loved how soft the material is. I wanted a lighter color and chose the two Amal colors to compare which one I like better. Both of them have too much fading for my liking so I returned both. Also, the stitching is a bit off and is more noticeable on these jeans than on the Lake pair. The Amal color has the wallflower logo on the back and stitched within while the Amal Pure does not. The Amal Pure has a plain black tag within and less decorative back pockets. Also, the Amal Pure has tiny front pockets while the Amal has more normal sized front pockets that you can actually fit your hands deeper inside. The Amal color jeans are slightly lighter than the Amal Pure. In my pictures with them side by side, Amal is on the left and Amal Pure is on the right. In terms of fit, I am 5'4", 140ish lbs, 38" hips, 29" waist. I usually wear a size 7 in juniors and a 4 or 6 in women's and the size 7 in these jeans was a tad too tight so I kept the 9 in Lake. I love how soft these are but they do slide down when walking around extensively or when getting up from sitting. They are more like leggings than jeans. UPDATE: I wrote my original review in Fall 2018. It is now July 2020 and Wallflower instasoft jeans are the only jeans I wear since they are soft and stretchy and don't bother my sensitive skin. I still have my original Lake and they are a little less dark but still in great shape. I also have and wear regularly Shannon and Hayden all in a size 9. The Lake seems a little tighter than the other two but all are softer than normal jeans. I always wear these with a belt since they are super stretchy. But overall, they have held up to constant wear and look great which is surprising since the material is thin and my thighs rub.

For my plus size girls 💖 So this is for my ladies who are 220lbs-225lbs with an apron belly! I didn’t find many reviews with my body type on here so I decided to bite the bullet and try them myself! First of all OMG these are super comfy, long enough, and stretchy. They’re not SUPER stretchy but they’re breathable and I can sit in them and not suffocate. THEY HAVE DEEP USABLE POCKETS ON THE FRONT AND BACK. The tummy control? Oh yes girl.. it’s there and I’m here for it. The flare of these beauties are AMAZING. Best jeans ever for my body type Legit my favorite jeans all around. I’ve brought just about every color of 726 flares high rise and it fits like a glove. I get compliments all the time on these jeans. I work in a warehouse, so I do a tremendous amount of moving about, lifting, squatting, twisting etc. these jeans are insanely comfortable, they don’t give you that saggy look even after a few days of wear. I like my jeans snug and tight. These jeans can go from warehouse work to an elevated and more elegant look with the right pair of heels. I tend to wear cowgirl boots a lot so these are perfect to cover a boot, even a high one. I personally wait for sales and then buy the jeans and posses them in so many colors. The waist is my favorite part cuz it actually hugs my waist instead of leaving a huge gap. Makes the bum look incredible too. I’m 5’5 and fluctuate in weight about 10 lbs so I really like tight jeans, with a small waist and small hips. These jeans make me look like a woman instead of making me look like I swim in them. I always buy size 24 by 32. I only use a belt for fashion rather than need. They can easily be worn without a belt and hold their integrity.

Cute, but returned I've long been wanting to try a pair of jeans that has the front split hem. I know they come in various styles from skinny to boot cut to flare, and I have no idea which style I will like. I was hoping it would be these, but I decided I need a different style. I feel since these are a flare style split hem, the slit opens up too much for my liking. Or maybe it's just that the slit should be shorter to give me the desired look that I'm going for. I actually fell in love with these upon initially trying them on. They are comfortable and I really like the back pockets. I have a flat butt and it's not often I find a pair of pants that are at least somewhat flattering on me, but I felt like these were fairly flattering and I liked that aspect. Unfortunately, I feel like the overall appearance of these jeans just wasn't the most flattering for my body type. These are high-rise, which I don't mind. They come just to the top of my belly button. But I felt like they were a little too short. I thought that having a 32" inseam, the length would be fine, but I would have actually preferred them to be at least an inch or two longer. These are nice for wearing with flat sneakers, I guess, but not with boots. Between the high-rise waist and the slightly too short inseam, they just look kind of funny to me. Also gives me the appearance of having short legs or just overall looking kind of short and squat. I didn't really like that. I'm 5'7", so I'm not really short. I thought I might be able to adjust the appearance by using safety pins to hold the slit together. I thought the safety pins actually looked cute and would hopefully allow me to keep the jeans. But the more I tried them on, the less I liked them. I'm also not a big fan of medium blue jeans. I feel like they look good on a lot of other people, but they just don't go with my wardrobe. I got my normal size 27 and they fit great. They are a true 32" inseam. The waist is 13" and the rise is 10.5". The ankle is about 10" wide and the slit is 9" long. PLEASE NOTE: the pictures I took are with the slit held together with a safety pin unless otherwise noted.

SIZE CHART IS NOT ACCURATE FOR LENGTH I’d give these 5 stars if I was given the right size in length for what I ordered. However, they fit like a glove and really flatter my body type. For reference I’m about 5’1, 136lb, with 29-30 inch waist. Im also a mom and these hide my small pooch beautifully. I am curvy so they are snug but not tight, which is what I wanted. I usually aim for jeans with 28” inseam if applicable, and with these I was given the option to get short and the size chart also mentions that 28 is the short, but upon doing my research, this is not true. On the Levi’s website itself for the 726 Flare, the short sits at 30 inches. I was going to return them for the right length but I would have been sent the same 30” inseam length. I will keep them and another pair that I got in black but will have to get them tailored. Size chart is not accurate in length for these specific 726. If you were to order something that was advertised as “ankle length” then it would be accurate, but for these 726 the short is 30”. Hopefully people see my review to be able to make the right decision about these.

Pleasantly surprised! I have always wanted to try on a pair of Levi’s and I’m so glad that I did! They are true to size for me. I ordered a 29 short, but I knew they would be too long because I’m only 5ft and 30 inches is not short enough for me, so as usual I will be taking 2 1/2 inches off the bottom. I like the flare because they are not too much of a flare, they are like a boot cut flare. They feel stretchy and comfortable which is always a plus! If I can find the same exact pair in a different color, I will purchase them for sure. The price is right! I took one star off only because I wish they would offer x-short like American Eagle does. Petite used to be 5’2 and under and now it’s 5’4. I guess short women are a dying breed lol Anyway for reference I am 5ft 128 lbs., I usually order a size 8 and the 29 fits great! I will add pictures. In the photos I folded the bottom under 21/2 inches.

THE PERFECT Corduroys! These wine color corduroys are exactly what I’ve been looking for! I own multiple pairs of Levi’s skinny style jeans, so I ordered the same size as the rest of my Levi’s, size 31. For the Regular length the size chart lists 30-32 length. I was super excited when I pulled these out of the package and saw that they are 32s. I’m 5’7, 175 lb and size 31W/32L fits perfectly and the length allows me to wear heels with these pants. The fabric is soft and somewhat stretchy, it is medium weight - not thin that feels cheap, but also not overly heavy. I love the style and the look of these pants, great addition to my wardrobe as I will be able to dress them up for the office and dress them down for casual wear. I feel like finding good quality corduroys that fit well is extremely hard (or at least I have went through several purchases and returns) that’s why I feel like the price is justified.

Cons: *If you have a bigger bottom the small back pockets placement enhances that (you probably already knew this but just in case you didn’t. *If you’re under 5’3 these will be way too long for you. I hope my review helps you make the best choice for your needs! (:

For me, the hardest part was determining the right size. The online size chart indicated that I should wear a size 18P, which is larger than anything I own. Nevertheless, I bought a pair and they turned out to be too big. I knew as soon as I tried them on, though, that I wanted one in every color. So, I ordered two more pairs in both a 16P and a 14P. The 16P turned out to be the best fit, even though it is still a little loose; but I definitely didn't want pants that were too tight. These are good every-day pants for a retired woman in her 70's. I am 5'2" and weigh about 160. I had no problems with making multiple returns.

Adorable. Comfortable. Affordable. It's a gamble purchasing clothes online. But with the easy returns at Amazon, I no longer fear being stuck with yet another piece of clothing I don't wear. While these took a long time to get here, when they eventually arrived, they were packaged well for travel from China. These jeans are just as pictured and true to color. I could have gotten away with a small, but I was scared and got the medium, figuring I could use a belt to keep my britches up if these were too big. They look identical to FP, at a fraction of the cost. Soft, comfortable and have a broken in feeling right out of the air tight plastic bag. I've washed them, but just casually tossed them in with my regular stuff and washed on cold. Hung to dry. Came out great. I'm probably too old to be wearing trendy fashions, but I don't care, I loved the bandana pattern. Not so sure about the whole weird barrel leg deal...but these are comfy, and there are a lot of possibilities for styling. Happy with purchase. I usually wear a 4, I got a Medium. I probably could have got the Small and been ok, there is a good deal of stretch to these. I have pretty long legs for my 5'5" frame, and these hit just above my ankle... making them look intentionally cropped, and not accidental floods.

I’m not sure, it’s not giving barrel for me that is!! Didn’t work for me. When I first say this style of jeans i thought they were ugly!! But most recently I saw a few different people on YouTube and social media style them and they looked cute! So I decided to buy these and another pair from somewhere else (waiting for them now). I happened to get them when they had a flash deal so I only spent about $29, perfect because I didn’t want to pay a lot especially if I didn’t liked how they looked. For reference, I’m 5’6”-5’7” & at the moment 216 lbs so maybe it doesn’t work for my measurement type 😒😬😩 There is stretch and they fit a bit fitted. The quality is good and the color is nice.

If you're tall, don't bother. Now I remember why they've just about gone out of business. If you're going by the product specs, don't bother. Listed as five pocket, but the three front pockets are fake/decoys. I'm 5'9" so I ordered a 29 Long. Specifications for regular are a 29 inch inseam, and "super skinny" leg. I like my jeans to fit tight around the ankle, like leggings, and sit below the ankle at the top of my foot. These arrived, the fit is forgivable dispite no front pockets and the rear pockets very small, but the inseam is only 28". On top of that, the ankles are more like a boot cut than skinny, much less super skinny, flapping above the ankles like high waters. I guess, you get what you pay for!

Too short, good fit though I got these in 27 petite. For reference, I’m 5’0” and 107 lb. These pants were supposed to have a 29” inseam, according to the size chart, and they are much shorter than that, as you can see in this photo. The fit is otherwise comfy and fine though, so I’m going to try to exchange for regular inseam.

Sizing is WAY off! I have a 33" waist and need a 31" inseam so I ordered a 32 Regular based on the size chart provided. What I received was marked 14/32 but it had a 37" waist and 26" inseam. I was sent a replacement incase it had just been mislabeled. The replacement was also 37"x26" so I returned them both. I believe that the inseam listed in the size chart is actually the total length from waist to hem not the actual inseam. (The review I originally posted was rejected so this is my rewrite.)

Love them Nice quality medium weight denim with a stretch. I’m 5’3” 130lbs , shaped like a rectangle according to my scale app (flattering, I know 🫤)l got a sz 27 & fits me perfect. Not too tight, a little loose in the leg like a boyfriend cut should be.

Decent fit, a bit big I ordered a 29 (size 8) as I am 5’5 and 152 lbs. they fit well in hips but the waist is a bit loose, I will be wearing a belt so they don’t go down when I squat or bend. I bet I could have sized down but shipping will take 1-2 months (?) so I’ll just keep these. Nice stretch, which would probably also allow for sizing down.

Best Jeans Ever These are the perfect casual pair of jeans! I’m only 5’2 so finding the right ankle length is difficult, but they hit just right. Soft with just the right amount of stretch. The first pair I ordered were too big, definitely run big so size down. The picture doesn’t do them justice.

Love at first try on The jeans are not as light-colored as I had expected, but I put them on and fell in love! They fit just the way I'd hoped - fitted to the knee then flared out. Fits like a damn glove! I usually wear 10, and the 9 fits wonderfully. I'm 5'6" and 150. Order them!

If you only buy one pair of jeans in your life... THESE are them!!!! Most jeans give me muffin top, sag at the butt, slouch down with my active lifestyle. Eventually I have to wear a belt and my jeans look sad and worn after a few washes. Instantly after putting these on, I just knew. It was heaven! They somehow made my lower stomach/hip area feel snug and controlled, yet No muffin top. I practiced squatting around in them, did a few jumping jacks, etc. There was no shift or need for adjustment. I was amazed. Truly, I did not want to take them off. Felt like second skin but durable. I don't know what kind of magic is in these, but keep it coming! If I wash these a few times and they fall apart, I'll revise my review. But after raving to family about these and those who know about Democracy already, I have a hunch they'll stand the test of time. Okay, they're a little pricy, so start off with a pair that are on a good discount. So far, I can say I'd spend the retail price for the luxury of a good, flattering pant. Photo: I'm 5'5" 140 lbs - size 6 regular

Comfortable waistband but too large for me everywhere else. I was so excited to find Democracy Jeans because I typically have trouble finding jeans that fit my waist comfortably. (I’m 5’7 and 140 pounds. I’d describe my body type as slim but with excess weight in the belly area.) I usually wear a 6, but these jeans were so stretchy that the 6 was too large everywhere so I exchanged for a 4. The 4 fits so nicely at the waist and really hides my stomach! Sitting down is comfortable and I don’t have a bright red waistband line across my stomach when I take these off! But the fit in the legs and butt is too large (definitely doesn’t give me the promised lift) and there are little folds of excess material everywhere. Also, the fly won’t stay up to save my life! Every time I stand up, the barn door is WIDE open. Overall, these jeans deliver comfort… and I love that they exist to accommodate more body types! But I don’t think mine is one of them :(

So, so comfortable!! These are probably my favorite pair of jeans I have ever bought. So, so comfortable and flattering. The prices vary wildly depending on the wash. The dark indigo I purchased were $35 and 100% worth it. Other washes go up to $75. I also got them in a grey color. They fit like a glove, and the waist sits perfectly on me, not too high, not too low. I am 5'3" and 135 lbs and I bought a size 4. I can move and bend over and they stretch and move with me....but not so much they get saggy. They continue to hug me throughout the day without bagging or bunching or riding up or down. Pretty sure I will be purchasing these in other washes soon!

Nice fit These were recommended to my by a friend who is short and curvy (10S). I got the 6S and am very pleased. I dont have the biggest butt, but being 5'2" and 135, it is difficult to get butt pockets on my actual butt! These are on there. The pic isnt very good, but I really like the curved cut and smaller pockets. The give on the jean is stronger than most jeggings Ive tried, which makes them feel more formal than stretch pants. has a true button/zipper and all 4 pockets are real. This dark wash is definitely fancier looking and will work very nicely for going out. I could have squeezed into one size smaller and had lobbage/been uncomfortable, so I am glad I got this size (fingers crossed they dont stretch out). I was reticent regarding the price, so hopefully these hold up for a long time.

Comfy, cute, and mom of 2 under 2 approved :) UPDATE! As of 10/21. GO WITH YOUR ORIGINAL SIZE I returned for a size 28. They fit MUCH better. Length is the same. But taking an inch off for better and more comfortable on the hips and waist. Not as big of a gap on the back. I kinda felt like I was forcing myself to be happy with the “baggy look” in the 29. I only got the 29 because that’s what Amazon suggested. However, the 28 and a size 8 is my true size in jeans. So I would say go with your original size! They are still that baggy relaxed fit, but more complimenting in your true size. Squat and sit down tested. Still comfy! (FYI, pics below are from the size 29, not the updated 28). The length was perfect for me. I’m 5’4 and about 127 to 129lbs. I usually wear a size 8 or 28 in jeans but I went with a size 29. I contemplated if I should have sized down to my normal size or not, but based on their chart saying size 28 is a 6/7 in jeans and how they don’t have much stretch, I’m afraid it would feel too tight near the crotch when I sit or squat down to pick up my kids, or that the length would be too short if I sized down. I only marked it one star down because I haven’t tried washing it yet, and maybe the fit will be more perfect fit after I dry it, and it shrinks a little, because I personally would like it to fit slightly more. I think it’s a good value for the quality of the jeans. I don’t really spend more than $50 on jeans anyway, so this was one of my more expensive ones I bought that will hopefully last many years to come. Coming from a new Levi’s jeans buyer this year, I’ve found that these jeans are a bit tricky with sizing because their jeans don’t have much stretch. So depending on your body, where you fill out more (hips, butt, thighs, height) the fit drastically varies. Material feels thick and durable, not much stretch. With that being said, I find the relaxed fit very comfortable as a mom of 2 under 2, and now days I don’t want anything constricting me. I usually opt for a high waist after my 2 c-sections I had for emotional support lol, but these mid rise jeans hung on just right, while covering that little mommy pooch hehe. My booty has shrunk after I gave birth, but I still find the jeans to be flattering in that baggy, “I don’t care” but looks cute look, without it sagging on the crotch. I think this could be dressed up with a cute top, heels, and accessories as well.

New Favorite Jeans! I absolutely love these jeans! The fit is that perfect in-between relaxed without looking baggy, and super flattering through the waist and hips. The denim feels like real quality: sturdy, not too stretchy, and they hold their shape all day. The knee distressing is cute and trendy without being over the top. I’m 5’7” and the length hits just right with sandals or sneakers. They’re comfy enough for running around with the kids but still stylish for going out. I’ve already gotten so many compliments! If you’re looking for that effortless, lived-in denim that still makes you feel put together… these are the ones.

Definitely Worth it! Will be ordering more! Had to come on here because after lots of research and comparing prices these are IT! I am 5’4” 140 lbs athletic curvy build and while there’s a bit of a gap in the back they’re great for having no stretch. I went with size 27 and they’re perfect.
