Perfect for big backs I have a big bum so hard to find jeans that support behinds. 5'7 220lbs wear size xl/16. These fit perfect for a round behind and wide hips.

Dokotoo casual jeans for women. Stretch denim loose and baggy wide leg jeans; raw edge hem. Color black, size 18. Light to medium weight very stretchy denim is very forgiving and super comfortable. Definitely a casual style and not one that I would personally wear “out”. I realize that this is the style and how they’re intended to be - but I still wish they had just a little more structure in the front panel area so they would give some “structure” and an appearance of neatness to the pants and not just sag out with my belly - and thus looking a tad sloppy (in my personal opinion and to my personal taste). Sizing seems to be on par with other brands. These are a size 18 and I usually buy a size 18 in most other brands as well (Carhart, Frye, and others for instance) and these fit as expected. For reference I am 5’8” tall (used to be 5’ 9.5 when younger) and weigh 200 lbs. I have an extremely long leg inseam (tall or 34”+ usually when available) and these pants are still an appropriate fit in length for me. I’m not sure if they’re meant to be worn as a shorter ankle pant (thus meant to be cuffed up perhaps?) or just meant to be whatever length they end up being lol… I’m not a huge fan of the raw hemline - but then again that’s a fashion choice and to each their own. Also, I happen to be 65 years old so I’m probably not the ideal target market for this brand, but I’m trying to branch out and try new things :) The pants achieve what they advertise themselves to be. I deducted one star not for differences in style preference but because of the unflattering nature of the fit on the front belly area. Because of the excessive amount of stretch in this area I feel it can make the jeans look a little sloppy and unattractive (so definitely harder to style around) and inevitably they may stretch out too much in this area requiring that they then need washing/drying after each wearing in order to return them to proper fitting form. So all in all good jeans that are what they claim to be (casual, baggy jeans). Accurate sizing. Nice color choices. Comfortable. Solid 4 Stars. Thanks for reading and I hope this review has been to you. Have a Blessed Day :)

I ordered EMMIOL Women Bootcut Denim Jeans. I ordered them in the Black color, in a Size 6. I am about 5’4” tall, weigh around 125lbs, they fit very very nicely! The Fabric is 70% cotton/28% polyester/2% spandex, and MACHINE-WASHABLE! The stretching ability of these Jeans is awesome! They are considered Mid-High Waisted Jeans, (they are more High-Waisted fitting on me, which I really like). They have awesome flared legs, definitely a Boot-Cut, so they fit over my boots perfectly. They have 2 front pockets and 2 back pockets that are usable, which I truly am glad. So they are not only comfortable but practical also. They have a faded look in the front on both legs and on the back pockets. Kind of gives them a ‘Old-Worn-Look! OVERALL….. AWESOME! And AWESOME AGAIN! Soooo comfy, breathable, stretchable! Quality is GOOD! Price is FAIR!!

Nope. These did not fit anything close to anticipated. Quite hilarious to try on.. I’m 5’4 125lbs with athletic build (more muscle than skinny). Actually fit My stats: almost 70y/o, 5’4”, 112lbs, waist 27”, hips 38”, inseam 29”. I bought size 6 with a 30” inseam. These fit my body type well. The fabric is not very thick like jeans with a higher % of cotton. I’m assuming like all jeans with a synthetic/cotton fabric that they will stretch out as the day progresses. My Go To Jeans, Very Pleased! Read All - I am very happy with how these jean fit. I was not sure what size to order, as my weight has been fluctuating. I usually were a size 12 in most pants, I weight 164 and I am 5'5". When I looked at the size chart and checked with another pair of wranglers I already own and still fit at size10W, I went with the 10W 30L. Length is perfect for my built however the fit is pretty snug. What I mean is it feels like I'm between sizes. The jeans are made with a stretchy material and give enough (At least for me) to get into. Maybe I should have ordered a 12W but these look flattering and if I lose 5-10lbs they would be the perfect fit. Side note-I hold my weigth mostly in my hips and some in my gut. I'm not sure how they wash up or if they shrink in wash. Although they are on the snug side, they are comfortable. I have attached some photo and hope this helps with your decision.

Although I loveD these jeans, unfortunately I had to return them bc of defective pant leg, one was frayed and the other was not. The down side is they didn’t deliver on time as specified. Not for the long legged girlies I really wanted to love these jeans because they hit all the marks for a cute, semi-cropped flared look, but the proportions were a struggle for my height. At 5'10" with long legs, the "cropped" cut felt a bit too short; they ride up significantly on my calves whenever I sit down. Additionally, as someone with a long waist, the high-rise fit only reached my belly button rather than sitting higher up. While the fabric has a really nice stretch and is very comfortable to move in, I did encounter a very strong dye smell right out of the package. Even after a wash, it took a few weeks of "airing out" before the scent fully dissipated. The color held well after the initial wash being a black jean but time will tell if they fade more. Ultimately, these are stylish and well-made, but I wouldn't recommend them to my fellow "tall girlies." If you are a few inches shorter, these would likely be an A+ fit, but for me, they just needed a little extra length to be perfect.

Super Cute Boutique-Quality Styling and Fit - without the High-End Price Tag! These wide-leg black jeans are absolutely on point for current denim trends. The high-waisted fit, front and back pockets, subtle distressing, and relaxed wide-leg cut give them that effortless, cool-girl vibe that works year-round. Trendy Queen continues to be one of those brands I can rely on for consistent sizing and modern styling. The fabric blend is excellent - soft, breathable, and just stretchy enough. The cotton base keeps them comfortable, while the spandex gives enough flexibility to move easily and still hold their shape after wearing them all day (no sagging or bagging, which is huge). The denim is a typical mid-weight - not too heavy, but also not thin like a chambray. They are a solid but SOFT denim! I’m typically a size 2–4 in jeans and ordered a 4, and the fit is spot-on in the waist and hips. The only thing I’m slightly undecided about is the length. On my 5'6" frame, it lands in that in-between zone - not quite cropped, not quite full length. That said, it’s an easy fix and honestly adds styling flexibility: I may hem them to an ankle length or cut a raw edge for a more casual, street-style look. These jeans are incredibly versatile and easy to dress up or down - pair them with sneakers, boots, crop tops, or cozy knits. Comfortable, flattering, and very wearable, they’re a great addition to any denim wardrobe. Another solid win from Trendy Queen!

High‑Waisted Wide‑Leg Jeans — A Hidden Gem for Short Ladies As a short lady who constantly battles the “too long, too baggy” jean struggle, I have to say these jeans were an unexpected win. At 5'2", finding a pair I can wear with flats or tennis shoes without hemming feels almost impossible yet these hit the perfect length right out of the package. I have an average‑length torso but shorter legs, so online shopping is usually a gamble, but these surprised me. They even work with a small heel, which feels like a bonus. The high‑waisted cut has become one of my favorite styles because it’s genuinely forgiving, especially if you’re carrying a little extra around the midsection. These trouser‑style jeans create such a clean, flattering silhouette. The fabric has that ideal balance: structured enough to smooth and slim, but still very much real denim and not flimsy, nor stiff, just right. It hides imperfections without feeling heavy. They come in several colors, but I went with black for the winter season, and it’s been the perfect versatile staple. I’d say order your true size since there’s a bit of stretch; if you prefer a more relaxed, drapey look, sizing up could work too. Honestly, I haven’t stopped wearing them since they arrived. They’ve quickly become one of those rare pieces that make you feel comfortable, confident, and stylish all at once — and that’s worth sharing. I hope the pictures help you decide.

Good quality material, runs a bit small and short For reference, I am 5’5, 119 pounds. In pants/jeans I wear a small or US size 2. I ordered these in a size 4, the smallest size offered. I do buy some of my jeans in size 4 when I want them to be baggy or a looser fit. My first impression when I took these out of the package was there was no way these would fit me. The waist looks so tiny. They do fit me, but I personally think they fit more like a size 2 than a size 4. I wish they were longer too. At 5’5 they sit at an odd length on me. They are not as long on me as they are on the model. I will not be drying these jeans in the dryer because I don’t want them to shrink at all in length. I did measure the waist of the pair of jeans I received and it isn’t quite the 26.6 inches as stated. The jeans are a soft stretchy material made up of cotton, polyester, rayon, and spandex. The material is good quality! They do feel like real stretch jeans which I appreciate, they are not simply pants that look like jeans. The material is nice and thick, not flimsy or thin. The jeans are soft and comfortable to wear. With as tiny as the waist looked to me on first impression, the jeans are stretchy and I can comfortably wear them. As the day goes on the waist stretches out more. The style of the jeans is cute. The front pockets do not offer much in actual usability, they are more decorative than practical. The cuffs at the bottom flare out slightly and have that wide trendy cuff. The back pockets are a decent usable size. The jeans have a button fly and zipper, all which work as they should, no flaws. While baggy is in the title of the jeans, these jeans are not baggy at all. They are straight leg with a bit of flare at the bottom. These sit right at the belly button on me. There are a few seams that are uneven and have thread gathered in places, but that shouldn’t be noticeable from a distance. The price is reasonable as far as jeans go. The quality is good, especially compared to other jeans I’ve ordered from Amazon. Pay close attention to the size guide. I think they run small compared to other jeans I own from name brand clothing stores.

Thank you Wrangler! Wrangler replaced my jeans, they have decided to stand behind their product!!!! Out of the seven pairs of Wrangler jeans I’ve bought, only TWO haven’t split, ripped, or betrayed me like a backstabbing ex. One pair actually blew out on the first wear—ripped clean down the leg like I was attacked by a bobcat (spoiler: I wasn’t). The Sadie jeans? Cute name, but unless you keep your shirt tucked like you’re reporting for duty, those grippy waistband things will pinch the living daylights out of your skin every time you move. Surprise! Instant exfoliation. On the plus side, these jeans are actually long enough for us 35” inseam gals to wear heels. That’s a small miracle. But here in small-town farm country, where I’m actually using my jeans to work—not just pose in a pumpkin patch—I don’t have time to wash ‘em, drive to FedEx, and send them back like I’m returning fine china. Wrangler’s solution? “Just send them in.” My solution? Calculate the fuel cost and just wait for a sale. I must be a sucker, because I keep buying them. Why? Because they fit like a dream. They hug the right spots, make my legs look like a 90s denim ad, and don’t stop mid-ankle like some toddler-length trend. If only the low rise was actually low rise (and not mid-rise in disguise), I’d be throwing stars like it’s a rodeo. So here’s the summary: If you’re into jeans that feel amazing, look fantastic, and then explode at the seams without warning, these are for you. If you enjoy that rush of will my butt be covered today or nah? and like your denim with a side of personal injury from waistband claws, then saddle up, baby. But if you want durability, actual workwear grit, and not just fashion farm cosplay, Wrangler’s got some serious patching up to do. Wrangler, fix your quality. Your fits are gold. Your fabric is chaos. Update 05/22/2025- looks like the right leg stitching is about to let go like the left leg. Just added that last photo

Great fit for curvy, pear-shapes I’m 5’4” and my weight fluctuates between 170–180 lbs. My pant size is usually 12–14 regular, and I’m a true pear shape (average leg length). I’m also in menopause, so my body has changed - a little softer, less firm, and my butt isn’t as full as it used to be. That said, these curvy fit stretch skinny jeans in the 31L (Long) fit perfectly! The material is solid and structured - not flimsy or “jegging-like.” They look like real denim with no sagging in the crotch, and the longer length is just right. What I really love is that they don’t leave a gap in the back at the waist-they hug just right without digging into your stomach. They have enough stretch to allow room for your belly but still fit smoothly and comfortably around the waist and hips. For those of us who are pear-shaped, that’s a big deal! They even give my backside a little shape again. I was hesitant to order after reading mixed reviews, but I’m so glad I did. I even tried them on over another pair of pants and they still fit the same, so I’m not worried about shrinkage. The zipper is solid and the front pockets are deep enough (5 inches). I love them. I got soft black on sale for $20. It was worth it!

Not my favorite, but they're cute. I purchased these in a size 28 Regular and 29 Regular, both in the color (New) We Have Arrived, because I wasn’t sure what size I’d need. I’m in the size 28 in the pictures with the black top, and I’m in the size 29 in the pictures with the pink top. For sizing reference, I'm 5'3", currently around 143 lbs with measurements about 39"-30"-39" (bust-waist-hip), and I'm wearing size 4-6 in jeans or a medium in bottoms in most brands right now. Another random note but I have a shorter torso with longer legs (proportionally), aka I don't wear short length in pants and even mid-rise bottoms hit pretty high on me. I was on the fence about which size I wanted to keep because I could really wear either one. The 28 was a snugger fit without any bagginess and looked good, but it dipped down a little lower in the front, while the 29 still fit well but had some extra room in the waistband, though it had a little extra material in some spots. I ended up holding on to the 29 because it was more comfortable. I feel like Levi’s are always inconsistent in their sizing, but these fit pretty true to size, so I’d say go with whatever size you usually wear. I would recommend getting the short length if you’re my height! I don’t normally get short length, but these are long on me – I have to roll them twice to get them to sit at my ankle. Material wise, these are thin with good stretch, so don’t purchase if you’re looking for a thick, stiff denim. I don’t see these lasting for years with regular wear and washing, so not great durability. They have a zip fly, thank goodness – thanks to Levi’s, I know I hate a button fly, haha. This color is the perfect standard denim shade, too. Overall, I got these at a great price and I’m happy with them because of that. I’m probably going to grab them in another color.

Flattering fit I am pretty happy with these jeans! I’m 5’8” and about 175lbs. I’ve been losing weight and am sort of between a 12/14 in jeans right now, depending on the cut or brand, so I went with a large. I think the higher waist helps hold in a bit of the tummy area, without giving a muffin top. The stretch of the fabric is nice and holds up through a whole day without getting stretched out. I am definitely considering getting another pair in a different color.

Great, slimming boot cut jeans I love how these jeans fit! I’m the one on the left in the picture. I have thin legs a flat bottom so I usually wear a slim fit jeans but these are a nice option to be more stylish. They are slightly fitted in the thigh and narrow toward the knee then start to flare out to the bottom. It’s a spinning design without being too fitted or too flared at the bottom. The jean material is stretchy with flat pockets on the front and back. I am 5’5” 145 lbs. I can wear a size 10 stretchy jeans but it’s not flattering on me (because my derrière is flat) so I usually wear a 12. I ordered these in size large which I think is pretty accurate. They are between a 10 and a 12 fit.

Comfort Meets Retro Style – Great Versatile Bootcut Jeans 5 Stars – Comfortable, Flattering, and True to Size These Luvamia women’s high-waisted boot cut jeans are a great find. I ordered a medium, and at 5’5” and 165 lbs. they fit perfectly — snug enough to be flattering but still very comfortable. The denim is soft with just the right amount of stretch, so you can move freely without losing shape. The high waist and slim-fit cut accentuate the waistline and visually elongate the legs, and the 30-inch inseam gives a nice bootcut silhouette. On me, the length runs a bit long, but nothing a pair of boots or chunky-soled shoes can’t handle. I don’t mind a slight bunch at the ankle, but if you prefer a completely smooth line, you may want to hem them. I also like the four patch pockets and the classic zip and button closure — they blend everyday practicality with a subtle retro style. These jeans are easy to dress up or down and would work well for casual outings, work settings, or even a country concert. Overall, these jeans are a versatile wardrobe staple that run true to standard US sizes. Comfortable, stylish, and flattering — I’m very happy with them.

Comfy fit, nice looking jeans. These bootcut jeans from luvamia are well made of a medium weight fabric that consists of 75% cotton, 23%polyester, and 2% elastane. The material is quite soft and has a good amount of stretch in it for ease of movement. They have 4 patch pockets. The front ones are very roomy and the back ones are average sized. The button and zipper closure both work smoothly. I ordered this pair of jeans in my regular size of XL and they fit me very comfortably. They are high waisted and thanks to the elastane there is no pinching or binding when I bend over or sit down. The are a tiny bit long for my 5' 4" height, but not enough that I will get them shortened. The classic blue color looks really nice and is easy to pair up with all types of shirts. I like them and they are a good quality pair of jeans, so I will definitely recommend them. The fit is true to size. The price (9-26-2025) of $35.99 is a fair value for them. However, there currently is a coupon available to reduce the price to a better value. I would buy these again. The quality and comfortable fit are worth it, in my opinion.

Jeggings-the best of both worlds!! I am in love with these jeggings!! They fit me like a glove-they are so comfortable, no pinching at the waist and plenty of room in the thigh/seat. I’m happy with the mid rise waist-I have an ‘apron’ stomach/belly from my c-section deliveries and the mid rise allows for a more comfortable fit than the restrictive high rise. These jeggings have a good amount of stretch and feel great on and I don’t look like I’m stuffed in them-which is what has kept me away from jeggings for years. I really love the look the color adds-they have that perfect broken in look without looking worn out. I’m 5’3” and ordered short length for the first time-and I couldn’t be happier!! No more wet pant bottoms!! I will definitely be ordering a few more pairs.

Soft and stretchy jeggings. These white denim jeggings fit great. I am 5 ft 6 in, 130 lb. I ordered a six long. They wash great and don't shrink much. Even with my long legs I still had plenty of length left. You can even tuck the bottom hem underneath to shorten the length just a little and that will get rid of any wrinkles. They are very soft and stretchy. Even though they're called jeggings, to me they fit and look like skinny jeans and I like that.

Soft, Stretchy, and the Right Length, but the Waist Fit Misses the Mark These jeans have a lot going for them. The fabric feels soft and has a nice amount of stretch that makes them easy to move around in. The stitching looks clean and solid, and the overall quality feels dependable. At 5 feet 2 inches tall, it can be tough to find jeans that hit the right length, but these sit exactly where they should without bunching at the ankle. The downside is the waist. It is noticeably too loose and slips down throughout the day. I constantly find myself pulling them up. A belt does help, but it causes the waistband to fold and pinch, which ends up being uncomfortable. If the waist were more fitted, these would be an easy favorite because everything else about them works well. With the right body proportions, these could be great. If your waist and hips are close in measurement, you might not run into the same issue. If your shape is curvier through the hips with a smaller waist, the fit may be tricky. Potential shoppers should take a careful look at the sizing chart before deciding.

Great jeans but a little on the snug side I agree with other’s reviews in that these jeans are a little on the snug side on the waist. If you’re a postpartum mom like me with that extra pooch our babies have so graciously blessed us with, you may want to think about sizing up. Otherwise, you may need to pull out the faja for this one! Overall they look great and are comfortable besides the waist being snug so definitely don’t wear these to Thanksgiving or anything! The quality is great for the price. Not much stretch to them though. I would get these again but probably mainly as a motivator to lose my baby fat lol! Also I will say I appreciate that the “short” length is actually short and perfect for me as 5’3” woman. No more scrunched up pant legs or rolling the cuffs, woo! PS- if you’re a double coat dog owner like me keep in mind these are not as forgiving when it comes to lint and hair attraction as most denim tends to be so fair warning. See my photo for proof 😂

These skinny jeans are IMPRESSIVE! I have lost a lot of weight and with the cold seasons coming, I am in need of new, smaller pants. I have had good experiences with other Amazon Essentials clothing so felt confident in buying Amazon Essentials Women's High Stretch High-Rise Skinny Jeans. The first thing I noticed upon getting ready to place my order is that the size chart is incorrect - waist measurements seem significantly incorrect - but hip measurements seem correct. I took a chance based on hip measurements that I would be a size 14 short. These jeans are made of 65% Cotton, 20% Cotton Recycled, 13% Polyester Recycled, 2% Elastane, and I love that they are size inclusive, ranging from size 0 to 20 and short to long lengths. These arrived tonight and they’re super cute! I thought they looked a bit small, but they fit amazingly. I am 170 pounds with an apron belly remaining after significant weight loss and I’ll likely wear them with tunic style tops. They fit my body well. The short inseam is perfect for my height at 5’ 4”. They have some stretch, but it’s not super stretchy. They maintaining a denim jeans material vs jeggings and I wore them for several hours without them losing their shape. They aren’t super skinny - they're a bit looser than I like my skinny jeans and look much tighter in the model pics but I have a bit stomach and small legs so that might be why. Even still, I love these jeans. The seams are stitched well, and they feel high quality with a zipper and top button fly. The pockets are nicely sized. The high rise fits me perfectly above the belly button. The medium blue vintage color is perfect for me! I think they will look adorable with the cuff rolled once and ankle booties! In conclusion, these are another Amazon Essentials home run! Great quality, great fit, stylish and super reasonably priced at $28.50. The only downside is they’re slightly looser than I’d hoped and the size chart for waist measurements seems off. I noticed they just released several styles of women’s jeans and jeggings and I absolutely intend to buy more pairs!!

These jeans fit pretty well true to size but don’t really smooth you out or synch you in. They do have decent stretch and feel comfortable overall. The color isn’t as bright as I expected but I prefer a darker wash anyways so I’m ok with it. The price point was decent. Seem like they’ll hold up well for washes overall. There was no smell or issues on arrival. Allow for decent mobility even with the skinny legs design. The length hits perfect on me at 5’5 size 10. I just wish they had more compression for the lower belly as that’s my area of concern but it protrudes in these so I would only wear them with a longer shirt.

Great Jean but super long if you’re shorter but need a large or X-Large. These are a nice thick pair of Levi’s that are well-made. The fabric is nice and thick. Their zipper and seams are sewn well, they actually remind me of my Lucky jeans. They are fairly comfortable and soft but tighter on my tummy. I got them in a large so they would fit my waist and they are several inches too long. I actually think they’d be too small in my waist because I don’t really have a waist but they have a ton of stretch in the material. They fit my hips and butt, and then the legs are very roomy. So they are a true baggy jean with a whittled waste. They are just extremely long on me. If I stand on my tippy toes, they fit right to the floor, which would be perfect if I wasn’t on my tippy toes. For reference, I am 5’4 and weigh about 175 pounds and am wearing a Large.

Decent jeans but needs a longer shirt These are really heavy jeans, but they are actually quite soft and comfortable. They fit pretty well but are just not super flattering. The length is just barely long on me without shoes, but that's pretty normal for me at 5'3" 160lbs. I'll probably just cut the bottoms for a more casual raw edge. The waistband is tighter, but stretchy enough to still be comfortable. I'm not a big fan of the fit of the butt, but the biggest problem is in the front packets. The pockets don't have enough jeans material, so the lining pops out the front even though I don't think they are too tight. These won't work with a shorter shirt, but they look fine with a shirt long enough to cover the front and back pockets. These are casual jeans, I don't think these would dress up very well, but they'd be good with an oversized top for a comfy fit.

On trend, quality jeans! I love these so much! I was nervous when ordering because I couldn’t find a size chart on this listing anywhere. I am 5’7” - 168 lbs and have somewhat slimmer hips and thighs for my weight but I have a mom pooch at the waist, so depending on whether the jeans are high waisted or not (and how stretchy they are), I tend to wear either a juniors’ 11 or 13, misses 12 or 14, and then either L or XL in letter sizes. Since I wasn’t sure what to expect here I went with the XL as I prefer my jeans to be a little looser than having them be a little too tight. The XL ended up being a fantastic fit! The jeans are maybe medium stretch, I wouldn’t call them “stretch denim” but they have more stretch than some jeans if that makes sense. What really impressed me is the quality of the denim. The asking price of almost $60 is a little higher than I usually pay - I try to stick under $50 for my jeans - so I was skeptical that these would be worth the price but I get it now. This is just really nice denim that looks great and is very comfortable too. I love these jeans and I think the color and style is just like it’s represented in the listing photos. As for sizing, like I said I’m usually a 12/14 or juniors 11/13 and the XL fit great on me, and XL is the most common letter size I order for denim shorts or jeans. So I think they essentially run true to size but maybe a tiny bit smaller as the XL is a comfortably snug fit, so it does fit just a tad smaller than I’d expect an XL pair of jeans to fit me. Basically I’d recommend going with your regular letter size for denim. If you’re between two sizes, pick the larger if you want to make sure they are roomy where you want them to be, pick the smaller if you don’t want that baggy look. I hope this helps!
Perfect Jeans! I'm 5'4", 165lbs, I normally buy Silver Jeans in 30W/31L and these were as expected, perfect. These are comfortable, good quality and good value!

Love these (and the brand) - def have some stretch I’m 60, 5’5” and 160lbs. Waist is approx 35,” hips 42” and I’m also very short waisted. Per Sidefeel’s size guide, I ordered sz 14. These fit me loosely, but I actually prefer a slightly oversized fit. However these also have some stretch to them so I feel strongly I could easily wear sz 12. I received these through Amazon Vine at a discounted price in exchange for my review, but I’ve paid full price for several jeans from Sidefeel in the recent past. It’s one of my favorite Amazon brands for denim. These are super comfortable. Not rigid - very soft and a bit on the thicker side. I’d consider these more of a Jogger style than true Barrel, but still, they make for a slimming silhouette. At the time of this review, these are slightly marked down. Due to the stretchiness, I’m considering trying sz 12 in one of the blue shades. Will update my review if I do. Definitely recommend these, but keep in mind the stretch factor.

Very comfortable! I cautiously trusted the manufacturer's size chart and decided to order one size larger than my usual size. This decision was based solely on listed waist measurements. I am glad I did. I do not like my clothing to fit snugly or tightly. These fit comfortably and I even have room if they should shrink in waist or length. The jeans are much heavier weight than I prefer. I like thinner, lightweight denim. However, they fit well and are very comfortable, so I will wear them. The front pockets are enormous and extend nearly to the knee and are nearly the width of the top of the thigh! I am 5' 4" and usually wear a size 12 due to my thick waist. I ordered these in a size 14. If you have a slender waist, you could certainly order your regular size. These rest below my natural waist, even in a larger size. I imagine that these would take much longer to fully dry because of all the gathers and heavy weight denim. I usually only dry my clothes for a few minutes, then hang to fully dry to avoid wrinkles. If you think you would like this style jeans, I highly recommend thes Sidefeel Barrel jeans. They are well made and very reasonably priced!

On this light colored pair, the variations in color were a little strange in that it almost looked color blocked with a dark section in the waist/hips and ankles and a lighter section in the thighs/knees. I would have preferred a bit more subtlety. The stitching shows in some places on the side seams so I don’t know how well these will hold up in the long run. But overall, a good casual pair of jeans.

Great Fit! I am so happy with these pants! They fit well, are stretchy, and flattering. I bought 8S and they just touch the floor with no shoes on so they are a tad on longer side than most short length pants but not by too much For reference, I’m 5’2” and 161 lbs (thanks to Xmas treats 🥴), hourglass shape, pretty evenly proportioned w/ weight evenly distributed. W/ a mom tummy. They say to measure at my natural waist which is 33” an hips 41”. The size chart is way off or I do not know how to read a size chart?!. I am normally a size 8 or 10 so I bought both sizes and the 8 fit (a bit snug in waist but I hope to get rid of Xmas goodies waist) According to their size chart I’m a 12! I measured the size 8 pants on my body. from the crotch seam to the top of the pants is the rise =10.5” Waistband =35.5” Hips = 41” Inseam 30” The waist sits right in the center of my navel. If you have a shorter longer torso they may sit higher or lower - which is why garment measurements help

We’ll fit wide leg jeans Over the last year, my daughter has really gotten into super wide legged shorts (or jorts) and pants. I totally understand why though. At her age I loved super wide legged pants too because they’re cute and incredibly comfortable, not to mention they’re really in style. These are no different. These are made from a fairly light to mid-weight denim material. It’s soft to the touch and feels nice against the skin. Because they’re not thick you can wear them year round. My daughter already had a pair of shorts made by this company so I ordered the same size in these as I had in the jorts, which was size 4. The waist fits her perfectly. They’re not too baggy in the waist, they don’t slide down and she doesn’t need a belt but can wear one if she wants to to add some flare. These are definitely super baggy jeans as shown, but they’re supposed to be for this style. They really do look as pictured. These are a bit long on her, but that’s common because she’s on the shorter side. Overall, these seem to be well-made out of a comfortable light to mid-weight denim. According to my daughter, they are very comfortable and despite how baggy they are, they really do fit very well and the waist and they run true to size. I’ve ordered my daughter wide legged jeans shorts from this brand in the same size and these seem to fit very similarly. I feel like the $40 price tag is a little bit high but it’s not unheard of as long as the jeans hold up as well as the shorts from this company have I’ll get my money is worth. For a reference my daughter is 5‘2“, 115 pounds and we ordered the size 4 and they couldn’t fit more perfectly other than the length.

Wide leg, comfy & no shrinkage in hottest water ⭐️: These jeans in the darker wash and wide legs with the vertical front seam are giving me a sprinkle of 70s retro vibes, with a tiny splash of 90s bagginess. They’re fun. :) 👍: The fabric is surprisingly comfortable, although there are a few “raw” seam edges inside at the top waist which are irritating to my sensitive skin. 👖👍: The length on my 5’4” frame goes almost to the floor and based on the item’s product size chart and actual item received, the jeans I got run true to size. ➡️: SIDE NOTE: do wish these came in a smaller size, as they are baggy on my 26” waist, so they aren’t the most flattering in the booty area. But they don’t gap out in the back as many jeans tend to do on me! ⬅️ 🧺 For those needing a smaller size other than a 4 or just wondering about shrinkage in the wash– I did launder and dry a couple times on the hottest setting to try and shrink but they did NOT shrink up at all. 🤪 I would have given a 5 star rating if the seams inside were smoother, but overall these are priced really well at $40 for a fun, on-trend and comfy pair of jeans! Might as well as try ‘em out if you are curious. See my Video for fit.

Not True Jeans – More Like Jeggings, Sizing Runs Small These are definitely not jeans—they are jeggings. The material is very stretchy, which I personally like, but if you’re looking for a true denim feel, these are not it. I’m also curious to see how they hold up after several hours of wear, whether they’ll keep their fit or start to sag. I ordered the ripped 1010 light blue, and the pair I received does not match the picture. The color is different, and the placement of the rips and bleach marks are off. At 153 lbs, fit build, I usually wear a medium, but I ordered a large because I wanted them to sit lower on the hips and fit loose through the thighs and legs. Instead, the large fit me perfectly—not baggy at all—so I’ll be exchanging them for an extra-large to get the fit I originally wanted. Overall, if you like stretchy jeggings, these could work for you. But if you’re expecting solid denim that looks exactly like the listing photos, you may want to reconsider, especially for the price. I’ll update with another review once I receive the extra-large.

My favorite jeans! These are perfect! I am 210, 5’4 (apple shape). They have a nice stretch and fit around my waist comfortably and are not so big in the leg that I look like I’m in balloon pants. I ordered both 2x and 3x and did not notice much of a difference, so I am keeping both.

Finally, Jeans That Don’t Make Me Look Like a Potato Sack I’m 5’2” and usually buying jeans feels like choosing between tripping over extra fabric or looking like I accidentally borrowed a child’s pair. But these? The “short” length is actually short enough for me—no impromptu denim hemming project required. 🙌 The fabric is a little thicker than what I expected for stretch jeans, but honestly, I’ll take it. They hold everything in place without feeling like I’m wrapped in shrink-wrap. Let’s just say I can bend over without hearing the distant threatening rip sound that some other brands give me. Bonus: they’re well-made, comfy, and flattering. My husband said, “Nice jeans,” which in husband language is basically a standing ovation.

I wanted to love these I am 5ft 8.5" tall, at tje moment I have a 33" waist, 45" hip and 27" thigh. In the pictures the pants have been washed and tumble dried low. Before washing I was excited about these pants, after washing I was super bummed. I knew they would shrink because of the cotton content, but there was significant shrinking. The covers for the strings all but crumbled. I love the fabric of these pants, and the softness. They are a good weight for spring and fall. I would say they are good winter pants except my ankles would be freezing, lol. I love that the back has elastic and it has a string for tightening on the waist. I am someone who fluctuates in weight quite often, so these could fit multiple sizes without a belt. I was hoping for the oversized look, if that's what you are looking for I would suggest sizing up when factoring shrinking. I ordered my true size based on size chart (14). They fit perfectly (relaxed fit) before washing. Unfortunately I did not get photos before washing.

Perfect work pants. I originally purchased these in the reddish brown color and loved them so much that I wanted a second pair in white. Amazon/Dokotoo decided to send me a completely different pair of pants…. Nice try. Anyway they fit great and I like to wear them with the bottoms rolled up one since they sit right above the ankle. (I’m 5’7). They’re fantastic work pants and a perfect addition to my wardrobe.

Khaki cargo pants, good material & quality, looks polished but feel casual Even though the listing calls them jeans, the khaki option clearly looks like a khaki pant in the photos - and that’s exactly what I was expecting and what I got. The material is solid and not at all thin, and the color matches the pictures perfectly. There’s a drawstring front plus some elastic in the back, which works well for my shape (wider hips, narrower waist). I also like that these come in numbered sizes rather than just small, medium, or large, which makes it easier to get a good fit. They hit right at the ankle. That’s not my favorite length because of how I grew up thinking “too short pants”, but it’s definitely in style today. My workaround is to cuff them slightly, which actually looks deliberate and holds well thanks to the sturdier fabric. They’re comfortable, versatile, and could work as casual or even a little bit business casual depending on how you style them. One small drawback is that there’s no back pocket. For me, with a rounder shape, that makes my backside feel more highlighted than I’d like, but that’s easily balanced out with the right top. The cargo-style pockets on the sides are functional and deep without feeling too young or out of place for me at age 50. Overall, I found these pants to be high-quality, true-to-size (I wear an 8 and the 8 fit well), and well worth the price. They feel more polished than other cargo pants but still just as comfortable. Pros Sturdy material, not thin True-to-size numbered sizing Elastic back and drawstring for easy fit Stylish ankle length that can be cuffed Functional pockets Comfortable for all-day wear Cons No back pockets, which may not flatter everyone Ankle length may not suit all preferences Product: Dokotoo Womens 2024 Fall Mid Waist Jeans Casual Cargo Pants Drawstring Elastic Waist Khaki Pants with Pockets

I ordered a size Small in mid-blue, and I’m usually a US size 4. They fit well overall, but I do think an XS would have fit me better because of how much stretch they have. Overall, super comfortable, great color, and easy to wear — perfect for a casual date or everyday outfit.

More comfortable than expected Alright, look. I’m a millennial mom trying to hang onto my skinny jeans for dear life. This new wide leg jeans trend is significantly less flattering for those of us with hips, unless you’re into elephant legs. Especially for the short hourglass girlies like me. I’m 5’2” and currently in my holiday-hibernating-storing-fat-for-the-winter era. If I can’t have my skinny jeans because they’re “out” then at least give me some comfortable wide legs jeans, ok? I reluctantly ordered these barrel leg jeans from Zesica. I’ll start by saying I do really like this brand. I have a quite a bit of Zesica clothing items in my closet now, though mostly dresses. This was my first jeans purchase from the brand. Here are my thoughts: 1. They ARE comfortable. I’ll give them that. They’re roomy and baggy. Don’t expect tummy control. They’re so loose in the waist that I could pull them pretty far out, and because of this they did make me look like I had a pooch. Not loving that so much, so tucking shirts into these jeans is probably out. I generally wear a 8-10 and could have fit into a M, but I sized up to a L to accommodate these hips that don’t lie. 2. Because of their barrel leg shape, they give “Canadian Mountie Oompa Loompa.” Not gonna lie, I don’t even hate it. Like I said, they’re crazy comfortable, and some days I’m too tired to care about anything beyond that. I could Oompa Loompa through all of my errands in these, but they feel a little too casual to count as dressy jeans for a night out. 3. The color looks like the listing photos but it also doesn’t. I can’t put my finger on how. I’m certain I wore this color jeans in the 90s. 4. The stretch. So much stretch. These jeans stretch harder than our moms did in 1992 to a Richard Simmons VHS tape. 5. They’re cut short, like the listing photos. Remember, I’m 5’2” so even ankle jeans are still grazing the tops of my feet. They’re high-waisted just like the description says. 6. Expect a very breathable and lightweight denim. They *almost* feel more like soft cotton than denim, like the jeans version of a favorite t-shirt. They’re most definitely true to size — I’m probably less than 5 lbs away from these being too big on me and would need to switch out for my regular M, even as a curvy girl. They’re very on-trend right now, so if you’re on the fence, I’d say go for it. While not the most flattering jeans for my particular body shape, I’ll still rock the Willy Wonka out of them.

Pretty good jeans! Weird pockets though. I’m 5’4” and currently around 220 lbs, usually wear an XL, but ordered an XXL based on the size chart and they fit very comfortably, especially over my postpartum belly flap. They’re baggy without looking sloppy and have a tapered leg, which I really like for winter since they don’t drag or catch snow. They’re comfortable to sit in and don’t press into my stomach, even while breastfeeding. I will say there is a bit of a weird thing going on with the front pockets where it's like there's not enough material between them and so the white part of the pockets ends up showing sometimes. These are not pants that you're gonna want to wear with a shorter shirt, or with your shirt tucked in like where you're showing the button fly of the pants. I'm not really comfortable showing that part of the pants so you'll have to just trust me about it. It's not flattering! The denim has good stretch, and I probably could have sized down to an XL and still been fine. I ordered the mid blue color and it’s a nice darker wash with a very 80s-style denim look. Overall, they’re flattering, comfortable, and easy to wear for everyday outings and I can overlook the pocket issue because there's so much I still like about them. I'm obviously not sure how they would fit for someone who is thinner, but for a person who is trying to figure out how to dress a newly plus size body (thanks babies!). These have definitely made me very happy and given me a solid option for my winter mom wardrobe.

Comfortable, but Barrel? So right off the bat, these are incredibly comfortable. It’s more like a denim pull up pants than actual jeans. It’s denim, but thin, and and light. The way they’re cut is like a comfy pant. Even the way their pockets are placed toward the front and loose. It’s great as an alternative to sweats or like a pant to wear around the house, but easily wear out and about. I was nervous about this barrel jeans trend, but i think these end up fitting more just like a wide leg jean than barrel. I ordered my usual size Medium (for reference, I’m 5’3” about 150-155 lbs. ). Maybe if I sized down, they’d seem more barrel, and now that I’ve been wearing them, they seemed to have stretched out too, even though the actual jeans aren’t stretchy. It feels really wide. The wash color was great dark, like pictured. And it’s good for short girls, but hits more like regular length, than ankle , like would on taller ladies.

These came used !!! Washed and kept due to how I liked the product, not dirty, but... The item is great, love the pants. HOWEVER... these were sold to me as new, they had tags, BUT I found this in the pocket after I wore them to town. I kept them because I really like how they fit. Cute and fun jeans with a trendy look These Astylish wide leg jeans are such a cute and fun addition to my wardrobe. The ruffled hem really sets them apart and makes them versatile. You can dress them up with a blouse for an evening dinner date, or pair them with a casual T-shirt for a cozy, relaxed day of shopping. The denim is made from a thinner lightweight material, which makes these jeans very comfortable to wear. They have a slight stretch, but I didn’t find that they stretched out or lost shape throughout the day. Since these aren’t made from standard heavy denim, there isn’t a lot of tummy support which I personally would have liked, but that trade-off does add to the overall comfort. I will say that I struggled a bit pairing them with shoes as they didn’t quite lay right with some styles. After some tinkering I found they looked best with sandals or an above-ankle boot. Fit-wise, I’d say they are true to size. I ordered a size 8 in dark blue, and they fit perfectly for my 5’4” height and 150 lb weight. Overall, I personally love these jeans. They’re stylish, comfortable, unique and I would definitely recommend them if you’re looking to add something fun and trendy to your wardrobe.

Functional pockets! I've gained some weight recently and regular jeans just don't work for me anymore. I find them to be too stiff or too tight or too restrictive so I've purchased a couple "jeans" from this brand before because I love how comfortable they are. This pair is no different even though the style is not exactly the same. These "jeans" are not the usual jeggings that are totally fake and look fake (jeans print). They actually look and feel like real jeans but only thinner, softer, and more stretchy. They are pull-on pants with the crossover wrap high waist like yoga pants and flare legs like bell-bottoms. I'm 5'1" and 130lbs and I usually get the shorter 29" inseam so I can wear them with sneakers. These are a little long for me (31" inseam), but I can wear them with boots or other heels. Also, all the pockets are functional and I'm a sucker for pockets!

Inseam not 31", comfy, more like leggings than jeans Update: At time of order, sizing included choosing inseam length. It's now changed to petite, standard and tall, and my review header shows "standard", which was definitely not the case and I have the invoice to prove it. Just FYI if you read my review- I'm not crazy, I ordered a 31" inseam. Maybe they've sorted out their inseam problem with the new sizing choices, idk. Original review: Fit/Sizing - I'm 5'8" tall, US women size 6/Small. Ordered Small w/ 31" inseam. Fit is comfy like leggings, but inseam is over an inch shorter than stated. Regular sizes are often too short on me (and tall sizes too long, ugh), but these are offered in different lengths, and I really wanted that length. Awkwardly short at high ankle. Stretch out slightly while wearing, tug them up occasionally. Materials/Design - I like the cotton/poly blend. Most of this is evident from product photos, but in case there's confusion, these are definitely leggings w/ a denim look, not to be mistaken for "stretchy flare jeans". Mostly because the waist is pull-on, no zipper or closure. The flare swishes looser like a legging than denim, which is sturdier. Wide crossover waist reminiscent of maternity wear. Not a high-end look but that's ok, still more put together than sweatpants and as long as the length works for you, fit is flattering, doesn't smash the rear or dig in at the waist. Rating details - I take time to write honest reviews that I would find . Previously ordered similar pants from diff. Amazon store, also had wrong inseam. But I liked the hybrid look for lounge wear that wasn't too frumpy, excited someone else was offering them, and they have the same problem! Fit details are important, it wastes time and effort ordering something that is wrongly described. Plus, there is a large obvious defect in the material. Value is subjective but if I saw these at T**get I'd expect about a $25 price tag.

Not my favorite, but cute! For sizing reference, I'm 5'3", currently 145 lbs with measurements about 40"-30"-40" (bust-waist-hip), and I'm wearing size 4-6 in jeans or a medium in bottoms in most brands right now. Another random note but I have a shorter torso with longer legs (proportionally), aka I rarely wear short length in pants and even mid-rise bottoms hit pretty high waisted on me. I purchased these jeans in a SIZE 29 in the colorway ACCENT OF AZALEA. As far as sizing goes, I went with the safe bet and ordered a 29, despite being a pretty solid 28/6 right now, and I think it was a good choice. I could have done a 28, but these are comfortable, so I’m fine with it as long as they don’t stretch out. Like most bootcut jeans, these are fitted through the waist, hips, and thighs, then looser in the calf area. I’ve been wanting something that isn’t a skinny or straight leg, but isn’t too big and baggy, and these did the trick. I can’t even remember the last time I owned bootleg jeans but I’m glad I tried again. They are a little long and drag a bit when I wear sneakers, but nothing too dramatic. If you actually wear boots (which I don’t), they’re probably perfect. For the material, these are a thinner stretchy denim. I doubt they would last through years of wear and tear, so if that’s an issue for you, maybe try a different style like the 501s. They do have a zip fly, thank goodness… The construction is also pretty good. The color of these is nice. They’re a lighter wash that doesn’t dip into looking bleached. It’s a nice change if you’re someone like me that mostly sticks with standard denim shades. Overall, I’d definitely recommend these if you’re looking for a more casual, thin pair of bootcut jeans.

Boyfriend gives stamp of approval (he noticed, haha) I'm in love with these jeans!! Love that they've got some stretch to them (they did bag out from some casual around the house wear, but nothing crazy.) They are certainly high waisted, after a meal I did have to undo a button for comfort because I prefer my size, if not a little smaller than my usual size because of the stretchy material. My boyfriend says the "fit like a glove!!" So I've won in that department. They feel sturdy, great value for quality and material has a good amount of give to it so I'd say they're more comfortable than the standard for of jeans, I did order 2 different colors / styles from above (I'm 182lbs, 5"4' and usually about a size 14. The dark pair fit perfectly on me in a size 32 x 32. For some reason I was sent the incorrect size in the ripped knee light wash pair so I'll be returning those unfortunately (I got sent an 18 L... not sure how that happened and kinda disappointed but I love this pair before so much I'll keep it at 5 stars.) The question is: are they real Levi's?! I thinkkkk so?! A bit hard to say but winter way I'd recommend the dark wash racer stripe pair. If you're between a 180-190 and between 5"4' to 5"6' don't size up or down, stick with the 32 x 32 !!

Stretchy, forgiving but slightly too long! I got the *Eternal Indigo 30 Regular*. For reference, I’m 5'5”, about 155 lbs, with shorter legs and a medium torso. I originally ordered the *short* length, but they were too short. The regular length is about two inches longer, which was a surprising jump in length, but they ended up working okish with heels or boots, still a little long for my personal preference.. may get hemmed. The fit is very flattering: slim and stretchy on top, with slightly more than a bootcut and a subtle flare at the bottom. They definitely have real stretch, so even if they feel a little tight at first, they’re forgiving and comfortable. I’m four months postpartum so a little extra water weight wasn’t a problem with the forgiving stretch of these jeans. They sit just *below* my belly button, so I wouldn’t personally call them high-waisted, but the overall fit is still good enough that I’m keeping them. One note: they did lose a bit of color after the first wash. I’d recommend washing cold on delicate and hang-drying to keep the dark indigo.

725 vs 726 I'm 5'8 with a long torso but I have short legs. I wear around a size 14/16, and I have wide hips and a larger lower stomach. I ordered a 34/30 based on the measurements listed and they fit fine, but I don't think these are the jeans for me. Here's a comparison review between the Levi's 725 (bootcut - color - is it denim) and Levi's 726 (flare - color - seams to work). I prefer jeans that have a higher rise and sit above my belly button to help lessen a Muffin top. The 725 sits a little higher above the belly button than the 726, but neither sit much higher, and it's like they sit right at/slightly above my bellybutton. The 725 have a medium/high stretch, while the 726 have a more rigid, mild stretch. For the 725, I could probably wear a 33, while a 34 is necessary for the 726. I prefer the look of the 726, but the 725 are more comfortable. The 30 inch inseam is great for my shorter legs. I've attached pics wearing both of these styles, and side by side comparison pics.

Very durable! I am 5'4" and have an hourglass figure, with a 40" hip and 28" waist; so it is difficult to find a pair of pants that fits well; but these pants were a pleasant surprise. I usually only buy curvy jeans; but I needed some that would not only fit me comfortably but would also last a long time. My curvy jeans fit me like a glove; but they are almost worn away after only a year. So I thought I would try these Levi's, at least to wear when doing chores around my family's farm. I bought the 27 Regular size. They were on sale for $50 instead of $70, otherwise I would never have gotten them. But I was paying $40 for my other jeans to only last a year. I figured that, if these Levi's last me a few years for only $10 more, it would be worth the difference. These jeans are a much better quality than my other jeans. They are just slightly large about the waist and a little snug around my behind. But that difference is totally doable, for me. Unfortunately, I cannot wear my backbrace underneath these pants (which I have to wear almost every day because of my broken back). It just doesn't fit. So I can only wear these pants when my back is feeling alright. These pants have very spacious pockets and did not have any shrinkage after the first wash. I would definitely recommend these jeans. They feel very durable and the stitching is very well done. The back pockets are a little oddly wide. When I put my phone in my pocket, it feels way too close to my hip to feel right. But that doesn't bother me enough for it to be an issue. These jeans look so good with boots! They fit so perfectly over my cowboy boots that I feel like I have never had a pair of actual bootcut jeans, even though that is the shape I have been getting for over a year. They are the perfect length and width to fit over my boots without getting in the way of the heel. This was a great purchase, for me! (edit) I took away a star because, after more wear, I noticed that the dye in the jeans would bleed onto my skin at the knees whenever I had to kneel down.

Perfect fit and style These pants are perfect for a business casual dress code. The fit 8 short) was perfect for me. I am 5'3", 132 lbs.The fabric doesn't show wrinkles, and I love the boot cut. These were on sale for less than $17 (USD) and well worth buying.

I’m a curvy size 12 and these trousers are a 12. I didn’t think the zip would do up when I first put them on but the trousers have a good amount of stretch in them and they fitted great. The only place they pulled a little was by the side pockets but the 14 would have been too big for me. So if you’re more an athletic 12 they will hang looser. I’m going to say they are true to size. They were very comfortable to sit in and loosened up again. They are 3/4 length on me being 5’7 but they are a cropped style so perfect for Summer and evening wear. Wash on warm. Made in Indonesia. Amazing value for money. I hope my video helps to compare body sizes and I’m happy to recommend ☺️

Need some chinos? How about some 3/4 lengths? These are the Good Threads Womens Stretch Chino Utility Pant, Made From 98% Cotton, 2% Elastane, Sizes 10 - 18 And Colours Black, Dark Olive, Ivory, Mauve, Navy, Camel Or Rust Orange. These chinos are thinner than normal chinos, they are slightly stretchy though. The length is 3/4 length, so almost like pedal pushers. They have 2 x front hip pockets, 2 x buttock pockets, so pretty standard jean layout with belt loopholes. The one thing that is odd, is the bizarre front zip, what is wrong with Traditional zips? The good thing is these are both machine wash and dryable, so no faffing around. But there will be colour bleed to begin with, so wash with similar colours. My eldest lives these, she is 5"11 and looks great in em with her long legs, and they fit superbly. She's happy. Overall I'd recommend these chinos, as they are very well made, albeit a tad pricey. Oh and overlook that bizarre front zip!

LOVE these jeans! Bought multiple colors! LOVE these jeans so much! These are perfect for a little bit of ”extra” of an outfit! I first bought the blue jeans to try to wear with T-shirts in the spring and summer. I loved them so much that I purchased a pair in white to wear for my upcoming vacation. Okay for now I purchased these because I have a pair from Sanctuary that I LOVE, but I wanted a darker pair, so I figured I would give these a try. I tried on the pants and the fabric was heavier than I expected. It gets hot down here in Florida, so I like the light weight jeans. Also, the front pockets looked ‘off’, not symmetrical. When I took my Sanctuary pair out to compare them, the Sanctuary front pockets were much bigger, they had a decorative run of stitching toward the top, and of course, pockets were symmetrical. I posted photos, maybe you can see it. I got the medium as per Amazon’s suggestion. I’m 5’4, 138 lbs. they felt good enough so I washed them. I wore them out today and after a couple hours I noticed they were loose, my tucked shirt kept slipping out. I did like the length, I’m 5’4” with a short torso and long legs and they stopped above my ankle. I just ordered the small and hope they fit. It’s frustrating being between sizes. We’ll see how it goes and I’ll update

Great quality but sizing is inconsistent I’ve bought 3 or 4 of these in different sizes and colors. The fit is so flattering, with a kick flare cropped leg that hits above my ankle (I’m about 5’5.5”.) The rise is high, slightly above my belly button. The fabric is thick with medium stretch. The quality is good, with functioning metal zippers and well-sewn pockets. One pair snagged on something metal, but I latched it with one of those iron-on denim patches (applied behind the fabric), and it still looks good. My only issue is with sizing inconsistency. I have a dark and very light blue, as well as a black and washed black. Some are in Large and others in XL. One of the L pair is unwearable because it’s so tight (med blue - tight in waist and upper thighs), but another fits - albeit snugly (also med blue but a different shade). The XL pair in light blue is wearable but quite baggy. The washed black L fit pretty well but shrank slightly and now digs in quite a bit in the waist and crotch when I sit down. I would order more but am afraid to get a pair that’s wildly small or too baggy. Everyone on my office has bought these because they look great on so many different body types.

Quality, comfortable and flattering high waisted jeans. Fit/Size: The product fits me well, I have a hard time finding pants, especially jeans, that fit me. I am 5'2 about 150lbs and have short legs and wide hips so it is very difficult to find pants that not only fit me length wise but that will also fit properly over my hips as well. Surprisingly these fit with no problems and are comfortable on my waist without being too loose or too tight. I would have preferred these jeans be a bit more form fitting around my derrière and then widen more because they feel loose throughout so I feel like I lose some shape, but considering they fit so well everywhere else it seems like a fair trade off, I am hoping a couple more washes will help fix this. I also notice they do end closer to my ankles so when I sit down they rise up higher which seems to be how they are meant to fit, I don't wear wide leg jeans so it bothers me a bit, but overall the look is flattering and the fit is comfortable, I only mention this so buyers are aware they will sit higher especially with heels. Color: I ordered Lakeside blue and I really love the color and it matches the online photos. Quality: These jeans are very well made, the material feels durable and soft so they are very comfortable to wear. I don't see any wonky, loose or poor stitching. The pockets are decently sized on both the front and back and feel like they will keep their shape well. Based off the quality I think these are a great value for a pair of jeans that will last me for a long time.

Could be cute but run short and small. Don't seem to complement curvy figures. I ordered these pants in black, because I was looking for a bit less contrast between the regular jean color and the flipped up color in the legs. I do think the contrast is less obvious, but I was disappointed in the overall fit. As background, I'm very tall at 5'11", but I have shortish legs, and I go between tall/long pants, which often only work well with heels, and regular length pants, which tend to ride up when I sit. I estimate these pants at maybe 1/2 in shorter than most regular length pants, so on me, they're too long to be high waters and too short to be regular pants. Even more upsetting for me, these pants are very straight from top to bottom with no flare at the end, while I'm on the curvier side. This means they're unflattering to my body shape, with no shape of their own to improve the look. And although I ordered a size up, since my size normally falls between the stated ranges, these ended up being too tight on me to be comfortable. Overall, I think these pants are just ok. The stretchy jean material is nice, the stitching seems solid, and the washed out black is an appealing color for casual dress. I'd say they run a little small and a little short, though. If you're an average sized or shorter person who's not too hippy, these might be cute. Unfortunately for me, they don't work.

converted cuffed jeans into tall sizes jeans This review is for the “ GRAPENT Wide Leg Jeans for Women Trendy High Waisted Cuffed Hem Stretchy Denim Trousers Pants Work Casual Jeans“ I ordered the white jeans plainly based on the look of the cuff. There are no inseam measurements state in the listing, but in the photos they looked long. I was lucky, I am 5’10, 156 lbs and ordered the Large. The fit and size are perfect on me, and when I opened the seam and let out the cuff the pants came to a 34” inseam, exactly what I need. There are so few sellers offering tall sizes that I had to become creative and find ways to convert regular jeans (like cuffed ones) into something in my length. I love the quality and the stretch of the 2% elasthane. The pants washed well and i stretched them lengthwise to get even a half more inch in length. he white is not see through, a great bonus! So, for all the tall ladies, if you see these, the cuff is not just a piece of cloth sewn on, it is actually the leg of the pants just folded up and sewn into a tall outside cuff. With some sharp scissors and a bit of patience you can take the cuff off easily. the price of $37 is a good deal for this nice pair of jeans. I would get this item/set again.

Stylish. Comfortable Jeans The Grapent wide leg, high-waist denim jeans are stylish and comfortable. The Washed Black is a medium black color denim that has a worn look. This is a good price for a pair of comfortable and stylish jeans.

BEST JEANS! EVER! Over the years, I've gone from living stretchy jeans, to essentially trying to LIVE in super stretchy, jegging type jeans. I don't have a lot but I want more. I'd love to look good while feeling extremely comfy, so I took a chance on these. I am 5'3 and have a 32" inseam but these are almost perfect as they are. They have a cute fit. I'm not crazy about the little stitches on the back pockets (seems unnecessary) but the fit is just cute and flattering! Style wise, I love that these look like regular jeans as they have real zipper and button closure, real pockets, but have the comfort of Jennings or pajama denim. I want these in a million colors!

Give these jeans a try - You might be happy too! These are a soft denim with just the right amount of stretch to be comfortable, but not being all stretched out on you during the day to where you have to yank on them all the time I would totally give a five star rating for comfort and fit, but the denim coloring in the stock photo is way off of what you receive. The photo looks very attractive. What you get is a nice denim but it’s more of what you expect of every day faded look denim. The stock photo looks spectacular. You don’t get that color in actuality, but they’re amazingly comfortable. I love the side pocket in this particular variety from the company. because it fits your cell phone nicely and totally hides it away. I like that it’s a very loose fit after coming down from the skin tight skinny gene era. It’s nice to go back to comfort while not compromising style. It might be difficult for some to decide on sizing for these because general small, medium, large type sizing is kinda vague. Every person’s body style is different. So it’s very hard to say they fit “all” but these happen to fit me perfectly. I bought the medium I am 5’7” and I weigh 145-150 it fluctuates, I’m 64 with a little pooch and a flatter bottom 🤣

Comfy and Stretchy! Moves with You! WallFlower Fearless Curvy 17.5" Capri Super High-Rise Insta Vintage Jeans, SIZE 1, COLOR Evan: I'm 5'2" about 193lbs. I have a 39” Waist and 45” Hips, Upper Thighs 26-27” and Lower Thighs 20-22”. I normally wear a Large or 12/14. These size 11 fit me nicely. I don’t consider these ‘super high-rise’ by any means. I have room to breathe, sit, and squat. These jean capris are made with stretchy jean material. I consider these to be of good mid-weight quality. I did not notice any loose strings or seams. The darker color is flattering. I can see how these will stretch out throughout the day, but I haven’t found a pair of jeans that don’t. I also like how I can fold over the bottom to make them look more like bermuda shorts. Overall, I’m very happy with these. They are comfortable and run true to size, maybe a little larger than true to size, but I haven’t washed/dried these yet. Would recommend.

Ordered every color love them Love love love... Ordered a lot of different brands they were too short even though they said 31 inseam. Ordered first the grey blue in medium 5'6 160lbs they were slightly snug as I pulled them up to my waist. Decided to order the large to compare. Large was much better. Missing the flare I normally wear my jeans with a straight leg that are too small to take off with your shoes on. I liked the way these jeans looked in the ad. They look curvy with a flare at the bottom, and I thought I'd give them a try. These are a good-quality pair of jeans with five pockets. The front pockets are pretty deep, with a lining that feels soft and silky. The zipper is silver-colored, although it's a little stiff. Upon receiving them and seeing their shape, I could tell that they didn't have the flared or "bell bottom" style. I tried them on right away and then took them off even quicker. Being plus-size most of my life, I've always hated jeans like this. It's not just the bottom that's flared, but actually the whole leg. I've always felt that plus-size jeans were made with the thought that just because you have a larger waist, you must have legs the size of an elephant. A bell bottom I can handle, but not elephant legs. I used to wear the largest size I could find, but now I wear anywhere from a size 12 to 16 depending on the pants and the amount of stretch. I ordered these in size 16, and they are too big; plus, they are very stretchy on top of that. They're just too uncomfortably big without any kind of curvy shape to them. Additionally, they are too long for me, but I'm 5'5", and I usually run into this problem. Currently, 25% off is a fair price, and they're a good pair of jeans for someone; just not for me.

Nice pair of jeans I bought 3 different colors,“leisure time”(black wash),“indigo”(dark blue wash) and “lunch plans”(lighter blue wash). All size 29. I am 30” waist, 42” hips. Give or take half an inch. As reference. The “lunch plans” color definitely felt more stretchy/give to it than the other two colors which felt a little more structured/less give. However they all are labeled as the same material %’s. I could probably do the size 30 for a more loose fit on the “leisure time” and “indigo” colors but I’m sure with daily wear they will loosen a tad. I only keep them bc my hip to waist ratio always tends to have the waist gap on pants. The gap on these is not bad as it does sit flush to my back probably bc I chose to size down. My favorite pair of jeans!! I needed a new pair of jeans so decided to try on these Dokotoo jeans for size! This is the first pair of size 16 jeans that I've bought in probably 15 years, really crossing my fingers hoping that these fit. Very pleasantly surprised! The pork chop pockets are cute and functional and so are the back pockets. The cuff area is more straight and not flared or bell bottoms. I love the way these Dokotoo jeans fit and the way they look! There is stretch around the waist AND all through the hips and thighs. The waist is a traditional button fly design. Took photos of the jeans on and off plus front and back. I am an apple shape and carry most of my weight in the belly area if that helps as far as sizing. (I'm 210 and 5'8)

I love the fit and the look of these jeans I ordered the Classic Blue cropped, wide-leg pull-on jeans in my normal size after consulting the size chart. I am very happy with the comfortable, relaxed fit. The hem is a couple of inches above my ankle just as I expected for cropped jeans. I confirmed the 26 inch inseam with my tape measure, and while I was at it, I checked the other dimensions. They all matched the size chart, so these jeans are true to size for X-Large at least. I like the flattering look of these jeans with their wide legs and simple fly-less front. The functional drawstring is small and understated, and it looks nice. The front pockets are deeper than I expected which was a pleasant surprise. I also like the lack of any distressing. One difference from the photos is the thread. All exposed stitching was done with classic gold thread, not white. I actually prefer the gold thread, so I'm OK with the difference. The medium-weight denim fabric feels good and is not coarse nor scratchy. The overall quality of construction is quite good.

So Cute, So stretchy, So Adjustable, So Versatile These are such nice shorts. They aren't too short and have plenty of room, yet look stylish and cute for almost any woman. They must be sized smaller since I can fit into the size M. The hips are a bit too wide for me. I'm 5'5" and 110 lbs. with a waist of 27 1/2" and hips of 34" and purchased the size M's which fit comfortably except the extra material in the hips. What makes these shorts so versatile for most anyone are the adjustments: all throughout with the stretch, then in the waist with the wonderful elastic in the entire length of the waist, and then the drawstring you can tighten up in case the waist ends up being too loose on us thinner women. And the leg length is just long enough without being too dumpy looking, but still modest enough for older women. They are so cute. There's no wonder why these shorts cost a bit more for denim shorts at $25.59, with all the adjustable features. They've been distressed washed, so they are a very soft. These shorts get an A+ from me.

Heavy denim stretch waist look at model closely 5’7” 195- 200lbs Size 18 is too big because I thought the regular size 16 would be too small. Should have bought regular size. I liked: * Heavy weight denim 70% cotton. * Stretch 28.5% polyester and 1.5 % elastic-elastine * Loose fit in belly and legs * Not tight on ankles * Real pocket hold a cellphone iPhone 13 Pro * Not too hot - not too cold * Very comfortable on the skin after washing. I did not like the length. I know they are supposed to be at the ankle. They can be rolled up to look stylish. This is great for business casual days at work and for nurses who wear regular clothes but still need to do patient care. Nice to not burst out of pants while bending. I purchased mine too large. I was worried that the belly would be too tight. The elastine lets the waist stretch without being granny style elastic. I recommend offering your actual size. I would buy again. I will keep these because they are not jeggings and can still be worn baggy style. I recommend a figure defining top to give you a shape because the pants are baggy. I will keep these size 18 and order another pair sized to my regular size 16 and then style like the model. The first pair will become lounge jeans and work around the house jeans because they really are comfortable without being jeggings. Fits good! Not too long for me. Something I’ll often run into the trouble of, is for whatever reason pants manufacturers seem to think wide hips mean long legs. I know so many narrow-hipped women with long legs, and wide-hipped women with “short” or average sized legs and a big bottom. I’m in the latter category. For whatever reason, it is often hard for me to find pants where the waistband will go around my hips, but the bottom of the leg won’t go past my ankles. I’ve gotten good at hemming the bottom of pants over the years because of this, but, luckily, I don’t have to with these! In fact, I often look for “boyfriend cut” jeans for exactly that reason, because they tend to fall just above or just under my ankles, while being the correct size for my hips and waist according to the sizing. These end just below my ankles, and won’t trip me over. (I’m 5’4” for reference). As much as I’d love to be able to rock the flattering boot-cut look, I can only pull it off on days where I’m wearing high-heel boots, and even then, I sometimes have to fix the bottom so the denim doesn’t go past my high heels. On a day to day basis where I wanna wear flip-flops or sneakers in the summer, it’s gotta be a boyfriend cut jean, so that it has the right shape and length, without being too circulation-cutting tight like skinny jeans would be. These pants fit PERFECT. The last boyfriend cut jeans I bought were from Levi-Strauss but they no longer have the boyfriend cut on here for that brand as far as I can tell. So I decided to take a chance on the Lee Indigo Riders series, and I’m so glad I did. It seems lightweight but durable, as long as you buy the correct size for yourself. These are not hugging my thighs (they’re not supposed to, it’s called “boyfriend” cut for a reason). But, they do make my butt look nice! And they hide my belly pouch reasonably well. If you’re looking for tighter jeans, instead of sizing down and breaking these like some reviewers complained of, these are *not* oversized, they’re boyfriend cut. Understand what that style means, and go buy yourself some skinny jeans if you want to wear tighter pants without ripping the seams with your thighs. These are not built to be super form fitting, they’re built to hug in a few flattering places and be slightly lax slacks type fit in other places. That’s exactly what I wanted, and that’s exactly what I got. Flattering but not “too sexy” and professional looking, kosher to wear around older family members, etc. I recommend these!

Great jeans! Love them! 160 I love these and own them in every color and in 3 sizes! 8,10 & 12. My weight fluctuates due to a thyroid issue. Anyway, I have trouble with jeans/capris because I have a small waist, thicker thighs and a booty! I'm 5'3 currently 160lb. Can weigh 175. Cute Fit For My Teen I got these Cicy Bell Jeans for my 13 yr old in size 2. She’s 5’1” tall and 95 lbs. The size 2 is just slightly too baggy in the waist for her. She must wear a belt to keep them from sagging. The length is a bit long, but not so much that she’s tripping on them. The baby blue color looks very trendy and cute on her. She says they're okay and she'll happily wear them, but they're not her favorite jeans. The length is just right, but they'd be too long on women of regular height. You could easily pay twice as much for equally serviceable (but not great) jeans.
