Let's Talk About Sizing... I have several pairs of these jeans & I love them all. Never returned any. I am 5'2", 110lbs and I have always gotten size 3. This time I got a 1 based on amazon's reccomendation. I think I'll stick with the 3 moving forward as these are a bit tight. I feel a little self conscious wearing these since they fit like 2nd skin and I coyluld use a butt lift @ 52. They are comfortable though as there is plenty of give. If you are purchsing these for the 1st time or are unsure of what size to choose, I would definitely say NOT to go up too many sizes. I see people who say differently, but I think those people are not being realistic or don't actually know what their REAL size is. These measurements are 100% accurate. I have checked each pair that I own against my measurements- every one is dead on.

Style 92000 is definitely boot cut/straight NOT wide leg. There seems to be a lot of confusion as to the style being sold. I believe this is possibly because a few different styles are being sold under one listing. I could be wrong but I think if you focus on the number listed as the "Style" instead of color it may help. I ordered "92000" in dark blue (see pictures) and the legs are not wide at all. I would even consider them almost a straight leg rather than bootleg. I am short (5 ft 1 in) and average around 100 lbs. I bought a pair of Wax jeans size 1 (different listing/style) last year and while I really like them I have found they have really loosened up over time and are almost too big now. I went with a size 0 this time and while quite snug on me they are not uncomfortably tight and given how much my last pair stretched with wear I'm pretty sure these will as well. The size 1 was way too long (easily fixed; when you are short you get used to everything being too long) but the size 0 is only 1 or 2 inches too long. In summary: Style 92000 is NOT wide leg. Size 0 fits a 90-105 lb body well, though will be a bit snug for anyone over 100 lbs and is only slightly too long for someone around 5 feet tall. Very nice looking pair of jeans. Recommended for anyone who doesn't like skinny jeans yet wants comfort and style.

Sizing is interesting Sizing on these was really difficult. It took a few tries to figure it out so hopefully I can help someone. After reading about how raw denim works, it is best to choose a size that is slightly tight along your body.Because as you wear them, it will conform to your body.And stretch about a half an inch. So keep that in mind when you are looking at the size chart.You may want to go smaller I am 3 month postpartum looking for pants, leaving me somewhat squishy in the middle leaving me at a 31 waist and 42 hip. When I tried the size 11 to my measurements they where waaay too big. I tried the 9, still not good. Left crotch saggy. So I decided to skip the seven and went to the 5. I like the fit in the legs and honestly I think I will get them to fit great after constant wears, just a lil tight in the waist but I think if I get them wet amd wear them around I can fix that. I think the moral of the story is I woild size down 1 or even 2 sizes if you like your pants to fit tighter and not have a baggy crotch. This is coming from someone tall without alot of booty so If you are more curvy you may have a different experience but if u look like me size down. In the end u really like them and I think they will last me a long time

In love!! But wait.... EDITED REVIEW*** Umm... I'm not quite sure what happened, but I decided to order these Cowboy Cut in Blue because the Black ones were perfect. These jeans are the exact same size, but the blue ones are HUGE. They don't fit. Additionally I believe these were a return because they came with no tags and they feel like they've been washed. I'm disappointed. But my black jeans are still great. So these are some TRUE 100% cotton Jeans. There is no give and zero stretch which is what I wanted. After looking at the size chart I decided to get a size 1×30. I am 5'4, 125lbs and wear a size 25 in Levis 501s. In my opinion these jeans fit and look similar. I am definitely glad I got the size 1 because the zero definitely would have been too small. I can't wait to break these in and I'll definitely be getting another pair in a different color

The quality is awesome! COMFORT LEVEL is HIGH! I ordered a pair of women’s casual high waisted jeans by DOKOTOO in blue size 6. I am 5’4” tall, and weigh about 125lbs and these fit extremely well. The fabric is a blend of 72% cotton, 26% polyester, and 2% elastane. It’s quite stretchy when you move it sideways, but not much when you move it up and down. The stretch goes sideways, making the waist incredibly stretchy. I find this feature really awesome, to me stretching around the waist is important, especially after eating. These light blue jeans have a distressed look that goes down each leg and also in the back upper area. I absolutely love the distressed look. These jeans feature a design on the front pockets, both of which are functional. Additionally, there are two more usable pockets located in the back, on either side. They come with a zipper and a metal button closure. In my opinion, they have EXTRA wide legs that extend all the way down, making them incredibly comfortable! The stitching throughout the jeans is exceptional, making the quality very good. I think these jeans are a bit pricey, but the quality is awesome!

Great wide-leg jeans! Fantastic wide-leg jeans that are comfortable and trendy. The seaming, stitching and detail on these jeans are perfect. The fit is true-to-size (I'm 5'4" and 128 pounds). The black color is deep and rich with a washed hue for effect. There is a small amount of stretch to the material so the waist is not binding or tight, and there is no elastic on the back of the waist, so the lines are very clean. The material has a full, rich denim feel yet is soft on the skin. The small pockets on the front add just enough dimension for the overall style and do not add any bulk. The zipper is high-quality and glides with ease. The legs are cut wide, falling from the lower hip to the hem, and this creates a thinning silhouette. The jeans fall just above my ankles and I wore these to the office with a medium-heel pump to further elongate my legs and received many compliments. Overall, these jeans are a great value for the money.

Soft and stretchy, true to size, great for shorter frame I am on the shorter side and have always purchased crop pants to wear them as regular length jeans. The crop on this GRAPENT fits me like ankle jeans, not full length. I am 5 ft 2 inches and about 115-120 pounds and the medium was a bit large on me. I would need a belt but I think I would still be able to wear it. The small would have definitely fit better. The jeans are stretchy and soft, which I love. The jeans are wide legged, but not overly wide. I would categorize it more like jeans and not trouser jeans. I have worn the jeans with casual tennis shoes and they are adorable.

Perfect Ankle-Skimming Fit & Exceptional Quality! (5'2" approved!) I am absolutely thrilled with these Grapent Cropped Jeans in size Medium. They are an awesome pair of stretch jeans and the overall construction is fantastic. Quality & Construction: The material is comfortably thick, giving them a high-quality feel without being stiff. I inspected the seams closely and found them tight and the stitching impeccable—zero loose threads anywhere. It's clear these are built to last. Fit & Comfort: The stretch element is exactly right. They hug my butt and hips beautifully, providing a flattering silhouette while the elastane gives enough give to make them incredibly comfortable for all-day wear. They truly move with you. Color & Length (A Note for Petites!): The color is spot-on, exactly matching the product photos. Although these are labeled as "cropped," at 5'2", they hit me right at my ankle. I actually love this length! It’s the perfect hemline to let all my favorite ankle boots and dress shoes really shine without having to cuff or tailor them. If you're looking for high-quality, comfortable, and flattering jeans, especially if you are on the shorter side, look no further. Highly recommend! About me: 5’4 in 125 lbs 29 in waist 39 in hips 27 in inseam

10/10 RECOMMEND!! I’ve been reading the hype about democracy jeans for a little while, so naturally, I had to try for myself. AND I AM SO GLAD I DID! They far exceeded my expectations. Arrived smelling perfect (I read several reviews of them smelling chemical like, but was not the case for me). The fit is amazing, as well as the stretch. I would’ve liked to have sized down to a 0, but that size was not an option, so I went with a size 2 which is around my normal size depending on brand. The length is one of the best length jeans I’ve ever owned. I’m 4’11” 115-120 pounds, so it’s difficult to find jeans short enough, these win by a long shot! ( Adding pictures for reference, just excuse the dusty mirror and packages everywhere! Today was a heavy Amazon delivery day 😂)

Stylish, Accurate Sizing, Great Price UPDATE: After wearing all day, they stretched out even more. Now I plan on drying in dryer. Hoping the length doesn’t shrink. If I had ordered a Medium (even with them stretching out) I think they would be too tight in the waist. NOTE: I carry my weight in my stomach. My hips and thigh area are straight. Love Love these! Ordered a Size Large. For reference I’m 5’ 7 1/2” and 169 lbs. Put them on today for “Jean Friday” work day. They fit a little snug in the waist this morning but after wearing for about 2 hours they stretched out (in waist area). Speaking of the waist - it’s perfect! High wide waist, 3 metal buttons plus a zipper. The length is perfect with my UGG Boots. For Spring they’d work with flats or sandals (I live in SC). I plan to wash in cold water, fluff up a bit in dryer on low and then air dry. Might need to iron after that but not sure. FYI - I’ll be 70 in a few weeks! Still want to be stylish 😊.

I’m 5’4”, about 150lbs, pear shaped and these are flattering on my shape. I got the size 10 and they are true to size. I love that they actually fit my waist as most jeans leave a gap. Please note that these might be a bit short if you’re taller than 5’4” as they are just right on me wearing a flat shoe. Another thing to note is they are a smidge short in the crotch in that the middle seam fits snuggly/high. (You may have to adjust them out your backside). However this makes them look better on my bum as I don’t have much going on in that area. I would definitely buy them again!

Buy a size down… The waist section runs a bit large Good material, not that stretchy, not that dick. But they are comfortable. They’re very flattering to the curves. Especially the posterior curves. So if you’ve got a little bit in the trunk, and you’ve got a nice round shape to it, and you’ve got some round curvy hips, these are the jeans for you. But I would caution you to check their sizes, and maybe be considering buying a size down as these ran a little bit big. If you are short, you’ll need to hem the bottom of these jeans as they’re a little bit long, but if you’re in the tall range like a 5’7 Ish, These jeans would fit perfect on you. The print is nice, but it’s not as pronounced as it looks in the photos. Overall, this is a good jean. If I could, I would get 4 1/2 stars.

Great for taller full figure women- heavier like work jeans I think I am more of a full figure- I have a butt and hips with a smaller waist. I bought size 18- my normal size these fit perfectly. The denim is more like 90s denim but with some stretch and they are high waisted with a boot cut/flare. They give Y2K vibes. If you are full figured and like high waist boot cut these are for you! I own a farm and really struggle to find a denim that I can work in and still be comfortable- these are perfect. Thicker denim offers more protection, these are comfy for riding or working. They also look nice enough to wear out. They are a great multi use jeans. These are made for taller women, I am 5’7” so not to tall but if I am not in my work boots they drag, I have a 31” inseam ( longer legs with a short torso) if you are taller with hips/ butt these are for you. I can see why shorter women do not like them, or women with small butts find them uncomfortable. If you have a pan fold and a butt these are great- order your true size, the stretch does loosen after a few hours of wearing so if you are in between sizes order down, if you are a smaller figure you will also probably want to order down a size. These are the best jeans I have found for my current size in a long time. My new favorite.

Comfortable, Stylish Wide-Leg Jeans with a Flattering Fit I ordered these wide-leg denim jeans in blue, size 10, after checking the size chart. I usually fall between sizes 8–10, and with my waist around 30–31 inches, the size 10 fits perfectly. The fit feels true to size and very comfortable. I’m 5'2", and the length is slightly long for my height, but it’s not an issue at all when worn with shoes. From the product photos, it seems the longer length is intentional, and it actually adds to the overall look. The fabric has a nice amount of stretch—soft to the touch yet structured enough to hold its shape. The straight-leg, wide-cut style is very on-trend right now, and I love how effortlessly stylish it looks. The blue wash is classic and easy to style, and the cross-front pocket detail adds a unique design element that makes these jeans stand out. Although they’re designed as high-waisted jeans, I prefer wearing them slightly lower at my natural waist, which worked great for me and felt more flattering. Even after sitting for long hours at work, the jeans stayed comfortable with no tightness or irritation. The natural creasing that forms with wear actually adds to that relaxed denim look. These jeans are very versatile and easy to dress up or down. I’ve worn them to work and can just as easily imagine pairing them with sneakers for a casual day or heels for a more polished outfit. Overall, they’re stylish, comfortable, and well-made—perfect for everyday wear, office days, or casual outings. I’m really happy with this purchase and would definitely recommend them!

Overall, these jeans are comfortable, very good quality and they look good.

Utterly gorgeous- as both cropped & capris! About as close to perfection as possible (especially given my wonky body shape)! I’m 5’3”-5’4” (right smack on the line between regular and petite). My weight fluctuates a lot, but currently around 175-185. As pants are concerned, I’m middle heavy, but with a hourglass shape overall (technically speaking). However, my legs are disproportionately thin. This all obviously makes pants shopping really difficult. Stretchiness is my friend! After exchanging a size 15, I landed on a size 13, two sizes smaller than the size 17 as recommended by Amazon, and one size smaller than the 15 I’d normally wear in juniors’ sizes, (12-14 in ladies) so when you read they run big, believe it. To my delight, the 13 fits as well as I can expect (they’re a bit more snug in the waist than I’d prefer, but I’m on the heavier side of my ever-fluctuating weight, so it won’t be long before they’re perfect- and until then they do a good job of keeping my belly under control). Length-wise, in the full length Wallflower “luscious curvy” fit flare jeans, I have to get short length, or have them hemmed, as they’re way too long on me. But the great thing about these are that they are the perfect length w/one cuff (as worn by the models) to wear as cropped-style, and with a couple cuffs become super cute capris! But the crowning jewel (yes, a shameless pun) has to be the rhinestone embellishments! I will bedazzle ANYTHING, but you don’t have to be obsessed with sparkles to appreciate these pants. They’re beautiful, but not over the top. You can dress them up for a night out, or down for a casual summer barbecue. Lastly, I’m on a really tight budget, so I had two concerns: first being which style to buy (bc I’d have bought them all if I could)- after much thought and difficulty finding a similar design to ‘Clarke’ on any full length styles (as I could the others) I decided on them. Second, I saw they were available for just $15 as “Used: Like New” with the description of ‘Condition consistent with having been tried on but not worn.’” Given that they were being sold & shipped by Amazon, I had enough faith to give them a try and return them if necessary but they’re indistinguishable from being brand new! They were already a great deal at full price, but if you see your size in the “tried on” condition, go for it- I sure am glad I did! -Colleen Jane

Tall girl approved I am 5'9 and got the size 2. The legs end right above my ankle (that's good considering pants usually hit mid to low calf on me). They are relatively stretchy but still hold their size, they don't fall during the day and I haven't noticed the holes distressing/ripping too much on their own.

Another solid pair of Levi’s, runs small though I like these, they’re a good pair of Levi’s, what else can I say? They’re definitely cropped (obviously) and on me they’re verging on capri pants (I’m 5’9” though). I don’t mind this though, they’re nice for summer and I guarantee capri pants will officially be back in at some point. Lol That said, I do think these run small overall. I’m usually a 29 or 30 in Levi’s. If the jeans have any stretch in them then I will wear a 29, but for the non-stretch 100% cotton 501s, I wear a 30. Other reviewers have also said that these run small so I ordered a 30 even though these have 1% elastane. I’m glad I did because they definitely fit more snug around the waist than my other size 30 Levi’s 501s that I have. They’re also snug around my legs, especially for being 501s. They make my booty look good though, lol. I definitely recommend to size up on these. Edit: after wearing them for a few hours, they have loosened up a little bit.

Get a little tummy control in trendy, soft, lightweight stretch denim When I first opened these, I thought "Oh no, how did we get the size THAT wrong?" I double and triple checked everything, and yes, they are the size they're supposed to be. Well, they stretch, so try them on. I was skeptical as I pulled them up all the way and . . they packed in a lot of my overweight, slimming me, and ever so comfortably. Sold. But so long! It's the trend now, to have your pants puddle around your feet. I'll probably hem them up a bit but in the meantime, they look great with my two-inch heel mules. They come out of the wash beautifully. They're much lighter than regular denim, but they're still substantial. And they're soft. Back to the size; I ordered Large based on Amazon's recommendation and didn't look at the size chart, being in a hurry. As it turned out, the Large really is a bit small, though I wear it comfortably. I'm 5/5" and 160 pounds. Skip the size chart link at the top and scroll down to the seller's size chart on the listing. I'll probably get at least one more pair of these in another color.

I’ve recently lost weight and have been wearing 10/12 or M/L in jeans/pants. I’m not that curvy on the bottom. The SIZE 12 are way too big. These jeans have a lot of stretch in the waist and hips. I am able to fit both my arms down either side (see photos). So, I did what anyone else would do…immediately order a Size 10 and a Size 8 to verify which fits better. The size 10 fits better overall but after wearing them for a couple of minutes, I found that they stretched out too much giving me that unwanted jean pooch. So, believe it or not, the size 8 fits great and I have room to breathe, squat, bend, not tight! I love the way these jeans look. I am wearing size 12 in photos. CHECK MEASUREMENTS – VERY STRETCHY WAIST/HIPS. Would recommend.

My Mom Couldn't Be More Pleased At the time of review, the price was: $49.99 which seems a bit high for these. My mom loves these. She's 5' 0" and not only did they fit exactly as expected, since she's shorter they look like flared jeans and work really well. She wears them all the time now. They are comfortable for moving around and getting stuff done and the cut is very stylish. They aren't very stretchy so you'll want to size them well. The color is very close to the listing picture. The stitching looks great. They've already been through the wash a few times and they are holding up super well (as most jeans do).

More Mid-Rise than Low-Rise, but Great Overall I like these jeans a lot. They feel great, with the perfect amount of stretch. They aren’t quite as low rise as I’d expected though, falling about an inch above my hip bones, but otherwise are great. They’re not too baggy and not too long. I’m 5’3 and in flat shoes they drag a tag but would probably be fine wearing a bigger boot. They fit true to size.

Thinner and stretchy type of denim I wasn’t sure what to expect when I ordered these Elleven Women’s Low Rise Baggy Jeans in the Blue Denim color seeing as the seller’s title claimed these jeans were both wide leg and straight leg. Best way I can describe these jeans is that they are wider than a typical straight leg, but definitely not as wide as typical wide leg jeans. I’m normally a US size large so I ordered these in a size 10 to try. The jeans hug my waist and booty perfectly and then have a wider straight leg all the way to the floor. I’m on the shorter side, being only 5ft 3in and these pants do bunch up a tad on the floor, but nothing that rolling the pants up once couldn’t fix. I will say that these are a thinner more stretchy denim and not your typical non-stretch heavy weight denim, making them nice to wear on warmer days. But because they are thinner, I’m not sure how well they’ll keep me warm during the winter here in the Midwest US. In the end I do like these stretchy jeans. When referring to the thickness of the material, these jeans aren’t exactly what I imagine when I think of denim. This thinner type of denim always makes me a little leery of the quality and if they’ll hold up in the long run. But because of how well they fit my frame, I’ll be keeping and wearing them.

Right amount of flare, fitted upper, material not as soft or stretchy as I hoped for. These capris jeans are fitted at the waist and buttocks, with the right amount of flare that flows into a wide leg. I am 5'2" and they fall a few inches above my ankle, so they're more like ankle jeans on me. The fit is comfortable. I am a size 16 and ordered the XL. However, if I were a size 18 I'd recommend sizing up as they are a little tight when pulling up over the hips. They aren't as soft or stretchy as I thought they might be, which is why I didn't size up initially, thinking there would be more stretch. It states not to put them in the dryer, but I'm not sure why. In the rinse cycle, I put a little vinegar to soften them but with an air dry, they're a little stiff. The craftsmanship is ok, but there were a few loose threads on them when they arrived and the white material used for the front pockets feels cheap. Also, I ordered the powder blue but they were closer to medium blue. All in, for this price point, they're not bad. They cute & comfortable and I'll certainly wear them this winter as I live in a warm climate. I do like the wide leg that really does come in with the right amount of flare.

There are two pockets in the back and front. Suitable for beach days or a cruise.

Lee Women's Ultra Lux Comfort Any Wear Straight Leg Pant I originally bought these in the navy in a size 14 (5’6” 200# and an apple that carries most of weight in the belly). The price is excellent at around $16 right now. A solid buy

Lee Women's Ultra Lux Comfort Any Wear Straight Leg Pant First the stats: I am almost 70, 5’4”, waist 27”, hips38”,inseam 29”, 112lbs. AI suggested size 8. I like my waist band a little loose because of a back issue. I probably should have gotten a 6. These are a little bit big; just a bit. I will definitely need to tuck in my shirt to help hold up the pants. I’ll probably use a belt as well. The rise comes even with my navel.They fit like a nice pair of slacks. I will be able to wear these when playing tour guide to visiting friends and I can wear a merino wool base layer underneath without feeling uncomfortable. I live in the Colorado mountains and it can get pretty cold. The length is what I like. The legs reach the top of my shoes. The fabric is very nice. Not thin. Pockets are a decent size and don’t show through the fabric. Rear pockets are fake but I don’t ever use those when pants have real ones. The sewn in crease makes them look dressy. I am satisfied with these pants and will keep them. If I find they are too big after wearing them for a while I will run a little bit of elastic through the waistband. I would recommend these pants if you want to look nice in cold weather.

Lee Women's Ultra Lux Comfort Any Wear Straight Leg Pant Before you look at my photos, keep in mind that I am an overweight (size 16/XL) senior citizen. I think these fit me perfectly. I rarely wear pull-on pants because for some reason they always slip down on me, so adding belt loops to pull-on pants was a genius move on the part of the designer. I am 5’7” tall and usually wear a 31” length, but based on other reviews which mention that they shrink, I ordered the 33” length and I love the way the pants buckle onto my shoes. I will do everything to keep them from shrinking, even if I have to figure out how to hang dry them in the basement. As soon as I tried them on, I sat down and ordered two more pairs !!!

Let me lay out my body negatives so you know just how awesome these wide leg jeans are. I'm 5'3 weighing 180lbs, short legs & short torso. I have a solid athletic build with a firm muffin-top and small fupa. I carry most of my weight in my stomach and breast. I'm never without shapewear on; most times two of them on. I purchased a medium in these, the perfect boot cut denim stretch jeans, and boy are they comfortable. At first when I pulled them from the package, the waist looked really small, esp in comparison to the nice wide leg and ample bottom part. So of course I tried them on without shapewear just to see how much stretch was in the waist part. I was able to button them, but not comfortably. I knew they would be perfect with my shapewear on. I ordered the 'wased black'; I love the faded look. The stretch on these jeans is a nice firm stretch. And because they are not really denim material, they are not heavy like denim, but sturdy enough to still feel like a great pair of jeans. The best part, they got my booty looking great. I absolutely love these jeans, so I plan on getting two more pairs.

Lee Women's Plus Size Ultra Lux Comfort with Flex-to-go Utility Pant I am 5’6 and 170 lbs. these pants are really comfy, the flex band in the top is a game changer. The pockets are nice and real , and the cargo pockets on the side are good for putting your phone or keys in. These are thick and would be good for outside jobs or the winter. I normally wear 14 or 16 . I got these in 16 plus and they are loose on me and I do need a belt but I feel 14 might be too tight. As you can see in the pictures they are the correct length when standing however these ride up SO much when you sit down and they become extreme high waters when sitting. I put them in the dryer and there was no shrinkage. I would like another pair in long.

Update the very next day. I changed my four stars and gave all five stars. I had quite a bit of errands to run today getting in and out of the car, bending, turning etc. The belly area that was too tight with the buttons stretched out perfectly, which is not a lot but just enough and the jeans stayed extremely comfortable and in place the entire day. I never had to readjust or lift them up at all. I literally had them on from 11:00 a.m. this morning and just took them off about 20 minutes ago which is 9:00 p.m.. WITHOUT ANY GIRDLE etc. Extremely comfortable and I will be buying another pair in a different color. I'm very thankful that I decided to go with the size chart recommendation instead of my normal size 16.1st bought review. I really like these alot. I am 5'7" 200lbs with a waist of 43" and stomach of 36". I went with the 14 based on the size chart and other reviews recommending to size up. But, I usually wear a 16 and the size chart suggested 14. They fit perfectly in the legs, thighs and butt. Nice and snug exactly where I want it. They are a bit tight in the belly, though. (I have to suck the stomach in to button up.) They do also give a bit of a camel-toe image but not too uncomfortably and I always wear longer shirts anyway so it doesn't stand out. If I had gone up a size, to my normal 16, they would've been too big around the legs, thigh and butt so I will try wearing a girdle to hold in the belly. (Losing weight is not that easy for me ... no matter what I try) The length is spot on and perfectly tight at the ankle. The material is seriously comfortably soft. The pockets are as they should be on any jeans. When I do wash them, I will use the 'light' wash mode and on cold. Hopefully, they won't shrink. If they do, no girdle will help me button up. I have many errands to run tomorrow so I plan on giving these jeans a test run. I will revise my review (with photos) after that.

If I had gone up a size, to my normal 16, they would've been too big around the legs, thigh and butt so I will try wearing a girdle to hold in the belly. (Losing weight is not that easy for me ... no matter what I try) The length is spot on and perfectly tight at the ankle. The material is seriously comfortably soft. The pockets are as they should be on any jeans. When I do wash them, I will use the 'light' wash mode and on cold. Hopefully, they won't shrink. If they do, no girdle will help me button up. I have many errands to run tomorrow so I plan on giving these jeans a test run. I will revise my review (with photos) after that.

I really loved the quality of this Women's Denim Dress—it is thick and heavy, which gives it a substantial feel. The dress is described as a babydoll fit, but I found it to have a more awkward A-line shape. There is a photo where the model pulls the dress to the sides to show its shape, but unfortunately, the dress retained a "Christmas tree" silhouette on me. I was able to use a pin on the inside to adjust the shape somewhat. I ordered my typical medium size (I am 5'4", 135 lbs, 36 band, square shape). In hindsight, I probably should have sized down to a small, but I was concerned the shoulders and chest area would be too tight. The fringe detail by the buttons tends to stand up awkwardly, which detracts a bit from the overall look. A few care notes: be sure to wash the dress separately and even twice, as a lot of color bleeds out in the wash. Unfortunately, there is a noticeable smell to the dress that does not come out even after three washes. It’s not unpleasant or sour, but definitely noticeable and a bit off-putting. Overall, while the dress is well-made and substantial, the fit and lingering smell make me less likely to reach for it often.

This denim dress is such impressive quality and truly looks expensive! It’s soft, lightweight, and surprisingly breathable — I expected it to be heavy since it’s denim, but the 82% cotton and 18% polyester blend makes it light without feeling thin. It’s sturdy enough to wear with boots in the fall and winter, and the sleeves are absolutely lovely. The A-line shape is super flattering and doesn’t draw attention to the midsection, which I really appreciate. I’m 5’6” and it hits right above the knees. Overall, such a beautiful, comfortable, and versatile dress! I’ve already gotten compliments on it! True to size!

Lee Women's Petite Wrinkle Free Relaxed Fit Straight Leg Pant I am satisfied with my purchase. I am 4'11 & 143lbs and it's so hard to find clothes that look decent on me. I got these in size 6 petite size and they fit perfectly. Just know they do not stretch at all.

user_comment: I wasn't expecting top quality for the price, but these were so awful in so many ways that I don't think they're worth $1. The material feels very cheap and there isn't much stretch to the fabric. There's also these very odd puckers of extra fabric on the outside of the hips and on the inseam in the crotch area, which looks ridiculous. I'm 5'4", 140lbs and got the large size capris because I wanted a looser fit, but these were slightly snug in the waist and loose around the thighs, especially with that extra pucker of fabric. Also, these were not capri length on me. I have short legs and a long torso and, as you can see, they go down almost to my ankles. I'd have to roll them up many times to get the length I want, but the fabric isn't stretchy enough and is too snug around the lower legs to do this. Finally, there are back pockets, but the front pockets are fake. And be mindful of the tag that warns about bleeding dye even after washing. Given all of these faults, I wouldn't recommend these to anyone. There's really nothing positive to say about them. Don't waste your money.

These jeans are 75% cotton and the rest stretchy, so they're almost as comfortable as yoga pants. The decorative stitching on the side seams gives them some pizzazz. My husband immediately noticed that they were new jeans and said they looked great (he doesn't usually notice new clothes!) The zipper is a lighter fast-fashion-quality zipper, I do wish it were a little stronger, but otherwise I'm delighted with these. I wore them all day comfortably without once thinking "I can't wait to get out of these jeans and into some yoga pants when I get home". I doubt that anyone would look at the style of these and think "I'll get those for baling hay", but just in case ... these are definitely town jeans, they're not barn jeans. The fabric is a lighter weight and they aren't sturdy/protective/suited for barn or field work. They're a little longer than my usual jeans too, which works great worn with heels. I'm 5'5" and wear a size 16.

LOVE THESE JEANS. I have been searching for a black pair of jeans with no holes in them that fit my body and the style that I want. These jeans are super stretchy and are long enough for me since I am tall(5’7). They are very comfortable and are worth the money!

Love the cut and length. I’m size 4 with a mommy pouch, size S definitely fits size 4-6, waist has a lot of stretch, I can put my whole hand in waist and still have room. For length reference I’m 5’6”.

I ordered these jeans because I like the way women's jeans fit so much nicer than mens on me. I'm 6'/173 and have a athletic body, so I ordered a medium, which fit perfectly! The length is perfect for tall people and the stretch in the legs and waist, is enough so they don't constrict, but they also don't move up and down as you move around. The material is light weight and thin, yet feel like reshape jeans material when wearing them. The butt area is very plain with no designs or embellishments, but has two tiny pockets for a small phone/etc. Overall, a very nice pair of skinny jeans for men. They fit well and look really nice, but the price is way too high in my opinion. There are many other name brand jeans at almost half the cost.

I'm a fan of Roswear denim! I like their shorts and I recently ordered and love a denim jacket! I have been wearing thei shorts since early Spring of this year and I own enough of them to wear them on a fairly consistent basis throughout the warmer months (okay... I wear them EVERY day unless I need to dress better for some reason). So when I saw that they made skinny jeans, I was THRILLED! And now that I've received them, I still am! First, let me explain the fit you see in my photos... The looseness of them is by design. I am a VERY typical size Small. At 5'6" and fluctuating between 118 to 124 pounds, I can manipulate the fit of clothes from a pair of pants that are meant to be deliberately tight to look semi relaxed on me by simply ordering up to a Medium. and that's exactly what I've done here on purpose. I had this idea that if I order skinny jeans in a medium, and being knowledgeable of the brand, I might end up with a gene that has a style I really enjoy, but a fit that I prefer. I think it worked in this case! The only drawback is that the fit between the waist and the bottom of the crotch is a bit longer than I would like, but as I will likely be wearing longer sweaters, I can really just pull my pants up a little higher and no one will notice. 😊 However, if you like what you see in the brand's ad, you should order your typical size and I can tell you with a high degree of certainty they will fit exactly as described. And they will look amazing like that as well. I just have enough super-skinny jeans AND wider-legged baggy jeans, and wanted something sort of in between. "A skinny cut with a relaxed fit." If you'd like something more classic like that, order a size up and you shall have it! Rest assured, Roswear denim high quality! I love the texture, the weight, and the amount of stretch offered. Their denim is super classic looking and updated enough for the latest styles. While I cannot bring myself to subtract a star for this because it's very common these days, there is a slight chemical odor when receiving new jeans from this company. It is the typical chemical odor you get with jeans that are imported. I think it has something to do with the packaging and, it's not super strong. And after one or two washes it's gone anyway. But folks need to be aware that there is an odor and if that bothers you you should not order these. It just doesn't bother me. these jeans are worth it because it's too hard to find a decent fit and style in jeans online to worry about something so minor that is gone after a few hours or washes anyway. So there you have it! I am so pleased that this company decided to produce these skinny jeans! I will continue to like them a little more relaxed for my own taste, but I'm sure they look fantastic as "tight" skinny jeans as well! Please just remember that I ordered a size up on purpose and that you do not need to size down. Order your typical size. Well done, Roswear!!

Roswear Skinny Jeans Mid Rise Ripped Slim Fit Distressed Jeans, COLOR Blue, SIZE Large: I’m 5’2”, 174 lbs and normally wear a large or 10/12. The large fits but is a tad big because these stretch. I can pull these on and off without unbuttoning or zipping. I’m hoping they shrink a little. I like the color and quality. The material is a little thick, like good old-fashioned jeans used to be. The pockets are amazing because they are real pockets! These jeans look great with high knee boots. The quality is very good. Good value! Would recommend.

I have these pants in black and blue and I love them! They are so comfortable and you dress them up or down. I love them so much I decided to order them in gray also. For some odd reason, the exact same pair of pants in a different color are significantly longer than my other 2 pair. To the point that I am tripping on them when not wearing shoes. When I am wearing shoes they still touch the ground. I am 5'5" and the other 2 pair I have are the perfect length. I lost the return window by a day so I'm stuck with them. Now I'm worried that if I buy another pair they will also be too long. If you are a little taller than me, they may be the perfect length. It's just odd that I have 3 pairs in the same size and these are longer than the other 2. ** Update** I liked the color enough that I gave the first gray pair I bought to my daughter, who is 5" taller than me and took my chances and ordered another pair. (The length was perfect for her and she 5'10".) The second pair was also too long, so it's clearly that color.

These fit perfectly for length and fit. 5’6, 125 lbs, long torso and these were just as pictured. The length was good for the spring/fall. The pockets in the front are flattering. They run a little big because I normally am a size 2, but got a 4 in these. They aren’t very thick and are a little stretchy, but that’s fine. It’s very hard to find the right length and rise, but these fit the bill.

I ordered these jeans because I've been looking for a nice pair of wide leg jeans. I was extremely pleasantly surprised when I put them on and they fit like they were made for me. They were a bit long, but with a little taller shoe, they were the perfect length for me (note - I'm 5'2", so most jeans need hemmed even with taller shoes). They have just enough stretch to be comfortable, but don't look stretched out at the end of a long day of wearing them. I will definitely buy these jeans again.

Sometimes I like to take a fashion risk, and these cropped, ruffled jeans are that in my book. Firstly, they are a nice, lightweight denim- not so thin they feel like jammie pants, but good for warmer to hot weather. The material does not have any stretch to it, but it's not stiff. They were a little tighter than the size chart would have indicated, and to get the very loose, boho look, I would order up a size. They are accurately represented in the photos in the listing, although the grey is a very dark grey that looks like faded black. There is an odd seam below the knee, again, it's a style choice, but I didn't notice it until I got the pants. I'm 5'2", and they hit me just above my ankle, not all that cropped for a petite but I'm used to that. I just can't decide if they are cool or ridiculous...maybe it will depend on how I'm feeling. I think these would be really cute with a slouchy tank and flip-flops or sandals in the summer.

I think this top is super cute layered on top of another shirt. I wouldn't wear it by itself because it seems more cropped on me than it does in the photos. My worst fear about cotton is it shrinking, and I do think it did, so I would size up to account for that. I'm 5'3" and got an XS. At first the sleeves covered most of my hand but after going through the wash land at my wrist. Photo with purple shirt is before wash and salmon shirt is after wash. The SXIDECGQI Women's Baggy Mid Low Waist Jeans Wide Straight Leg Frayed Raw Hem Loose Boyfriends Denim Jeans are perfection! The wide straight leg is flattering and elongates the legs. They sat pretty high up on me. Their only saving grace is the bagginess of the leg. Thankfully these are baggy in the thigh area and down so I was able to move in them but they are definitely tight in the booty area. These are nice pants for women who are not curvy. Overall, they are very well made and the jean quality is great but they are not for fuller figured women.

user_comment: I was a teen when baggy jeans first became popular so I was pretty psyched to try these out. They are okay. I am a true medium and this medium seems to be the right fit for me (5’1/149 lbs). They are very long on me..to be fair, I am on the short side, so that’s not a huge surprise…so I definitely can’t wear these without some type of heeled shoe. I included pics with and without heeled shoes to show the difference. The length doesn't bother me as much as the way the jeans fit under my backside. It feels like it was purposely stitched to give me a permanent wedgie. I believe it is meant to pull the booty up for the look, but I just can’t. It really stays like that no matter how much I try to adjust it out! I like that they designed the back of the jeans to be stretchy at the waist, but it kind of looks unattractive to me if i wear it with a shirt tucked in. The material of the jeans is thick and seemingly well-made, so I definitely believe they will last. Priced right around 30 bucks is very reasonable, especially for jeans. They may be a better fit/look for a different body type, I just don’t think it’s quite right for me.
reviewer_name_raw: Not the most flattering jeans—permanent wedgie

I’m reviewing 2 different pair. The first is the color “Reef Blue” in a size XL. I am typically a size 12-14 in jeans. I love that they’re high waisted, on the thinner side but not too thin to where you can see lumps and bumps. I am very hippy with saddle bags and currently sporting the menopausal gut. I read that these jeans are stretchy and I considered buying them in a Large but knowing my hip/butt/saddle bags I would’ve ran the risk of pulling at the pockets. I assume had I bought the Large, that pulling would’ve lessened due to the stretch of the material so I may order a second pair in a different color and in a large. I’ve read that they’re a heavy Jean and they are not. Unless other colors are made with different materials. These pants are great! If I didn’t have the wider butt I would’ve (and possibly still could) wear the size large. The color is a faded like color blue with a cream stitching. These color jeans look very different in each picture I took due to lighting that’s why I included the close up of the ankle area, it’s the true color. I hope this helps! I’m 180 and 5’6ish.The 2nd pair is “medium blue” in a size large. They were snug on me and I considered keeping them as I know they’ll stretch out throughout the day however, I did not like the dark color with the lighter stitching. The stitching stood out too much for my liking. I didn’t want each pocket or zipper seam to stand out, if that makes sense. Also, they were clearly purchased before me and had been washed but not fully dried. They were still damp when I took them out of the bag. Do better amazon. So now after purchasing two pair I will keep the original XL and return the wet size large.

These wide-leg jeans totally surprised me! The high waist is super flattering, and the stretchy denim makes them so comfortable to wear all day. AUTOMET Women Summer Tops Spring Fashion 2026 Lace Long Sleeve Shirts Fall Sweaters Dressy Casual Tunic Blouse Work Clothing This review is for the AUTOMET long-sleeve top in size Large, color “Apricot.” The material is soft, stretchy and the overall style could be very flattering on certain body types, but it didn’t work well for me. The fabric is extremely thin and quite see-through (as shown in the photos), and it really emphasizes areas I prefer to keep smoothed out, especially my love handles and “muffin tops”. It does fit true to size, and the sleeves are beautiful, but the open-knit areas make my bra straps very visible, which I didn’t love. For reference, I’m 5’5”, 175 lbs, and wear a 36D. This top would probably work best for someone who doesn’t need extra coverage or prefers a lightweight, sheer look.

These jeans are great! I have never tried the more stylish "wide leg" jeans, but I think these are actually excellent for my build and shape. I will say that I am normally a size 2, and I ordered a 4 based on the size chart. They were a tad big on the waist, and the denim has some stretch to it. The tag says not to put them in the dryer - not happening! So I washed them and put them in the dryer, and now they fit even better more like the size I needed to begin with. Since the waist is really high, none of my belts were small enough to fit, but I do like the high waist style. The pockets are HUGE and actually hold my phone in front or in back which is fantastic for women's jeans. They drape nicely and are not too stiff for the material. I like the contrast stitching and the dark color. The photos are before I washed and dried them. Now they might be slightly shorter but still a great length that for me at 129 lbs and 5'7" with curvy frame fit great. I do not have to wear heels and wore these with both my flats and sandals. I got a lot of compliments on them.
reviewer_name_raw: Great dressy jeans, check your sizing.

user_comment: I'm a millennial and thought I'd NEVER touch bell bottom jeans again; middle school fashion was cringy enough! But I decided to try these because I need a new pair of jeans. I'm a bit wowed by how good they look and their high quality! They are very comfortable and look really good with high heels or boots. The "Bell" on them isn't as exaggerated as it is in the demo pictures. These seem to be more like a slightly roomier boot cut jeans than true bell bottoms. They are true to size, which is fantastic. I'm usually a size 4-6 and ordered a medium. My only complaint is how high the jeans go (my belly button) and that they still drag a bit on the ground even when I'm wearing kitten heels (I'm 5'5") -- but I know high waisted jeans with long long pants legs is currently the trend. Also the crotch bunches up a bit strangely at times, but I don't think that would be noticable to anyone but myself. In all the fabric quality is really good and these are COMFY. I think they would pair really well with just about any kind of boot!

Belle Poque Women Straight Leg Jeans Stretchy Mid Rise Jeans with Pockets These jeans are similar to two pairs I bought last year at Loft, but I would argue that they are both more comfortable and a better value as I paid half as much for these. I have gained some Winter weight, so based off that and the size chart I went with a size 8 instead of my usual 6. The size 8 fit well. I am barely 5'3" about 150 lbs currently and half a wider backside, hips and thighs. I actually usually prefer my jeans with slightly less stretch to provide a little shaping, but these aren't so stretchy that they have no form at all. They are okay since they are extremely comfortable. The material is more on the thin side, so I likely won't wear them until it warms up in the Spring. They are ankle jeans for an average height person, but as usual for myself, they are too long for ankle jeans and too short for full-length. I feel like they look best on me with sandals or a shoe that shows a little more skin to lengthen my legs some. I will have to hem them if I want to wear them with sneakers, but I probably won't. I haven't worn them consistently or washed them yet, so I can't say how they will hold up, but I feel like they are decent quality for the price. I love that the waist is high and more narrow than the hips and butt so they don't slide down and I only have to wear a belt if I choose to add accessories. I would recommend for those like myself who usually prefer a curvy fit jean. They also seem like they would be forgiving if I were to gain or lose weight. I am losing at the moment so I'll update the review if I'm wrong. If I didn't have to hem them to get the ankle length I would definitely buy another pair in a different color. Overall I believe these jeans are a decent value for the price. Some of my pictures won't load, so I apologize. Hopefully what is there is enough to give some idea of how they appear on me.

Belle Poque Women Straight Leg Jeans Stretchy Mid Rise Jeans with Pockets As a person that can't wear tight things around my waist and being plus size it's hard to find pants that fit comfortably. When I got these and saw them I was unsure they would be a good fit for me, after putting them on I was so happy. they fit me perfectly. Comfortable light summer shorts! I am 5'7, 162 lbs with a curvy figure. I ordered a medium and they fit well. I like how light and airy they are. I LOVE Luvamia and own at least 20 different items from them. i was so excited to get these retro daisy jeans in my usual Luvamia size (large on my 5’5” 175# body) but so disappointed that they really don’t fit. This is so surprising because I find Luvamia to be very consistent. I was able to at least get them in for photos and to test the comfort and style. I really do love them and can’t wait til I lose another 10 - 15 pounds so I can wear them. Luvamia generally has stretch in their items so even if they are a bit tight first outing then on, they do get better fitting after about an hour. But these are still too small. Outside of that, they are well made and adorable. Especially for the price, I recommend them, just be sure to size up at least once and maybe twice if you’re between sizes.

I ordered the Luvamia Straight Leg Jeans for Women in Floral Print 3-Medium Wash, size Small. I am 5’8, 140 lbs., for reference. These jeans are so cute!! . I have about 15 items of clothing from Luvamia, and I am very impressed with the quality—they are my favorite denim brand sold by Amazon. The denim has a moderate amount of stretch—enough to allow easy movement, but not enough to make them get baggy as the day goes on. They fit at my natural waist. I do think these run a bit smaller in the waist than other Luvamia jeans/shorts I have. They have a true straight leg fit, so the jeans are not tight anywhere on the leg. They hit about ankle length. I do wish they were a little longer, but that is just a personal preference. The pattern is so cute, and it looks just like the listing pictures. I have washed and dried these jeans without any fading or shrinking. I style them with just a simple white shirt and will add a denim jacket once the weather gets cooler. I know I will get a lot of wear from these jeans. With the current price of $29.99, these jeans are a great deal! I highly recommend these!

I'm 4'11 and weigh 141 pounds, I got a size Medium, dark blue. I do love these pants, however, I did knock off a star because the shortest they come is 29 inseam, I had to take 2 inches off the bottom. Also, the waist is very tight if you have a menopause belly like me, they are a bit uncomfortable. And lastly, they have that slight mothball smell, after washing it somewhat went away. Other than taking these two things into account (if you're built like me), I would recommend them.

Levi's Women's 94 Baggy Wide Leg Jean (Also Available in Plus) These are close to perfect. The only thing is they are a bit short so you can only wear a kitten heel or flats with them. Stilettos would not look good. But I’m 5’6, so if you’re shorter, you’re all good! The fit is amazing. I got a 25 and they are a little baggy but I love that while they still hug your hips. I have jeans like this that cost $250 and aren’t as good. Levi’s for life. These do not disappoint. I’m typically a size 24 or 25 depending on stretch. These have NO stretch. There are a ton of reviews about sizing down but the I’m glad I didn’t. And I weigh 113 pounds. Maybe I got a color where the fit is perfect but I wouldn’t recommend going down two sizes like people are saying. Again- zero stretch. These are soft and comfy light denim. UPDATE - I ordered another pair in a dark wash and it’s like I’m wearing weird super wide leg uncomfy jeans with zero movement and the sizing is wildly different. The first pair I got was a light wash. Super soft and comfy. I got another pair in a much darker wash. They are twice as thick. Twice as uncomfortable and are tighter. Just be careful as this is true denim- no stretch whatsoever. Can’t wear this darker color. Feels like I’m wearing cardboard. Truly. Maybe they will get “worn” if I wear them more. Not sure yet. Update three- literally doing this for all my ladies. And men that would like this cut. I ordered a third pair in the light wash I first got. I am really small but do have hips. I got a 26 because I wanted a the super soft light wash and supper low hip hugging baggy. And sadly they are the exact same size (I measured) as the 25. Exact. In every dimension. One pair says 25. One says 26. If I put them on there’s zero difference. I actually think the 25 is bigger than the 26. The 26 is not longer. Not looser. And I’m wondering at this point if they are real Levi’s. I have about 10 pairs of Levi’s all different cuts. I love these ones so much but was confused that there was zero difference in the 25 vs 26. I will wear them both. But just be mindful of washes and thickness and length. This review had originally four stars. I can’t give it that anymore. The price is Incredible as they look like wide leg baggies I’ve spent over $200 on. But you truly can’t be taller than 5’6. I feel like I don’t even love where they hit at the ankle and I’m 5’6. One more inch on the cut and I could have worn them with heels. Be prepared for length, feel of denim, and sizing.Look at the photos. Side profile is a 25, front is a 26. Exact same wash. Exact same jean. They both hit the same at my foot. So strange they are so short. (I didn’t order short- I promise you all I know what I’m doing) if you have a very small waist but hips - get a size larger than normal for you.

Levi's Women's 94 Baggy Wide Leg Jean (Also Available in Plus) great baggy jeans alternative if you’re new to the style. i’m 5’0, 120 lbs, and it fits amazingly. i will say to size up on the waist, just because it can become a little constricting if too small (duhh…) but overall, great fit. a little long on me, but that’s kinda what i’d expect being so short. the jean quality is great, and the fading over time with 100+ wears is really nice. these are always on sale, too, so it’s a good choice. i own multiple pairs, lol.

Levi's Women's 94 Baggy Wide Leg Jean (Also Available in Plus) These jeans make me so happy and excited! They remind me of my old favorite pairs from my high school days in the 90s! Very 'skater-girl/boyfriend jean vibes'. I recently lost over 100 lbs in the last year and these are officially my new favorite pants I'll be wearing to show off my new slim figure. I'm 5 ft 7 and currently weigh 144 lb. Originally bought size 30 but were slightly too snug and I was worried about them shrinking in the wash so I exchanged it for a size 31. I've been so used to wearing stretchy type pants for so long, I'm looking forward to wearing real jeans again.

I am so incredibly happy with this product, I had to take pictures and do a review. I don't do this for every purchase (I couldn't possibly) but when I have an experience this positive, I have to acknowledge it so I give credit where credit is due and also provide other shoppers with information to help them with a decision. I bought these pants because I was searching everywhere for pants to match this bright purple blazer I had bought and it is just impossible. I saw these, and I knew it was a gamble, but they showed up in the mail the next day, and it was exactly the same color and hue as it was pictured on the internet. It is a perfect match! Not only that, but the fit is absolutely perfect. I think these are quite possibly my most comfortable pants in my wardrobe now. They are stretchy enough to not need a zipper or button (the ones you see are fake) but the pockets are REAL! All four pockets are nice and deep and functional. The fabric is firm enough so that it doesn't wind up looking like leggings (they look like fancy jeans.) They are formal enough to wear with professional attire. They are the perfect length for me (5'2") and they do not fit too tightly around the waist (a problem I find with way too many pants and skirts. They fit just like a glove. In fact, I was shocked at how perfectly they fit. They were almost out of my price range, but I am very very glad that I sprang for a few extra bucks and I may purchase a few more pairs in different colors now that I see how great a product this is. For your reference, I am 5'2", 122 lbs, 31" waist.

I saw these, and I knew it was a gamble, but they showed up in the mail the next day, and it was exactly the same color and hue as it was pictured on the internet. It is a perfect match! Not only that, but the fit is absolutely perfect. I think these are quite possibly my most comfortable pants in my wardrobe now. They are stretchy enough to not need a zipper or button (the ones you see are fake) but the pockets are REAL! All four pockets are nice and deep and functional. The fabric is firm enough so that it doesn't wind up looking like leggings (they look like fancy jeans.) They are formal enough to wear with professional attire. They are the perfect length for me (5'2") and they do not fit too tightly around the waist (a problem I find with way too many pants and skirts. They fit just like a glove. In fact, I was shocked at how perfectly they fit. They were almost out of my price range, but I am very very glad that I sprang for a few extra bucks and I may purchase a few more pairs in different colors now that I see how great a product this is. For your reference, I am 5'2", 122 lbs, 31" waist.

Vibrant Women's Juniors Bell Bottom High Waist Fitted Denim Jeans For reference, I am 5' 2", 155 lb,s and ordered a size 9 (L). I fluctuate between 150-155, and Amazon suggested XL for me. I'm happy with the 9. Though they are a bit snug, they are not uncomfortable in the slightest. An XL might have been too loose. I was looking for a snug/slim fit. In the photos, I am wearing 5" heels with a 1" platform. I like the fit and feel of these pants. They have a good stretch to them and are very comfortable. They didn't have any smell to them. They have a great flared leg to them, it's very dramatic, but not clown-like. The color is a true white. I haven't washed them yet, so I can't comment on shrinkage. You can see right through them. I am wearing black undergarments, and you can tell. I first tried them with nude undergarments, and I didn't notice the transparency. It was only when I went back with my shapewear for the full try-on that I could tell how bad they are. I have nude shapewear, but if you have colorful undergarments, be aware of the white because you can see right through them. The good thing is that the pockets and zipper blend in nicely. I tried two other brands, and you could really see the outline of the zipper and front pockets.

Vibrant Women's Juniors Bell Bottom High Waist Fitted Denim Jeans BEFORE I BEGIN- I want you to have details on the photos I added, for an accurate view. First photos are without shoes, last ones are with 2" heels. Close ups are for you to see the color change after 1 wash. It may be hard to tell but originally they were that deep beautiful black color. They are still black but much more faded then before. We just moved so don't mind the mess behind me 😛 Now for ACTUAL REVIEW! :-) I do really like these pants! High waisted flare pants can do wonders for your body! There are a few negatives but hopefully I can fix/prevent them. FYI I am 5'7 151lbs 36B 29/30" waist 39/40 hips and 31/32 inseam and I have 16" calves... I am curvy and have thick thighs and thick calves..I normally wear a 6-8 in women's pants/ M in shirts. I purchased a 9, they are 41" in length and have a 34.5" inseam. Here are my PROS AND CONS PROS- I look awesome in them! Perfect amount of stretch without getting saggy( even after a 10 hour shift where I had to bend alot) Love the flare, they sit high so you don't have to worry about butt crack sam. Material is sturdy but not heavy. Can be worn in any weather CONS- Zipper keeps coming down, tried to size up but they were huge on me, could just be my body but it's an easy fix I washed them in cold water on the delicate cycle, air dried them and they already are slightly faded. I do use color safe detergent that is supposed to help fading. I am 5'7 and have a 32" inseam but I cannot wear these without 2 in heels. I hate high waters so I like my pants to be even with my shoes or slightly above them but even with heels/ wedges on they still touch the ground. Perfect for girls 5'8-6 ft. I'm going to use temporary hemming tape when I don't want to wear heels. Sizing is a bit off, I got a 9 but normally I could fit a 7 in juniors. They are a jean material, you don't notice as much in the beginning but after your first wash you will. Now I am still going to buy multiple pairs of these in black and almost every other shade bc it's hard to find flare pants that fit so nicely BUT I will not machine wash these pants again! I will find a detergent for black clothing and hand wash them bc I cannot stand faded black pants, but 2/3rds of black clothing fade, so I don't blame the seller for this is Overall them! Be careful not all shades of these pants are the same style, some have buttons, some have odd light/ bleached lines across the front..but if you pick the ones I got, they'll look just like my picture. I chose the black pair that has the model with a white shirt on and some of her belly showing. They have another pair of black ones, with a different model but I do not like them. Those have bleached lines across the front. Avoid that selection.

Vibrant Women's Juniors Bell Bottom High Waist Fitted Denim Jeans First off, the obligatory relevant measurements so people can get an idea of the fit (I know I was skimming the reviews for this exact reason). My waist is anywhere from 28 to 31 inches depending on my bloat, hips are 38 inches, and 22 inch thighs. I also have square hips (aka, short waist, high hip flare). I bought a size 9 in "Super Dark Denim". Definitely snug on my high bloat days, but there's a good amount of stretch so I can still easily button these up. I very intentionally posted pics at my most puffy so you can see - these jeans got give! The fabric, besides being fantastically stretchy, is also remarkably soft to the touch. Indeed, if you've got on itch, you can effectively scratch through the jeans (I know you know what I mean about scratching an itch in fitted jeans!) I really can't make any sense of the fabric - it's thick and sturdy enough to hold you in and actually be called "jeans", stretchy enough to make you think they're jeggings, and soft enough to wear all day long. And they make my back end look FABULOUS despite the reality of my flat mom butt. By the way, I'm only 5'0", so be warned, fellow short ladies - these pants are LONG. I had to bring them in to get them professionally hemmed like 5 inches, and that's with extra length left to wear in heels! Absolutely worth it.

Vibrant Women's Juniors Bell Bottom High Waist Fitted Denim Jeans Oh my goodness.... I bought these on a 1:00 a.m. on a can’t sleep shopping binge and I woke up the next morning filled with regret. I am a size 12 pant and about a 6-8 top so very pear shaped with big hips, butt, thighs, and a little pooch from having a baby almost 2 years ago. When I opened up the package I knew this could go one of two ways - VERY bad or AMAZING. Thank god it was the latter, I will be ordering these in a couple more washes! I bought the black and sized up to a 13 and they’re perfect, I think an 11 would have hugged the pooch too much. If you have a similar body type I would suggest the black because it definitely hides the little tummy very well whereas I do not think the light wash would! I had my daughter at 21 and have been really struggling to find a good balance between young cute clothes and mom appropriate, these are high waisted enough that almost any size could pair with a crop top or body suit and they’re flattering. I am 5”1 and with 3-4 inch wedges these are the perfect pant length, just barely covering up the top of my shoes. Don’t buy these if you’re short and don’t plan on wearing heels or altering because you will be disappointed!

Vibrant Women's Juniors Bell Bottom High Waist Fitted Denim Jeans In love with these jeans! Definitely going to buy in the lighter color as well. Very 70s with bell bottom style that’s long enough for my 5’8” tall self to even wear heels with them without them being highwater flares. The flare isn’t too ridiculously huge, but just right and they are super flattering as well. VERY high waisted, which I LOVE. Keep in mind they don’t have front pockets, but the stitching makes it look like they do. It does have back pockets. They also aren’t super heavy material so I could wear in the summer with a crop top. I am usually a size 8-10 or 29-30 in women’s pants/jeans and I ordered these in a size 13 in the dark denim. Honestly, I could’ve gotten an 11 for a super duper tight/snatched look but I like breathing and these are tight enough. I’m no longer at the age (32), where I’m willing to sacrifice comfort just for my jeans to be a little tighter. BUY THESE! They are a staple. Can’t wait to get the lighter pair. Great price point too! Well worth it.

Democracy Women's Ab Solution Girlfriend Jean Love these jeans! I’m 5’7 and 164 pds, a little curvy on the hips which, is great about these jeans-VERY comfortable and stretch. I got a size 10 and they fit perfectly as well as the length. Distressed but not too much.

Democracy Women's Ab Solution Girlfriend Jean These jeans are pretty perfect. I am 5’7” and 175 lbs. and short waisted. I usually like a higher rise because bits more forgiving through the crotch area, especially with jeans and their bulky seams, but the rise in these is cut more generously. It’s more comfortable. These are a size 12. I usually wear a 14, and these stretch so definitely go with a size down from your normal. These look so cute with no show socks and sneakers or roll them up to show off a cute pair of flats. There is a smell as mentioned in other reviews, however, it became more noticeable AFTER a wash. It didn’t linger though, and now it’s gone after several hours. Definitely will be buying more if this brand and style!

Democracy Women's Ab Solution Girlfriend Jean I found this brand searching for another brand on Amazon… they have quickly become my favorite! I usually stick to the skinnies (millennial here), but decided to try the girlfriend style. I LOVE them, but I do want to note that they are much longer on my 5’7” with short legs frame. Debating on if I should’ve ordered one size down because they are loose in the waist, but as a girl with muscular thighs, I didn’t want to take from the fitted but looser look. 10s work great on day 1, but we’ll see how much they stretch out on subsequent days. FLYING BANANA Women's High Waisted Butt Lifting Stretch Bootcut Jeans I am 5’2”, 150lbs. I bought a size 10 short. They fit beautifully. Hug the bottom, no gap in the back, fit the thighs nicely. FLYING BANANA Women's High Waisted Butt Lifting Stretch Bootcut Jeans These jeans are really nice and I like them alot. They are well made. I do believe I probably could have went down to a size 10, they are a little big on, but that's on me. Also they washed and dried well. The reason I took off a 🌟 is, I ordered regular size 12 and these are so long. I'm 5'7 and these cover my feet. Even with hiking boots on underneath they drag on the ground. Overall though they are really nice jeans.

I have mixed feelings about these jeans. They come very well packaged, even a tissue covering the button. And they are straight leg as written in the description but I find that they do not fit quite like the picture on the site. They are the same width all through the thighs, knees and ankles which to be fair IS the description of straight leg. However, the picture shows a jean that is slimmer on thigh and opens below the knees. That said, I do not have a figure like the model in the picture and although different, the jeans fit me well. They look good both with trendy white sneakers as well as with heeled boots. I am including pictures both with a T-shirt and a little more dressed up with a black top. The size 6 was good for me (5’ 6” 145lbs) and the length was perfect. They are made of thick jean material and feel comfortable, not heavy and weighty.

These pants are a good value. The tag says the material is a cotton blend with some stretch, and that is how the material feels. It is a thicker denim material, a good quality that I expect will wash and wear well. The stitching looks well done. I like that the washing instructions do not indicate any need for special treatment. I can wash these with the rest of my clothes. As for the fit, these fit true to USA sizing. Buy your usual size. These are a good length for an average height woman. I am just shy of 5’4”, and the hem grazes the floor at my heel when I am not wearing shoes. The waist is on the higher end, falling at the natural waist. The fit does provide light tummy support without being uncomfortable. The lower leg is a slightly wide fit, in line with the current style and the ad image. The one negative with these is that the color is a more drab olive than the ad shows. I would have liked the brighter shade, but it is a decent color that I will wear. All in all, I would recommend these.

Jeans fit great with just a bit of stretch. Nice flair in legs so not tight on calves. Would recommend.FIT: I am 5'5" and 180 lbs and would wear a 12 or 14 depending on the cut. As I recently gained weight I was concerned that the 12 would be too tight, but was pleasantly surprised that they fit. The slight stretch in the waist really helped. For someone who falls in that 10 to 12 range, I would suggest going with the 10. The jeans are slightly long on me, but not long enough that they will touch the ground when I have shoes on.FUNCTIONALITY: Solid jean with just enough stretch to make it comfortable to move in. Stitching was solid with no loose strings. With the olive color it is a nice alternate to your typical blue jeans. Has a wider pant leg (not a boot cut) which allowed me to comfortably wear over ankle boots.FINANCE: In comparing prices, this is priced competitively with other similar products on Amazon.

I received this product for review as part of the Amazon Vine program. This review reflects my personal experience with the product. I selected the Amzdora High Waisted Jeans because I wanted a pair of pants in a fun color to wear this Autumn. Orange is a fun color. These pants are soft, before and after washing. They are very stretchy, so stretchy that I wish I had ordered one size smaller. Pull on pants FTW! The fringe on the bottom of each leg is super cute. user_comment: Hello Amazon family! I have been searching for a pair of white Capri jeans for a long time. I am athletic, 5' 2 at 125 lb and finding a pair of anything is super challenging. These fit in all the correct places! The fabric is super thick and buttery soft. I got a small white and let me tell you you cannot see through them. Included a picture with flash so you can see the quality and thickness of the fabric as well as how well it is. Stitched. Even in white, these are very flattering, especially on a short person like myself. The way that the waistline fits correctly, it does not poop out so I did not even wear a belt. They are form-fitting but not tight so they have great stretchy give. Also, and this is what I think is most important, I wore them from 4:00 in the morning until 8:00 at night on a flight to Las Vegas and they did not stretch out. Out they kept their form beautifully. The fabric is so awesome that by the time I got to the hotel I was expecting them to be a filthy mess and they were not! I have also washed them twice and they wash perfectly. They retained their form and go back to a bright vibrant white! So if you too have been searching endlessly for a pair of white jeans these are for you and they are priced so well. You won't even feel guilty getting a second pair And they are so versatile you could wear them with sandals and a bikini top or tank top, heels and pearls with a fancy blouse, tennis shoes and a t-shirt or a sweatshirt or a sweater! Put your hair in pigtails, flat ironed, classy waves, or a bun. ! Remember, when you feel good on the outside, you make better choices for your health on the inside! Yours in health, Dr. Navarro.👒👑👖👟👠👛👚👙📿🎽👝👜

This pair of Capri jeans are well made of 75% cotton, 23% polyester, and 2% elastane. The fabric is soft and has a good amount of stretch to make them comfortable for all day wearing. The thickness is between medium and thin, so they will be nice for temps in the upper 70's until the humidity arrives. They have nice side slits on the bottom of each leg to step up the style a bit. The button and zipper fasteners work well, without any stiffness or catches. They have 2 regular sized front pockets, the small watch-pocket on the front, and 2 back pockets that are regular sized. I like the Bay Blue color. It's a nice, middle shade of blue with a sort of washed look. They are easy to launder with a cold wash cycle and tumble dry on low heat. I ordered these in a size XL, which is my regular jeans size when going by letter sizes. They fit me nicely without any tightness or pinching around my waist. I think they would have been nicer looking with a slimmer fit in the legs. Due to my shortness they are a bit longer on me than a typical Capri length, but I get that with most all Capri length pants. It's not a fault of the jeans. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips and 182 lbs with an apple shaped midsection. These Capri jeans are good quality and I will recommend them. They are a comfy, relaxed fit. The current price (2-5-2026) of $29.99 is a fair value, but there is also a coupon available at this time, lowering the price to make them an even better value. There are several shades of blue and couple other colors to choose from, as well.

These capris are well-made from sturdy yet slightly stretchy denim. The blue ("Darkness Blue"), looks darker than in the picture, and apparently without the white stitching that are obvious in the image on the listing. To me they actually look better this way, but just wanted to note that for anyone expecting exactly what the pictures show. They're also a bit longer on me, but I'm only 5'4"-5'5" and wearing flats, unlike the model, so they are really more cropped pants than capris. They would be great for transitional weather in spring and fall, due to the longer length and somewhat heavier fabric (they really feel like true jeans). I normally wear an 18-20 which is really more like 20 in pants, so I got the XXL, and they fit perfectly. (They didn't look like they would, but they stretch, and they are comfortable). I can sit and move in them fine. The dark color is nice for dressing up to go out at night, with flats or cute sandals and a dressy top. I like them a lot and would like to get the other colors as well!

They're very comfortable and they have a moderate amount of stretch to them. I ordered them in XXL which is my typical size. I'm about 5'8 and they pull up halfway up my stomach. The waist isn't super tight or loose. The legs are a little roomier than expected. In that way they're not that flattering on me but they're a great pair of pants to lounge about the house or run to the store in. They just look look like "mom pants" on me. The material is a medium thickness and it's actually pretty smooth. They're easy to move around in and to sit on the floor or on a chair in. They don't have a gap against my back and they don't slide down at all. There's no worries about bending over and having any visible skin privacy issues. The little belt holder straps along the waist have space in the loop areas and you can definitely wear a belt through them without a struggle. I can fit most of my hand in the side pockets and curl them up. They're tight enough to trust putting my keys in to go for a walk without being paranoid about them falling. It's not my stomach that makes the button/zipper area pop crazy as it does in the pictures. I didn't pay attention to how they looked pre wash but post wash the button flap just kind of protrudes and that's the way it is. The zipper works well and zips all the way up. Super casual.
