I really liked these jeans, but they just didn't work for me. :( Quality is excellent; they're a good, stretchy jean with a wide leg--which is so on-trend right now. The front pockets are adorable and are actually useable. The length is ok for flats, but would be too short for taller shoes. The downside and the reason for the 4-stars; they run SUPER big. The waist was so large on me that if I were to sit down, you'd see my backside goodies. And, because they were larger on me, they made me look a little fuller than normal. I wold definitely SIZE DOWN in these. I wish there was an X-Small because I would totally wear them. For reference, here are my stats...I’m 5’4” and I’m 123 lbs. I have a short torso, so longer legs for my height. My weight is pretty evenly distributed, so I’m a natural hourglass. I’ve got a slight mom tummy going, but you can’t really tell too much in the jeans. My measurements are 34” (or 34D), 27”, 34”. I ordered the SMALL. NOTE: I paired these jeans with the CIDER Striped Mock Neck Sweater Women Long Sleeve Pullover Knit Top ( which is a winner!

These jeans are a nice lightweight material with just the right amount of stretch. I really like the wide hemmed cuff and the quality stitching—it gives them a polished, on-trend look. They are definitely a true high-waisted style, which isn’t the best fit for me personally since I have a short waist. I find they look best worn with untucked shirts, but not as flattering with tucked-in or cropped tops on my body type. For sizing reference, I’m 5’5” and 165 lbs, typically wear a 12–14, and ordered a large. The large fit but felt a little roomy, especially since they stretched out more after wearing for just a couple of hours. I would recommend ordering a size down if you’re between sizes. Overall, these jeans are stylish, comfortable, and a great value for the quality.

user_comment: Very happy with these jeans. The denim material is lightweight and has a nice stretch to it, making them very comfy to wear while still looking slightly more dressy. I am 5'4" and the length is perfect on me with flats or a slight heel; they may be a bit shorter on anyone with longer legs. The waist hits higher and the pockets on the front and the back are flattering, along with the wider/thicker hemmed cuffs which adds a slight retro look to the jeans. They do run slightly loose, which I liked in terms of the fit, but if you are between sizes and want them more fitted, I'd suggested going down a size (unless you're tall, then they may be too short as well). Overall very happy with the fit and quality of these jeans for the price.
reviewer_name_raw: Great fit, nice and stretchy denim!

user_comment: Wrangler Women's High-Waisted Fierce Flare I really like this particular blend of denim and the Constellation color. This color is more of a soft, muted black and not a dark black color. The stretchiness accommodates my curves well. I've got more of a posterior and the biggest problem I have in all jeans is that if they fit my posterior they are gaping in the waist or/vice versa if they fit in the waist well they are super tight on my posterior. I have none of the problems with the stretchy and accommodating Wrangler Constellation denim (see pic of denim composition tag I've attached to this review). I'm 5'8" with weight bouncing between 155-160. I can wear the Wrangler Constellation 12/34 for a casual, work, comfortable fit and also the 10/34 for a tighter, going-out fit. One thing I've found with jeans that are too long (we all have varying lengths of legs individually) is to find someone that hems jeans to your specific, individual length. It's relatively inexpensive and gives you that personalized, tailored fit. I highly recommend the Wrangler Constellation jeans for all my curvy friends out there!
Wrangler Women's High-Waisted Fierce Flare I love these jeans I’m usually a size 4 however after reading the reviews I sized up to a 6 and they fit just fine I ordered a 34 inseam to wear with boots

These denim capris are just wonderful! I ordered from the size chart and they fit true to size. This is a nice pair of denim capris from Grapent. I ordered the medium in the color darkness blue. I usually wear a size 10 and they fit well. I'm not always a fan of elastic waistbands, but this one has a nice wide band that doesn't roll. I'm 5'2" and short waisted, so I don't usually tuck my shirts in. The slim legs are flattering. There are 4 pockets and they just hold my phone. These capris are 70% cotton with some polyester and spandex. I washed them in cold water and dried them on low heat, like most of my clothing. They had very little shrinkage. A good value.

They are slightly long on me but I normally wear platforms so they are the perfect length with that.

Levi's Women's Classic Skinny Jeans, (New) Nice fit & good quality denim. I like that they are a bit heavier denim for cool weather/winter bc I wanted the skinny leg to tuck into boots. The dark denim in the photos is a 4S & the lighter is a 6S. I honestly couldn't tell a difference btw the 2 fit wise. I'm very pear shaped. I'm 5'3", 117 lbs with a 26" waist & 37½" hips. I thought the 6S were a bit loose around the waist so I ordered the 4S thinking the fit would be better but like I said, to me, they seemed the same. So I kept both 😁 Just FYI, with a belt they wear comfortably without " slipping" & they didn't start to sag like some jeans will around my waist, a common problem for pears.

user_comment: Levi's Women's Ribcage Straight Ankle Jeans These jeans are very comfortable. I got the wash “cool agenda” for the record. But they’re almost too stretchy. Reminds me of the millennial skinny jeans stretch and I like a little bit more stiffness so that they stay put. I say that because as I walk, I feel them riding up which then makes them feel too short. I got inseam 27 and I’m 5’1 128lbs and got a size 28. I think inseam 30 would work better. If you’re wanting jeans to lounge in then these will fit the bill. They make my butt look amazing as my husband would say and they have washed well. I’m just still unsure if I’ll keep.

user_comment: As an Amazon Vine reviewer, I’ve built my share of couple’s wardrobes—helping my wife Emily hunt for jeans that flatter without squeezing, flow without flopping, and transition from coffee runs to date nights without a second thought. Emily’s 5'7" with an athletic build, and she’s tired of stiff skinnies that dig in after an hour or baggy cargos that swallow her shape. When these ZPIUO Women's Wide Leg Jeans arrived through Vine (high-waisted straight-leg stretchy denim with patch pockets in a medium wash), I handed them over for her real-life test: farmers market strolls, park picnics, and a casual dinner out. After weeks of rotation—from fall layering to solo errands—they're the versatile vogue that's won her over, blending '90s nostalgia with modern ease for under $50. Set Up and Fit - Slips on smooth: Button-fly closure cinches secure at her natural waist (11-inch rise), no gapping or muffin-top drama. Size 10 fit her 38" hips with breathing room, straight legs draping to full-length (30" inseam) without pooling—petite-friendly with a cuff option. - Patch pocket perfection: Flap details on the hips add a trendy cargo touch without bulk; rear pockets swallow her phone, and the stretch blend (2% elastane) flexes for squats or stairs. Washes true—no shrinkage after a cold cycle. - Layering lightweight: At about 12 oz, pairs under boots or with sneakers; folds flat in the drawer, no hanger marks. Performance: - Moves like a dream: Stretchy denim recovered shape through a 3-mile market loop—no sagging knees or thigh rub, even after a big lunch. High waist smoothed her midsection subtly, and the wide legs swished cool in 65°F breezes without overheating. Wore them tucked into a sweater for dinner—flattered without fuss. - All-occasion ace: Held a tote on one hip during shopping sprints; patch pockets gripped keys without jingling. Post-wear, no creases or pet hair cling—shakes off dust like a pro. - Daily drape: Flowed with a blouse for brunch, cinched with a belt for errands—versatile enough for travel without packing wrinkles. Build Quality: - Sturdy stretch denim: Mid-weight twill with reinforced stitching at pockets and hems—resists pilling after two washes (gentle, air dry), and the wash stayed even, no fading streaks. Patch flaps are functional (snap-close), not flimsy; high-rise belt loops grip without stretching. - After a month's mileage (including one muddy park mishap), no rips, loose threads, or color run—feels mid-tier Levi's, not fast-fashion fleeting. Slight distressing adds character without wear-through. - Eco-ish edge: Cotton-heavy blend, machine-wash tough—hangs dry overnight, ready for round three. Pros: - Flattering high waist with built-in smoothing - Generous wide legs that move freely - Practical patch pockets for everyday essentials - Affordable stretch that holds shape wash after wash Cons: - Runs slightly long on shorter frames (easy cuff) - Light wash might show dirt faster (dark options available) My Take: Emily's denim game just leveled up—these ZPIUO wide-legs are the high-waisted high-note: comfy, current, and curve-kissing for the win. They elevated her basics from blah to boss, proving patch-pocket power doesn't need a premium tag. If wide-leg whimsy has your wife jean-jaded, leg into these—Vine stride for stretchy supremacy. ? Yes No (Reviewed in the United States on November 11, 2025) Disclosure: I received this product for free through the Amazon Vine program in exchange for my honest review. All opinions are based on actual wear by my wife Emily during daily activities and casual outings.

I really like these. The fabric is stretchy, but still has a good amount of structure to it. I like the color of the light wash. It’s not artificially blue, just a nice natural looking wash. The length is good for me, I’m 5‘7“ tall. And I wear a size 10, in order to 10, and they fit Pretty well. They’re a little bit snug in the waist, and the front pockets look a little odd in real life. I’ll probably wear it with longer sweatshirts, anyway, so it’s not a huge problem. I like the cut of the legs, a boot cut, it’s not overly tight in the thigh. I like these a lot.

**Excuse the crazy hair! It’s a little “breezy” in Kansas today!”** A new twist to the barrel Jean trend??? Maybe!!! If you haven’t decided on the barrel jean trend! This pair of jeans may have you deciding….YES!!! This pair of barrel jeans seem to be a slimmer cut than my others. It’s like if straight cut and barrel had a baby! The jeans have a very noticeable darting at the knees in the front and back and an addition seam at the side making this a unique design of barrel jeans! I am 5’4” 145 lbs wearing a size large (10-12). I normally buy mediums (8-10) but noticed the sizing chart for this brand is a little different. The jeans are a nice length and can be wore fall/winter and rolled for cuffs spring/summer. The fabric offers a nice stretch without stretch out and losing the fit of the jeans! 70% Cotton 20% Polyester 10% Spandex I made this purchase in Black Friday, so today is my first time wearing them. They seem to be a good quality jean! Very comfortable to wear. I honestly think they could easily make my top 10 favorites!

These barrel jeans are surprisingly comfortable with a lot of stretch. The quality is very good with reinforced stitching at stress points and pockets. The fabric is quite substantial not flimsy at all. I got the dark blue wash and am quite happy with it. It has some distressed features at the hem and pocket edges with some faded areas that adds a vintage esthetic. The sizing was a bit of a gamble. At 5'4" tall and 157# with 36.5" waist 43" hips, I opted for the size L becasue that is typically my size. My measurements put me off the chart >2XL which is completely out of touch for sizing. In any case, the Large fits pretty good because they are so stretchy. They are not baggy on me except in the legs. For a baggy look, the XL would have been better. The length given in the size chart is the outseam not the inseam. These jeans are little long for ankle length, but that is my lot in life. This is my first pair of barrel jeans, and I quite like them.

These are straight leg jeans with some extra stitching and definitely aren’t cut in a barrel style. The size small fit a 00 with room to spare in the waist. Model is 5’8 and they felt a bit short. The material is decent with no smell and the construction isn’t bad, the cut is just way off.

We got this for our daughter who is 5'1" and weighs 120 pounds. She is in-between girl sizes and women's sizes. We got her a small and they fit great. She loves that they aren't restricting anywhere. It helps with all-day comfort. All the pockets are real! She also loves the random pleats along the legs that give it character. We got white and these are definitely white. They look great and feel like great quality. They are easy to wash, just like any other jeans, throw them in the washer.

Levi's Women's Ribcage Full Length I have at least 20 pairs of all kinds of Levi’s, but mostly the high waisted and these Levi’s are by far my favorite right now!! They are sturdy but comfy and soft and look exactly like pictured! I got size 26 & they fit perfect, I just had to cut a few inches off because I’m short. . I am 5’2 & 118lbs and usually wear a 25 or 26. I get a 26 on the Levi premium or the 99-100% cotton, since they’re stiffer, but I actually think I could fit into the 25s too. All in all, I am loving these super high waisted Levi’s!

Levi's Women's Ribcage Full Length I’m typically a size 4/6 depending on the brand but in these I’m a size 2 (26) so definitely size down! They get great! I’ve been searching high and low for a good loose fit jean! Love the length! I’m 5’7 and 125lbs

Levi's Women's Ribcage Full Length I’m typically a size 4/6 depending on the brand but in these I’m a size 2 (26) so definitely size down! They get great! I’ve been searching high and low for a good loose fit jean! Love the length! I’m 5’7 and 125lbs

These black skinny jeans are very comfy. I love the elastic waist and that they are not skin tight. They are a little short on my 5'6" frame. The fabric is good quality and not too thin. They are as advertised. I gave them 4 stars because the fit a little weird in the hips.

Normally I wear size M in bottoms, but I ordered size S and fit perfectly on me, I’m 5’4” tall and 150 pounds bodybuilding type for reference , I love how they look and the shape they make on my body, fabric is not the best quality, for my opinion it is too thin, that’s the only reason why I’m giving 4 stars instead 5. But I definitely recommend it

These pants are awful. Tight in the butt yet no lift whatsoever! In fact gave me an ugly shape. These jeans are true to size and have a wide-leg fit without being overly baggy, which I really appreciate. The leg opening is relaxed but still polished, not huge or overwhelming. The ankle-length cut is perfect—I’m 5’3”, and they hit exactly where I want them to. They’re not too short, but also don’t drag on the ground. More like a longer crop that looks intentional and flattering. The blue wash is really nice, easy to dress up or down, and the overall fit is comfortable while still looking put-together. A great find if you’re petite and struggle with lengths.

I love the fabric for these jeans. There's a little stretch and they feel like real denim. I'm not a fan of the front pockets, but I'll just wear sweaters and things I don't tuck in when wearing these. Sizing is off though. The size 18 for instance says it has a 36.2" waist and it's actually just over 38". I was concerned about the waste so I had ordered the 18 when I usually wear a 16, sometimes a 14. I wish I had ordered the 16. The cut is flattering and I like the high waist. I like that I put them on in the morning and really didn't think about them for the rest of the day, no tugging, punching or pulling up all day.

The material has lots of stretch, making them very confortable and definitely flattering. They're high waisted and the flare at the bottom is just the right amount. I have gotten this brand in other jeans and they didnt have the belt loops, this pair does which makes me so happy as i can cinch in when needed or leave as is when Im feeling bloated. They run just a bit big - maybe its the stretch but for the smallest size I definitely felt like i had a little extra room. If you're tall, they might be a little on the shorter side. But overall, they're stylish, comfy, and easy to dress up or down. Recommend! For reference - i am 5'5'', 135lbs and wearing a size 4.

I ordered these in Black, and sized up one size. They are well made, but the curved style did not work for me, but may for another body type. They were large in the hips, but tapered so narrowly between the hip and knee that they were uncomfortably tight. Then, they flared out to an exaggerated bell bottom. If I tried to settle them down my waist to make the thigh area more comfortable, the rise was too long. I am 5’2, 118 lbs and ordered a size 6. The length was barely long enough for me. The fading of the black denim was also more exaggerated, and striped looking.

For reference, I am 4’11”, 110lb, so definitely on the petite side. I normally wear a 2P in pants. I ordered these pants “wished black” in Small. I hesitate to call these pants “jeans” because the material doesn’t feel denim at all, but they have the look of denim. This fabric is much lighter, relaxed, and airy than a true denim, and much softer as well. It is important to note it has NO stretch. Personally, I loved the fabric…it’s the star of the show on these pants. It’s perfect for spring and fall here in the Midwest. As expected, these were a little big and about 2 inches too long on me (see my pic), so they will need hemmed, but the fit was good everywhere else. They fit my 5’2” 110lb daughter perfectly, but these are so cute and comfy, I'm hemming them and keeping them for myself. The relaxed fit, high waisted, wide-leg style is versatile enough to dress up or down. The “wished black” color is awesome…it basically looks like a well-worn black. The drawstring is functional, or it can be removed if you don’t care for that look. It has four pockets that are actually roomy enough to be useful. If you’re looking for a stylish, soft, breathable alternative to heavy jeans, these are a steal ($30 at time of review).

They are a nice length on me- I’m only 5’1, and they are full length- so they fit great with a small heel, but I could get away with flats or tennis shoes! A great pair of jeans that I’ll be wearing a lot in warmer weather!

I really wanted to love these jeans, and honestly—they are so close to being perfect. For reference, I’m 5’11” and planned to wear them as a slightly cropped, relaxed style with sandals, which would’ve been super cute. The overall look and design are absolutely on trend. The fabric is soft, comfortable, and feels well-made—not stiff like some denim can be. Quality-wise, they definitely feel worth the price. However, the fit is where things fell off for me. These do not run true to size. They were tight in the butt and upper thighs, and the waistband made my stomach look more pronounced than it actually is, which wasn’t flattering. If you’re considering these, I would definitely recommend sizing up for a more relaxed, comfortable fit—especially if you want that easy, wide-leg look. Overall: ✔ Super cute style ✔ Soft, quality material ✔ Worth the price ✖ Runs small — size up for best fit I really wish these had worked for me because they’re adorable, but the sizing just wasn’t quite right.

My FAVORITE pants even after two years! I’m not exaggerating, I sometimes wear these about 5 times a week. I’m 6’1 and have the hardest time finding pants that are long enough, but these are great because they cinch at the bottom, so they don’t look odd sitting above the ankle. After two years, they are slightly faded, but not to an ugly dingy color. I’d still consider them a dark black and not a grey, like some black fabrics can fade to. After washing, they can feel slightly tighter but they loosen again after wearing for a little while. These pants are high quality, super cute, very comfortable, and a great length. The strings that cinch the legs are also adjustable if you don’t want it cinched, both ways look cute.

Women's Plus Size Skinny Jeans Stretch high Waisted Ankle Ripped Jeans The fit of these jeans is not at all what I expected. I'm 5'7" and the length of these jeans is more like a capri than ankle length. The bottom of the pants sit well above my ankles and that's standing up. If I sit down, they lift even higher around my calves. The fit is also really loose. Other skinny jeans I own have a much tighter fit. I know these are stretch material, but these are too loose even without stretching the fabric. Unfortunately, these aren't something I'd wear and I wouldn't order them again.

Levi's Women's Plus-Size 315 Shaping Bootcut Jean Absolutely love these jeans! These are my first Levi’s I’ve purchased in many years but love the fit, style and color. When I first tried on, I thought I’d have to get them hemmed but with a pair of 3” heeled boots, the length is perfect! For reference, I’m 5’3”, 140 lbs and pear shaped. I ordered a size 28 and they fit perfectly. I typically wear either a 27 or 28 (4 or 6). They have a good amount of stretch, and are high waisted without being too high (like the ribcage fit ones) and I like the pocket placement. The pockets sell me on jeans, they can be flattering depending on pocket size, placement, etc. When I lose my winter weight will probably purchase this exact pair in a size smaller.

They are not what I would call high-waisted because they cinch just below my navel, giving me a slight muffin top. I am sure they will stretch out quickly. Because I sized down, I will be wearing my shirts outside the pants. I ordered the pants in off- white , the muted color looking better on me coming out of winter without any tan whatsoever. Once again, this pair of pants makes a great transitional garment during spring. It is fair priced and has good value.

I’m 5’7”, 129 lbs, 29” waist, 38” at the widest part of my booty. Size charts always seem to be so misleading. I can never rely on them. I read reviews trying to figure out what size to order. Several reviews said the size chart was pretty accurate, which meant I would have needed to order a large. I’m usually a small, but in considering the reviews I ordered a large and a medium and figured I’d return whichever didn’t fit. I ended up ordering a small after they arrive because the medium was too big. In the photos, I’m wearing a small, and you can see the pants could easily fit someone 2-3 inches wider than I am. They stretch big time. I’ll probably keep the small but not sure because the length is right at where I would want it to be and after washing/drying, it seems like this fabric would shrink quite a bit and then be too short. Either way, I would order a size down or at the very least your regular size when ordering these.

I was really hoping to love these jeans. The reviews were mixed so I knew it would be a crap shoot whether they worked or not. Unfortunately, these are a swing and a miss for me. I’m 5ft 7in, 165lbs, carrying most of my weight in my belly. I wear 8/10 bottoms and M/L tops. I ordered size Large. I liked how much stretch there was but it’s almost too much. I suspect these won’t hold their shape over time and jeans this stretchy tend to accentuate my belly vs slimming it. Cute from the front, not as much from the side. The fabric is a bit rough and scratchy on my skin. The belt is super cute. These just touched the floor with bare feet, but became too short with a sneaker on. Because of that I would only wear these with flip flops and that’s just not versatile enough to add this piece to my wardrobe. Will be returning. Bummer :(

I can't decide what I like better, the cuteness or the comfort. Both are amazing. Starting with the comfort: They are really soft, like those old jeans you've worn for years and are your favorite pair. They are already broken in and have just the right amount of stretch to hug the curves without binding or being uncomfortable. The front of the jeans hit right below my belly button, which is perfect for me. They aren't low cut, so aren't going to fall off my butt when I bend over. I ordered my usual size (small) and they fit like a glove. For reference, I am 5'2" and 115 pounds. The worn/ripped parts are all in good spots, so they look good without showing skin where it could be embarrassing. The stitching is neat and spaced well, none are loose or look like there was an issue when they were sewing it. The inside seams are all reinforced at the stress points and even at the hem line where the side seams are, there is a spot near the bottom where it's stitched and reinforced. That is quality and attention to potential problem areas. There are rivets on the pockets, also to reinforce the pockets and insure they don't rip when you are trying to shove something big and bulky in them. The front has the usual metal button and zipper. Again, it's the zipper is sewn in well and even after doing a lot of up and down while pulling on it to see if it would easily break or jump the teeth, it kept working and didn't jam. The button slides in and out of the buttonhole easily, but not so loose that it comes unfastened on its own. When I washed them, I treated like my other jeans, cold water wash and regular cycle for the dryer. As expected, there was a bit of shrinkage but just like other jeans, they stretched out and were comfortable with in half an hour of wearing them. The washed-out color and the rips make these look (and feel) like jean shorts you have worn for years; I really LOVE them and recommend them.

LEVI STRAUSS SIGNATURE Women's Heritage High Rise Straight Jeans I am 5’3”, 155lbs. This pair is a size 8 (I am always able to wear a size 8 in Levi Signature - as they all run big IMO). The waist measure 15.5 inches flat, the inseam is 27 inches (ankle length, if you are taller than 5’4 these are gonna be short, almost a perfect length for me at 5’3”), 11.5 inch rise, the leg opening is 14 inches. They are a mid-weight cotton blend with plenty of stretch. The rise is long, maybe a little too long, so the crotch hangs a little low if you don’t keep them pulled up. They are a nice slim straight style, that are a pretty classic cut that will likely always be “in-style” They are comfy. I have washed themed and they really didn’t seam to shrink and they have done a decent job of holding their shape throughout the day. Overall a solid pair of not expensive jeans, great for wearing around the house, but definitely don’t do anything to enhance your figure; like you don’t put them on and think “wow, these look great” it is more like “wow, these are a perfectly serviceable pair of jeans”. I mean for $23 you can’t go wrong really.

Gap Women's Mid Rise Baggy Denim These Gap midrise jeans should be labeled as "ultra baggy" in my opinion. Or maybe it's just me not being used to the current baggy jeans fad. I grew up in the 80s & early 90s and used to wear baggy jeans, but they weren't THIS baggy back then. The fabric isn't stretchy, but it is super soft against my skin which I love. I'm just under 5'2" and around 125 pounds, and size 27 short fits well in my waist but is a tad too long so I'll need to wear these with heels (which I typically don't wear). I'm wearing 2.75 inch heeled boots in the pics I posted with this review. In my experience, Gap jeans are always high quality, and these are no exception. I just wish for my own preference these weren't quite so baggy. But if you're looking for a high-quality pair of ultra baggy jeans because that's your normal style, these will likely be a great fit for you. I recommend ordering "short" if you're under 5'4".

Gap Women's Mid Rise Baggy Denim These jeans are everything I hoped they would be. They fit a little snug at the top, and if I ordered again, I would go one size up for extra comfort. Once I wore them in, they loosened up just enough to feel relaxed without losing shape. The dark rinse looks polished and makes my figure look slimmer. I wore them all day, and while they rode up a little at first, that stopped after a few hours. I also love that the length works perfectly on my 5’5” frame and does not drag the ground. The denim feels sturdy, the stitching is solid, and they look effortlessly chic. They are quite stylish and I'm very pleased with them.

Gap Women's Mid Rise Baggy Denim Ok I actually really love that I accidentally ordered these a little too big. If I was going to do that with any jeans it needed to be these ones. These are SO incredibly comfortable and soft it’s ridiculous. It’s like those favorite pair of jeans that you finally get broken in kind of soft. I ordered the short because I’m 5’2” and even pulled up they’re still a bit long on me (see photo). That doesn’t bother me with these because of the style of the jeans though, and I can always hem them if I want to. These remind me of some of the jeans I had in the 90’s in grade school and I don’t care if that shows my age! I think everyone deserves a pair of baggy jeans or they’re missing out.

user_comment: - I ordered these because I have a large waist-to-hip difference/ratio and these looked like they could accommodate that. - The waist is unfortunately larger than expected, but that would probably be my biggest complaint with these. - They could be a tad bit more high-waisted for my taste, but they are advertised as mid-rise and I would say they are almost more high-rise. Just about perfect though. - They do also arrive with a slight chemical smell, but not too bad, and it will most likely come out in the wash. - Nice that there are no weird big logo names/patches on the jeans - The material feels like sturdy denim to the touch, but is somehow also very stretchy!? I can squat with no problem as if I were wearing leggings! Since it's not as rigid as regular denim it is much more comfortable overall and reduces crotch chafing. The stretch also helps it to not pull/ride down as you move, making the larger waist not as much of a problem. The waist feels large but doesn't fall down on its own. These jeans also accommodate my larger hip area without making things look too baggy. The straight leg (which verges on bootcut since the leg is pretty wide overall) will be flattering on anyone like me who needs to balance out broad shoulders with more volume on the bottom half of the body. - They do run wide overall, so it does make the knees look a little "fat" - The length is perfect for me and hits right on top of my feet and 1/2-1 inch from the floor in the back. I am 5'7", 135lbs, 25-27" waist, 35-36" hips, longer torso than legs. I usually wear small or size 4, and Small in these was true to size and good for me. - The front and back pockets are all REAL and big enough to fit my whole hand (still not as big as I'd like but that's good enough). - Overall they're not miracle jeans but I'm pleasantly surprised and happy with them. Decent quality for a pretty decent price. - I'll update my review if anything changes!

- I ordered these because I have a large waist-to-hip difference/ratio and these looked like they could accommodate that. - The waist is unfortunately larger than expected, but that would probably be my biggest complaint with these. - They could be a tad bit more high-waisted for my taste, but they are advertised as mid-rise and I would say they are almost more high-rise. Just about perfect though. - They do also arrive with a slight chemical smell, but not too bad, and it will most likely come out in the wash. - Nice that there are no weird big logo names/patches on the jeans - The material feels like sturdy denim to the touch, but is somehow also very stretchy!? I can squat with no problem as if I were wearing leggings! Since it's not as rigid as regular denim it is much more comfortable overall and reduces crotch chafing. The stretch also helps it to not pull/ride down as you move, making the larger waist not as much of a problem. The waist feels large but doesn't fall down on its own. These jeans also accommodate my larger hip area without making things look too baggy. I was surprised how soft and stretchy these jeans feel when I first tried them. The fit is relaxed but still look neat, not too baggy which I like. The length was good for me (I’m around 5'5), just touch top of my shoes. The denim fabric feels nice quality and a bit stretchy so easy to move and sit. The color looks same as picture, maybe just little lighter under sunlight but still pretty. They look stylish with tucked shirt or simple tee, I wore once with sandals and once with sneakers and both look good. Very comfortable for long day wear, no tight waist. I hand washed first time and it didn’t bleed or shrink, so quality seem fine. Overall simple but nice pair of jeans for daily wear, I might get another color soon.

Very pleasantly surprised! I normally do not like clothing with elastic waist bands. Aside from regular leggings. But I took a gamble with these because they looked so nice and crisp. I love the fit. They are just a tiny bit flared at the ankle and they are just cropped enough - not too short. Short pants not long shorts. I like that the pockets are classic jeans style pockets with the two in the front and the two square pockets in the back. No zipper or buttons. The jeans are decently stretchy. The one thing I dont like about many white garments is that you can see the pockets through the material since its such a light color. And I can see the outline of the front pockets through the jeans and I really dont like that but its only noticeable in certain light. I ordered a medium and I love the fit. I'm 5'9 and about 170 pounds. And I'm 36, 32, 42. These are very classic looking jeans that can be worn with a t-shirt and flip flops to the beach or with dress shoes and a blouse for a summer outing. Very versatile because even though they are jeans they have a very nice shape and fit.

These jeans are very comfortable and flattering. The stretch in the legs makes them easy to move in throughout the day. However, the listing describes them as cropped, but on me they were full length. I’m 5’1”, so that may be why they didn’t hit at a cropped length. Petite shoppers may want to keep that in mind. The pockets are functional, which is always a plus. One thing to consider is the high-waisted design. Because of how the waistband fits, it may not be the most forgiving option around the midsection. Those who prefer a more relaxed waist area might want to consider that when choosing their size or style.

I used to wear jeans all the time in my younger days, but I slowly stopped wearing them because -- let's face it -- yoga pants and sweatpants are much more comfortable when lounging around the house, but I have to say that these straight leg stretchy jeans are in fact quite comfortable and very flattering on. I am 5'4", 127 pounds, and usually wear a size small or medium, but I chose the medium size, and they fit perfectly fine. The inside tag instructs to machine wash cold, wash inside out with like colors on gentle cycle, and do not tumble dry. It also says that these can be dry cleaned. The material makeup reads 75% Cotton, 23% Polyester, and 2% Elastane. Overall, I think these are comfortable jeans that would be great to wear to potlucks, picnics, and get-togethers -- basically, anywhere where yoga pants and sweatpants are too casual to consider wearing.

These white pants have a clean, polished look and decent fabric quality, but the fit is very body-dependent, especially through the waist and lower abdomen. The 75% cotton / 23% polyester / 2% elastane fabric is thick enough not to be see-through (a must for white pants) and has a bit of stretch, but the waistband itself is quite firm and unforgiving. The leg cut is streamlined and flattering, but on my 5’5” height they were not cropped, hitting right at the ankle bone. The waistband sits directly on the lower abdomen and tends to compress rather than smooth, which creates visible tension lines for curvier or softer midsections. I ordered my usual size Large; the legs fit well, but I would personally want more give through the waist for comfort. These work best with longer or structured tops that don’t require tucking in. If you prefer a polished, tucked-in look, the waistband may be a dealbreaker. Overall, they’re decent white pants with good opacity, but the waistband fit will make or break them. If you have a flatter lower abdomen, they’ll likely work well; otherwise, sizing up or choosing a different cut may be the better option.

I rarely write reviews. If something does what it should… great. However if something surpasses expectations or is absolute crap… I’ll reviewThese pants are AWESOME. Been on the hunt for the perfect barrel jean forever. I’m tall (5’9”; 150 muscular build) and they usually are comically short. I bought an 8 and they are the perfect blend of length and loose fit- like they are supposed to look. I’m buying the black color next!

Love these pants! Being only 5' 2.5", they are a tad long , but that is typical of every pant I put on. I wish they would offer them in differing inseam lengths. But otherwise, they fit well and have just the right amount of stretch. They are slimming, without creating muffin top pinch in the waistband. The reason for a missing star is that they do create a bit of "camel". I tend to wear a longer top that covers that area anyway. But maybe the front seam could be done a bit differently

NOT SHORT GIRL FRIENDLY. I was to start this review with saying do not order if you’re a short girl. I, thankfully, know how to sew and was able to hem the pants myself. For the average shirt girl, I would strongly avoid, unless you can sew or want to pay a tailor to alter them. I am 5’2 and had to cut off five inches from the bottom. You can see in my photo that I have it pinned in one, I was measuring for the hem. The length look ridiculous, honestly it looked like I was wearing scuba fins with how much fabric there was at the bottom. To GTP: either shorten the length to adjust to the average woman’s height (5’3-5’5) or provide a petite option because it truly was ridiculous. The pros are that they are stretchy and indeed comfortable. The quality is as expected from TGP, as always it’s not great but it’s not bad. They look cute, but they don’t look like real jeans either.

So, for the past couple of years I haven't worn anything other than sweatpants. I have Crohn's disease, and often have bloating and overall abdominal pain, in addition to frequent weight loss and weight gain. When I saw these had an elastic section of waistband, I thought I'd give them a try. I'm so impressed with these! They're soft and comfortable. Not like traditional denim, which I'm so used to not wearing these days that I can't stand the feel of it anymore. I'm 5'5" with a shorter body and longer legs, and these hit just around or slightly below my ankle. This works for me, because the length in the listing images was the one thing that made me think twice before ordering, and the current length will look great with my croc platforms. I'm assuming they're longer, partially because I got the size 17. It seems that some companies add length as the size goes up, as if larger waist size means you are taller as well. Like I said, the length works for me, but it's something to keep in mind if you truly want the length shown in the listing. I don't have a full length mirror in my house, but when I do wear these out and about, I'll have someone get a picture so I can update this review with it. I love the look of them and they aren't oversized in the booty, hip and thighs, like most larger pants are. It's difficult to find things that fit my body type since when I gain weight, it's all around my middle, never, unfortunately, in my legs and behind. In fact, the straightness of these helps me to look a little better proportioned, which I'm thankful for. Overall, I'm extremely pleased with the value for cost, the fit, the fabric, and everything else about these. I would definitely recommend, and would consider ordering again. Think I'll try the darker ones next time.

I really wanted to love these jeans, I love the cut and the length is perfect for my 5’2” height. I decided to return them as they’re a bit tight on the crutch area, would probably bother me during long sitting. Thin material, not sure if they would shrink. My waist line is 23 and I got size 1.

I love my new pants. They are soft and have enough stretch when I move in them that they don't leave me feeling constricted but still snug enough to give me shape in all the right areas. The pocket size is perfect and I think the overall quality is good but I did see a small stitch in the corner where it's showing a small hole through the pants. These pants are fairly light feeling which is what I like, but if you're looking for something to wear while walking through a blizzard....yup these are not for you 😂. My only concern for others (unfortunately I'm just used to it) is that in the stock pictures it has the pants slightly above the wearer's ankles. So I'm about 5'4/5'5 and these fit like normal floor length pants. So for those that are vertically challenged like myself and were hoping for higher up the leg pants then you will be fairly disappointed. But I am 100% thrilled with these pants and I'm glad I bought them.

These size 13 short palazzos are great. The in between size is just right without making me fill like I'm huge. The hips fit is just right and the wide bottom moves freely. There's elastic in the waist that helps and the material has some stretch to it. I could have gone with 15 but psychologically, I don't want to think I'm that size and they would be too baggy elsewhere. The short length makes it fall just at the right height seeing I'm 5'2". They are jeans material but they feel thing compared to very good quality and long lasting jeans that I own so I'm curious how they will hold up. The generous pockets are perfect though. Very pleased with the style, fit and look.

I really like these dark blue denim capris. They are a mid rise with a relaxed straight leg fit, though the leg opening is definitely wider almost more of a flared style capri. The denim color is a very nice, rich dark blue, and the material has a good amount of stretch, making them very comfortable to wear and move around in. I ordered a size XL and it fit me very well not too tight and not too loose. For reference, I usually wear a size 16, and I felt these were true to size. They button and zip easily, and the overall quality of the material feels solid and well made. I’m 5'2" and short and curvy, and these fit my legs perfectly. The length is just right for capris and very flattering on shorter girls. The only downside is the chemical smell. Even after washing, there was still a slight chemical odor, which I didn’t appreciate. That said, aside from the smell, these capris are comfortable, stylish, and fit great. Overall, a great pair of capris with good stretch and a flattering fit just be aware of the lingering smell.

I am a bit on a fence about these jeans. On one hand, they are soft and comfortable. On the other hand, I think they have a bit of an odd fit. Or it could be just due to my particular physique. I think they would look much better on someone very slim with narrow hips. For the rest of us, it’s not a very flattering look. For reference, I am 5 feet 5 inches tall and 140 pounds. I normally wear Size M and got these jeans in Size M. They fit me just right sizewise. So I would say that these jeans are okay in terms of the quality and craftsmanship. And for someone who is looking just for a pair of comfortable capri jeans and not too concerned about the looks, these ones would work just fine. They are also affordable, priced at $30.

I got these black straight leg jeans for my wife and she likes them with some reservations regarding the unfinished hems. The sizing chart is accurate and the fit is definitely loose and baggy. These are made to be baggy and the fabric has a minimum stretch. The pants are not heavy weighted and they look just like the seller's pictures, they are very soft and the color black is not as pigmented as depicted. My wife is 5 feet and 2 inches tall and the pants fit her perfectly.

I love these chinos. They’re cut really well and very cute. I got them at a great price. I usually am a size 6 in stretchy jeans and 10 with no stretch/100% cotton, but in the Amazon essentials chino shorts I’m a 10 so I ordered these in 10 but these are definitely cut roomier than the shorts. Still, they don’t fall down at all throughout the day and my only other white pants (jeans) are butt tight. These as with the shorts have a little stretch. I showed close up of how long they fall on me: 5’9, long legs. I wore black underwear with them and although you could tell a little my husband said you couldn’t really unless you were staring. Don’t do what I did and cut the tag out carelessly! I cut a hole right in the fabric (not on seam!) and had to sew it. It is tiny and no one would notice but I mean I do. The one criticism I could maybe have for Amazon brand chinos (these and shorts) are that the button seems to be sewn in flimsily. I’d get these if went on sale in another color, but would probably get an 8. I’ll dry these in dryer since they’re big and come back to update if the fit changes drastically. They’re very comfy and I even took a long, hot walk in them. They don’t get too wrinkled (I didn’t steam or iron these I just wore them out of the package; I know they’re not perfect but good enough for the day I wore them!). Edit: I tumble dried these on low heat and they shrunk a TINY bit, just enough that the size 10 is now perfect. Not too short or too tight after the dryer. As long as I hung them up immediately, no ironing needed.

I’m 5-6, 150 lbs, size 12 is a perfect fit. The rise comes up to my belly button and the inseam is perfect (long enough to almost touch the ground). Legs are a straight fit. I’d give 5/5 for fit. The color is as expected and closure, seams and other details looks to be high quality, especially for the price. My only complaint - and reason for 4/5 stars - is the back pockets, although functional, are not deep enough to hold a phone. I like these cargo pants a lot. They are really comfortable and roomy. Just what I was looking for. As for picking a size, I would suggest deciding by your waist measurement. My weight is 136 and I am 5’4 and a half inches tall. My waist is 28.5” (in the morning LOL! )and my hips are are 37.5” I ordered a large because the waist size looked like it would be more comfortable. The medium waist only went up to 28” inches. Even with elastic in the back I was worried it would be cutting in to me. I really hate anything that cuts into my stomach. I also despise rolls and muffin tops. This waist size lays nice and flat and gentle around my middle not creating any layer spill over or fat. 🤣. I was looking for roomy and these definitely fill the bill. I like the bottom both cinched and not cinched. The fabric is a soft slightly brushed texture yet has a slight stiffness so they don’t just hang and flap in the wind. Over all a nice pair of cargos. I gave them 4 instead of 5 stars because they have elastic in then back of the waist and I am not a fan of that but everything else is what I was looking for.
reviewer_name_raw: Choose size by your waist size

Let's start this by saying that I have a small-ish butt and am 5'6" at 130 pounds so my tummy is noticeable to me. I got these thinking "Butt lift? YES! Tummy control? YES! Flare Leg? YESSITY YES!" Unfortunately, it didn't lift my butt or provide any semblance of tummy control. I've worn butt lifting jeans that do exactly that job and they're fantastic. These are not those jeans. They are very stretchy and comfortable though. Seriously stretchy... as in, I don't even need to undo the button to slide them on. I'm a women's size 6 so I got Medium size in these jeans -- in my experience, Small is typically too small. HOWEVER, I do love the cut of these jeans. They look so good with heels (see photos) because they're a little longer. I'm hesitant but I'm actually going to put these in the dryer after washing them to see if maybe they'll shrink to that magical zone between Small and Medium and maybe, just maybe, they'll lift and control!

They fit true to size (I’m only 5’3, so they’re not as cropped as in the model photos) and are extremely comfortable. They have an elastic waistband with a good amount of stretch. They are soft and don’t feel like jeans… I’m comfortable in them all day. I am still clinging to my skinny jeans, but these are an acceptable “in fashion” alternative for me. They seem to be of fair quality and have held up in the wash on a normal wash and dry cycle (no shrinking or anything).

Though these were called barrel jeans, I'd say it's more of a straight legged look when actually worn. They're comfy. The denim is soft and flexible, medium weight. The fit is true to size, but the elastic back waistband gives you a little "wiggle room." The pictures shoe these as a cropped or what we called "highwaters" when I wore the style back in the late 80s/early 90s. But I am only 5'4" and have short legs. They land right at my ankle bones. As for quality, they seem to be well-sewn and sturdy. I'll amend if I encounter any condition issues after multiple washings. They have decent-sized pockets at the front hips, but none on the back.

These pants are adorable and the quality is surprisingly good for the price. The material isn't too thin, so they don't look cheap. My advice on sizing: I’m giving 4 stars only because of the length. I am 5 foot and I definitely have to wear these with platform sneakers or heels, otherwise, they drag on the ground. If you are on the shorter side, be prepared to hem them or wear heels. If you are tall, these will be perfect for you! Also size up on these as they run small. I bought a 2xl and they wear like an xl.

TO say I love these jean is an understatement! They are so comfortable!!! Only downfall is that at 5'2", they were a little long on me. They have some stretch to them, so they are great for wearing when your going out to a big meal. LOL For me, I tend to fluctuate on the border of 2 sizes, so I love a pair of jeans like this that has that stretch so I don't have to worry about a little fluctuation in my weight from one month to the next.

The jeans are so easy to slip on and go! They are made from a lightweight denim material and feature a wide-banded elastic waist. I received compliments on them the first time I wore them out to an event. I appreciate how the flat elastic waist provides a smooth middle section -- no lumps and bumps from a button, zipper, and belt loops. These jeans are ideal for a fitted top, and perhaps, a shoe with a bit of heel for elongating the leg. That said, I'm 5'5 and could wear these jeans with flats, and it would be fine. I will note they had a fairly strong chemical smell upon arrival. Even after washing, the chemical smell lessened, but it is still there. It may take a few washings to rid them of it entirely.

Amazon Essentials Women's Low Stretch Mid-Rise Regular-Fit Jean Short I'm 5'6", ~130lbs and waist size 27. i ordered a size 2, which is not my normal size (I'd say I'm usually a 4). Social media kept blasting me with ads for these jeans, and I finally gave in. These jeans are flattering, VERY stretchy, and lighter weight for spring. The higher waist balances my normal length torso with my shorter legs! I’m picky about how jeans lay on my crotch, bum, and thighs - I like baggy jeans on the legs, but not baggy in the hips and crotch! These jeans fit the bill. The stretch made the jeans comfortable to wear with the look of being smoothed without being constricted. I felt the jeans held up to all day wear and didn’t get loose/saggy as some stretch jeans do. I don’t mind that there aren’t any pockets in the front as I like the extra smooth look. The pockets on the bum are ample and deep and held my cell phone perfectly. I bought the light blue first and liked the fit so much I bought the light green and dark blue (all three at ~$36 sale pricing) and am waiting to buy the pink or white (got to wait for that sale price to come back again - I got the purple on sale for $15!!!!!!!) My only con is these jeans only come in a raw hem which is too casual for my business casual work setting. However, again I like the fit so much I can accept just wearing for home and weekends! These are just a great comfortable pair of casual jeans! FYI, I am 5’3”, 115 lbs, 4 months postpartum with twins. I’m typically a size 0/25 and in these I’m wearing an XS.

omg where have these been all my life!?! these are the best pants ever! sorry this ins't the best photo but i am 5'7 and 125 lb. i have most of my height in my legs. these are not good if you have short legs probably because they are the highest waisted pants i have EVER found. also you are going to want to size down. i originally bought the small and had to return for the XS. they are SUPER stretchy. i like they still have structure though. just a nice stretchy denim feeling. they definitely are unmistakable denim though. i dont think i could get away with wearing them at work (denim is not allowed). i would love another pair but tbh i dont like the colors that much otherwise i def would!

Gloria Vanderbilt Women's Amanda Capri Jeans The cat does not approve! If you’re looking for bell-bottom cropped jeans, and you’re more than 6ft tall with seriously big calves, these are great. I was hoping for Capri jeans that fit close -as these are pictured in the item description - but these are not even close to that in reality. These are going back and the search continues!

Gap Women's Mid Rise Legging Pant Listen, I didn't ask them to, I didn't know about it in advance, heck, I didn't even know they were aware of my existence. But obviously, Gap did & does. They went ahead and designed a pair of custom jean leggings made to my exact measurements. Now, even though I plan to buy every single pair of size 6/28, I decided it would only be fair to share my stats with you as well as the stats of MY custom made, comfortable, flattering & 100% perfect fitting jean leggings. Me: 140-145# 5'3" 33.5" waist (measured at the bottom of my bellybutton because that's where MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS sit) 40" hips (measured at the widest point, mid-bum) MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS (Measured flat) 9" rise 14" waist 17" hips They have a REAL zipper fly, not one of those "just for show" kind commonly found on jean leggings. Every pair I've tried in that past with a "fake fly" always had extra fabric that bunched up at the crotch & made me look like I was smuggling oranges whenever I sat down (& they didn't look any better when standing up, either). The traditional 5 pockets are fully functional (real) as well. The stitching/color are absolute perfection & the plain, proportionately sized back pockets are what my denim dreams are made of. If you have time pls check out the pic I attached to see another one of the reasons why these fit so well. I hope my humble opinion helps you in some way. Thank you for reading.

Wrangler Women's Retro Mae Mid Rise Wide Leg Trouser Jean First off, I LOVE these jeans! Secondly, the reviews I read on here were a huge help in sizing. It’s weird because this are definitely Women’s jeans, but the sizing is in Juniors. For reference I am 6’0, 170-175 lbs, and a solid Women’s size 10/30, which would translate to a Juniors size 11. Per the suggestion of previous reviews, I reluctantly sized down to a 9. The last thing I wanted was for the jeans to fit for an hour, only to stretch beyond repair. I ordered a size 9 with 34” inseam. I am SO happy I did! When I pulled it out the bag I thought to myself, there’s NO WAY this is gonna work. But I squeezed myself into the initially doubtful fit. Wisely I decided to wear them around the house for a couple hours, w/o removing tags (just in case.. again, because of reviews). And to my pleasant surprise this wonderful pair of jeans decided to form to my figure in the best ways possible. So if you’re in doubt and you got some junk in the trunk, size down and trust the process. Because I could totally see if I had bought these in an 11, I wouldn’t like them now. Also the 34” inseam does almost hit the floor for me, but the next pair I order will be a 36”, just so I have some extra length.

I ordered the brown cheetah print and they are now my favorite jeans. The fit is awesome with a bit of stretch. The high waist and flare leg are very flattering and stylish. I ordered a small. For reference I am 5’4 and 120 lbs.

The material quality and stitching are good quality for the price. They are right on par with typical paints in the $20 price range. They should last for a fair bit but are not as durable as high-end pants. The print patterns look good and clear. The shaping is a little different in reality versus the listing photos. They are not quite as tapered at the waist, and not as flared at the feet. They are true to the size measurements so if your measurements match the listings size chart, you should be good to go. If you’re on the short size they will not ride high above the ankle like the picture. The listing says the model is 5’8” tall and my model is 5”2” tall so the pant length is pretty true to the photos. Overall at the price they are a good value and fit correctly.

This pair of leopard print cropped jeans are nice quality made jeans. The fabric is 98% cotton and 2% elastane. There is quite a bit of stretch in the material. The thickness of the fabric is between medium and thin, in my opinion. They are soft and comfortable to wear. The have 4 functional patch style pockets, 2 in front and 2 on the back. The button and zipper closures work smoothly without any sticking points. I ordered these jeans in a size XXL, with my regular pants size being an XL. I sized up because of the 98% cotton fabric, but missed reading that "stretch" was a part of the description on the item page, lol. I really should have ordered my regular size of XL for these jeans! While they don't fall off me, there is definitely a loose feel in the fit around my waist. I will likely being wearing a belt when I wear these, to be on the safe side. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. For my height, I think the crop on these doesn't look so good, but perhaps wearing them with flat shoes or sandals will work better for me. Or go the other way and wear a bit of heels? These are good quality, fashionable jeans and I will recommend them. They have plenty of stretch in the fabric, so I would advise to not size up. Check the luvamia size chart to get a nice fit. Their sizes are true to size. The current price (1-9-2026) of $35.99 is a fair value, and there is a coupon available at this time that would make them a good value, in my opinion.

I am obsessed with these pants. Finally my leopard search is over and couldn’t be more happy. The denim fit of these is seriously perfection. They actually feel like denim, granted there’s a 2% context of elastic - just enough to make them comfy when bending or sitting. I had no issue with them bagging out by end of the day either. I chose my regular size small. I’m consistently a US 4 at 122lbs with my measurements at 27”W/36”H @ 5’ tall. While advertised as cropped length, let’s face it at my height no much is cropped on me. So these hit just about the top of my foot. I’ve paired them with Slingbacks and also a pair of ankle boots with a shorter heel. Super cute with so many styling options. Dressed up or down works well. They washed up great also. I normally wash in a cooler temp and tumble dry on low. No issues with wrinkles out of the dryer. Well made, great quality and so super cute!

Lee Women's Petite Relaxed Fit All Day Straight Leg Pant Let me start first by giving measurements for reference: 5'3" , 137lbs , 29" waist, 38" hips, 27" inseam That being said, I have tried all 6/8 short/petite relaxed/curvy pants on Amazon (Rafaella Gaberdine curvy, Signature Levi curvy Straight, Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda & GV Curvy Skinny, Dickies Curvy Straight, Lee Relaxed Side Elastic Straight, etc) and ALL of them either had a gap in the waist, were too tight across the rear, or were simply just too long.. however THESE pants fit great and had neither issue! I finally found a pants that not only fit, but are modest for work-- flattering but not figure hugging. I bought these in size 6 petite in colors (pictured from left to right) Indigo Wash (like a blue jean color/type fabric) Black Imperial Blue Mini Glenplaid (Black on light grey- NO BLUE in this, which i actually prefer) Cypress (Dark Olive Green) Jet Black/White Rockhill Plaid Falcon (pinkish brown) Upon close inspection, I noticed 1 of the 6 pairs I had purchased (Falcon) were defective. On the back leg, it appears as if holes were accidentally cut in the pants and then attempted to be "patched" by resewing it back together. (see pictures) Very disappointed that Amazon would allow this and have reordered/requested a return on this one pair. Because of the issue and trouble, I am deducting 1 star.

Ok the important thing first; I'm 5'7", 138lbs and usually wear a comfortable "29", or 6-8, in jeans. I'm a pretty solid medium, sometimes a large, never a small. I got a size 8 in these jeans and they're definitely too big. The size chart has the 8 at a 29.5-30.5 and the 6 is 28.5-29.5, so i think me getting an 8 is my bad lol. You'll see in the pics that I added a belt & it definitely helps? But they're still super big. I do think if you're in between sizes, it's safe to size down in these!Moving on, they're DEFINITELY baggie and super soft. If you get the right size I think they'd be so cute for a baggie, comfy, casual look. The only thing I don't like is how LONG they are though, and I'm decently tall. But again, sizing down and wearing them a bit above the midrise length they're advertised as may fix that too.The price is also a plus. A nice, comfy pair of stylish baggie jeans for under 30 bucks is a great deal i think! I'll probably come back & buy the size 6 soon because they are so comfortable and affordable.ps- I didnt realize my mirror was so dirty please don't judge me lol. Have a great rest of your day!

At the time of this review, I’m 135 lbs,, 26", 37" hips, 5’ 7” and the size 4 fit is lovely. I love the high waist. I have really wide calves, 16" so I give them a cuff to make them deliberately more above-ankle length and balanced looking for my big legs. These are a medium denim weight with good stretch. They didn't "stretch out" after wearing them all afternoon, into the evening. I am not blessed my caboose area, and these are slim all the way thru my hips and behind. They've been washed on cold gentle cycle and tumbled dried on low - easy care, makes this pair a big win!

*****UPDATE***** [Changing my review from a 5 to a 3] After I left this review, I went back and forth with the sellers. (I know this isn't a seller review but this information is necessary because the product does not match the information posted online. This also isn't rating the quality of the seller, it's rating the quality of the product and the amazon listing.) I sent them the pictures of the capris I received and the description online. They said I was right, the jeans are mislabeled and what I received was dark blue. They told me to re-order them and choose light blue and this time, they would ensure I was sent light blue. I did as they said and they sent me another pair of navy blue capris. I contacted them again and their reply was that "there was a discrepancy between their color name and EDITED PRODUCT PHOTOS" and the real color. Basically, light blue doesn't exist and the photo that's posted is not real. To summarize: there are five blue colors they have listed online and literally four of them are NAVY BLUE and the images online are edited/fake. This is a big problem: when people shop, they choose the color they want. They expect to receive a color that matches what they select. Because these images on the amazon page are edited, the listing is inaccurate. *****ORIGINAL REVIEW***** I love these jeggings. They are NOT thick tough denim and they have a ton of stretch. Personally, I prefer them higher on my calf so I cuffed them and although the cuff certainly reveals they're not true denim, it looks okay. They're SO EXTREMELY COMFORTABE I can wear them all day. I do not have much of a defined waist and sometimes, the waistband can dig into me. Not these! They don't roll down at all and, if I had to, I bet I could sleep in them. Yep. They're that comfy. The only problem is I was looking for a medium blue. I chose the color "light blue" which, according to the picture, should be a true medium blue. What I was sent was a very dark blue. I cannot determine if I was sent the wrong color or if the listing was very off with the pictures of their colors. Anyone know about the color thing? I'd keep these but I'd still like the lighter ones too... Me: 5'2", 155lbs, apple-shaped, fat thighs.
Why did you pick this product vs others?: Amazing jeans, amazing fit, 100% cotton. I am SO HAPPY WITH THESE!!! (I am 5’2” & 130lb, size 4S fit like a glove) Fit: Perfect Material quality: Fantastic

This is my first time ordering jeans online because I can never seem to find ones that fit me even at the store. I'm 5 2", 120 pounds, and ordered a size: 8 Petite. It's pretty good, except I had to fold in the hem (so I will be fixing that myself, yup! even in petite size), but that is a minor thing) the rest of the fit is good. They are comfortable. I'm not really having the same issue as others with the low-back, though they are a low-rise for sure. Mine lay about an inch below my belly button. I'm comfortable with that. If you're on the edge, definitely worth trying these since they're easy to return.

As a 40 yr old mom, embracing the wide-leg jeans trend feels like stepping back into my early 2000s high school years. It took a few outfit trials before I felt comfortable. The fit was great, the high-waist is comfy, and the jeans themselves are stretchy--all pluses as a mom of 5. The length works great for my 5'2" height. I love that they don't drag umder my shoes ( I wore them with Adidas Court shoes), so I'm hopeful I can avoid the shredded hems I rocked back in the day. My husband is still on the fence about them. but my youngest sister-in-law and my nieces assure me I am "on-trend" which probably hasn't happened in a while! Definitely stepping out of (or maybe stepping back into) my skinny jeans comfort-zone, but I'm glad I gave them a shot!

These are so nice to have on hand because I really dislike denim and these are the perfect option for those casual Fridays. But although these are pretty stretchable they’re just not that flattering for me. I have prominent hip dips and a very strong postpartum mom pooch that this can’t contain and I had hoped it would be a little more snug around the waist area at least. The length is way too long for me because although I do have a shorter torso and longer legs, I have a petite body type (123-5’2”) that the hems are visibly too long, and being rolled up has to go halfway up my shin or else it’ll be rings around my ankles lol. I am normally a size 4 but the smalls feel like a 5-6 and sort of wish they did have an XS so it would fit my body a little more snugly but they’re not bad and being of stretchy material I had hoped it would fit more snugly like a 2-4 and not like 5-6. I think the price is okay but for the quality they’re at I think they could be a little better as I found a few stray threads and the fitting isn’t as advertised. It is very high-waisted which I love but I do have to wear a long tanktop or shirt because I feel like my c-toe and my hip dips are a ton more visible wearing this lol. It’s not that big of a deal since I tend to wear long tees and tanks anyways but I do wish it was a little more flattering. These are comfortable to wear and super easy to put on. I would probably rate these a 4.5* for convenience and stretch and color but not fully 5* because the sizing, length, thickness and flattery categories aren’t great. I still highly recommend these because if you don’t have jeggings and dislike the conventional feel of denim, these are a great option to have in faux denim blue and faux denim black on days you might need them.

Okay, listen up fellow Amazonians: I am 6'2" and 210 lbs, and these pants are so close to being long enough it’s almost disrespectful. Like… they ALMOST understood the assignment. Stupid close. Close enough that I’m willing to forgive them because everything else about them is fantastic. They’re thick but breathable (in a good, quality-fabric way). They’re wrinkle resistant, which is great because ironing is a chore I absolutely refuse to participate in. They have the perfect wide leg that makes it look like I’m purposefully elegant instead of floundering to both have pants long enough that are also stylish. Thy are pretty sturdy and not very stretchy so buy your actual size. And the navy color? Chef’s kiss. Honestly, if Lee ever decides to add two more inches of length, I will personally write them a thank-you letter and bake them cookies. Until then, these are the best “not flood pants” I’ve ever found. Would buy again. Already did. In black. Same review but you know, I'd say black instead of navy. Duh.

I'm 5'5, curvy fit, 135ish. Not long legged. I normally wear a 27/6 in jeans ... I sized up to an 8 in this. Nothing was tight but I didn't live the look. I've seen barrell jeans (not tried on) and mom jeans--each a baggy look. I do own the Free People knit jumper and a FP denim oversized set of bibs and like both. Each of those are an obvious oversize. These jeans, on the other hand, just were not baggy looking as the model shows. I read many reviews and hoped they'd work but they literally look like my mom wore in the mid 80s and hers fot about like this. Just not a cute look, imo, if they are not baggy enough. Go big, or go home. I still wear skinny jeans. I have wode keg jeans. I have cropped flares. I cannot wear straight leg or boyfriend style bc I'm a curvy fot (the waist always sticks out too badly). Providing this info, as I think it is all relevant. Last, I'm almost 50. This look might seem cuter on a gal my size but in her 20s. Or with no curve and less boots. Material was fine; blue color a tad off, seemed almost frosted-ish.

I'm a 40 yr old mom who keeps wanting to try all the new trends like cropped wide leg jeans (aren't these just capris?) like I see in Mom-toks and they all look great in their baggy jeans. I wanted to love them but I'm just not sure they're made for my body type.I'm 5'2" and 130lbs and got a small. The length was what I expected for a cropped jean but I felt like the waist just made me feel wide. Shirt tucked or front-tucked didn't really work for me either, maybe it's the shirt? I pulled back one of pant legs so you can see just how wide-legged these are on me. It's not a crazy palazzo but comfrotably loose. Overall, I still want to like them. I'm going to hang on to them and see if I can find the right combination of shirt/pants ration and the wide legged feel will grow on me.

Once it's washed, that may lay better. It has plenty of length to tuck it and keep it tucked, but not so much that it bunches up.As far as sizing, I followed the product size chart and ordered a medium according to the 39 inch bust. These jeans are super comfortable! They have a ton of stretch and are a lightweight denim blend. I typically wear an 8-10 in women's jeans and ordered these in the M and they fit how I expected. I love the color and the big cuffs on the bottom of the leg opening. I am a short girl (5'3") so these are slightly long to wear with tennis shoes but will work perfect with a slight heel or boot!

These are definitely high-rise and the material feels really nice. Great stretch. But they are definitely not belly friendly. If you've got a fupa and are looking for pants that are both comfortable and flattering, you're only gonna get comfortable here. If you don't care about showing some gut and your only concern is good fitting stretchy jeggings that won't show off your granny panties, these are a solid option. I'm 5'7" with hips and hormonal weight gain currently at about 185 (thanks, thyroid), and carry most of that extra in the lower tummy area. I also walk around with it really sucked in so it isn't as prominent here. I like a longer inseam, and hate ankle length pants. They fit perfectly, are a great length without bunching, and don't cause mooseknuckle.

The drawstring waist makes them easy to adjust and add to their comfort.

I am 5’7” and weigh 140lbs. I am curvy. I wear a medium in everything, except jeggings. I ordered a large in these, and they are perfect. I wear a lot of jeggings and they are all not created equal. Some have very thin fabric that stretches out and then sags in the crotch, and I have to keep pulling them up all day. Some have so much Lycra that they start to hurt at the waistline after wearing them for awhile. Some hit that side bulge right in the middle and now the side bulge can be seen through a tshirt. These, however, are the Goldilocks of jeggings. They are just the right weight fabric, just enough Lycra to hold me back a bit, but not cut off circulation. My favorite quality, however is the high waist! I have taken two pictures of my side: one where I pull the waist down a bit to show where normal jeggings fall (blubber bulge), and one shot of where these jeggings ride above the bulge. No more fatty roles to see through my tshirts!! I am super happy with these. These are my new go-to jeggings.

Favorite Jeans & affordable! First time ordering jeans online & after I’ve lost 40+ lbs, at that! Loves: I am 5’8” and these go below my ankle, so I can cuff them at my ankle, if I wanted & wear cute bootie shoes. I have SKINNY legs and they are very fitting & flattering! Material, zipper, & button all feel very durable. All 4 pockets are REAL! Also, the price is not bad at all compared to other pairs of jeans on here with much thinner material. Possible inconveniences: Not a lot of stretch in the waist area, but it works. I’m 168-170 lbs and a size 31 was suggested . I got a size 30 because all other stretch jeans I own, usually loosen up throughout the day; idk if these will, honestly. The material is on the thicker side. If my thighs and calves weren’t skinny, these would not fit, so they are SKINNY. I could have felt very comfortable in a 31, but I am expecting to lose additional weight, so these will do, but whatever size is suggested to you, these are true to size in the waist. BUY THEM!

I am a size 16 pant and ordered the large. I am 5’4” and weigh 185. The large fits well overall. I don’t think the waist is as comfortable as the popular H brand but these are a great alternative. I ordered the dark blue and they are very dark. There is no stone washed look on the thighs or calves. It is a true color drenched dark blue. There are four pockets. Two on the front and two back pockets. These are a cotton blend so will be breathable but wash in cold to prevent shrinkage. There is plenty of stretch to the material. The length is great for me but anyone taller may have an issue with them fitting their leg length. All seams and stitching were straight and closed well. There were no loose strings. Overall these are a comfortable quality pair of jeggings. Price is comparable to other brands.

For reference, I carry all my weight in my midsection so it's very difficult to find jeans that fit comfortably and don't show my apron belly. I typically wear a size 12 or 14 and am 5'1" tall, so length is also always an issue. I first ordered the $98 Free People barrel jeans in all the colors, hoping they would look cute. I tried one on and didn't even bother trying on the rest, I immediately returned them. Unless you're a size 2, the FP jeans were clownishly huge in the legs, but the denim was buttery soft. I am not typically satisfied with the quality of clothing on Amazon, but decided to try several barrel jeans because I wasn't having any luck from name brands. To my surprise, these fit GREAT! The fabric is a denim blend so not the same quality of designer fabric, but they are beyond flattering - comfortable stretch in the waist, slim through the hips, then a reasonable barrel flare with a tapered bottom. They skim the tops of my shoes so I have to cuff them up a little and they're perfect. Might hem them, but I hate losing the original stitching. I ordered 3 colors and love all of them. The Black is perfectly faded, the Deep Blue is a really nice medium blue, and I'm so happy they make the Retro Blue, which is just like the FP jeans with the slightest green tinge. I initially ordered a pair of barrel jeans from another seller, Grapent, and I kept them but the wash is not as nice, the pockets kind of stretch out, and these jeans are a better shape and quality of colors. If you're an apple shape and hard to fit, I really recommend these. I'm so happy to be able to buy into a trend for less than half the cost of the designer brands. What a find!!!

Let me start by saying, if the right person with the right figure gets the right size; these jeans would be amazing! I don’t think they look good on ME…. But the quality of them are actually quite nice and they have the potential to be someone’s favorite pair of jeans! Let me start with the material: it’s a thick & stretchy denim, a lot like jegging material but nice, thick, and slimming. I am 5’9, 148 pounds and I’m generally a size 8. I got a size medium. They fit but I think I’d maybe like them more if I had gotten a size large. I think these run a little small in length (27ish inch inseam) this could be a great thing if you are short! If you’re tall like me this might be way too short! The other place it runs small is the rise! I would describe these as more of a mid to slight high rise. The fact that these are “paper bag” style , the spot where they’re hitting me isn’t flattering. If the rise was 1-2 inches higher they’d hit me at the natural waist and possibly would provide a nice snatched look, instead; they hit me right in the middle of my “love handle” area, causing a bit of frumpiness. However, like I said, if I got one size larger I feel there would be room to pull these up to my natural waist and also get a bit more length! They don’t run small on the hip / thigh area though. So idk if sizing up would be a problem like in a saggy booty sense. I’d have to order and see. I do think though that if you are a person who really loves this style of pant for the paper bag waist, cropped wide leg, and trouser style pockets - they might be worth giving them a try!!!! Because the material is thick and stretchy so they honestly feel like a High quality pair of pants!!! Short girls will love these!!!
